item.title: 以残障理论为中心的AI框架：重新定义人工智能中的残疾经验
item.content:encoded:
提出Cripping AI框架，强调残障经验在AI中的核心地位。



摘要： 本文基于残障理论，提出‘Cripping AI’作为指导框架，旨在将残疾人的生活经验置于人工智能研究与开发的核心位置。不同于以往仅强调AI对残疾人的‘可及性’，Cripping AI致力于揭示并消除AI设计中隐含的优健主义假设，强调残障知识体系（即残障认识论），并尊重残疾人参与共创可及性实践的劳动。文章通过三个案例展示该框架的应用：聋人与手语AI、失明与视觉辅助AI、口吃与语音AI。最后，文章提出了未来工作的三个方向，包括在AI全链条中融入多样化的身心体验，并与其他正义导向的AI努力协作。

item.title: UniPool：一种共享专家池的高效MoE架构
item.content:encoded:
UniPool通过共享专家池提升MoE模型效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出UniPool，一种新型Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) 架构，将专家容量视为全局预算，取代传统的每层独立专家分配方式。通过引入池级辅助损失和NormRouter，实现稳定且平衡的训练。实验表明，UniPool在多个LLaMA架构模型中显著降低验证损失和困惑度，同时减少专家参数使用量，证明其在深度扩展中可实现非线性参数增长，提高效率和效果。进一步分析显示，UniPool的优势可与更细粒度的专家分解结合。

item.title: 大型语言模型安全对齐的动态退化机制与样本级风险量化方法
item.content:encoded:
研究揭示了LLM安全对齐退化的动态机制并提出样本级风险量化方法。



摘要： 本文研究发现，大型语言模型的安全对齐在微调过程中会因良性样本的累积影响而逐渐退化，导致模型向危险方向偏移。作者通过分析参数动态变化，提出了一种样本级安全退化量化方法（SQSD），该方法通过测量样本引起的参数更新在危险与安全方向上的投影差异，计算出每个样本的风险评分。实验表明，SQSD能够有效量化样本级别的微调风险，并具有良好的跨模型架构、参数规模和高效参数方法的迁移能力。

item.title: 基于数据驱动的烟雾浓度预测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
数据驱动方法高效预测烟雾浓度，提升火灾模拟精度。



摘要： 文章提出一种基于数据驱动的多线性算子方法，用于预测火灾产生的烟雾浓度。该方法通过分析点火时间与烟雾浓度场的主成分，建立输入系数与输出系数之间的映射关系，从而实现对烟雾扩散的高效预测。实验表明，该方法在计算效率和预测精度上均优于传统方法，在Upper Rio Grande Watershed区域的应用中表现优异，具有较高的实际应用价值。

item.title: 基于动态点云的4D物体潜在表示框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种高效且准确的4D物体潜在表示方法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种学习4D物体潜在表示的框架，该框架能够忠实捕捉物体的几何和外观信息，同时具有压缩性和易用性。Velox通过编码器将时空颜色点云压缩为动态形状标记，并利用两个互补解码器进行监督：4D表面解码器用于建模时间变化的表面分布，高斯解码器则将标记映射为3D高斯分布以学习外观。实验表明，该表示在视频到4D生成、3D跟踪和图像到4D生成等任务中均表现出色。

item.title: Gaussian Kernel Attention：一种简化且可解释的注意力机制
item.content:encoded:
GKA用高斯核替代传统线性投影，提升效率与可解释性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为高斯核注意力（Gaussian Kernel Attention, GKA）的新型注意力机制，作为Transformer中点积注意力的替代方案。GKA通过高斯径向基函数直接计算token之间的相似性，每个注意力头仅需学习一个带宽参数，而输出投影保持与标准Transformer兼容。该方法被应用于视觉和语言建模任务，并在nanochat框架中实现了因果掩码和滑动窗口约束。实验表明，GKA模型在参数量和训练FLOPs上均显著减少，且表现稳定，尽管在比特/字节指标上略逊于标准注意力模型。GKA提供了一种更简洁、可解释的注意力机制，为Transformer设计提供了新的效率-精度平衡维度。

item.title: 多模态度量学习的泛化性质分析
item.content:encoded:
研究多模态学习中模态选择对模型性能的影响。



摘要： 本文对多模态度量学习模型的泛化性质进行了细致的理论分析，探讨了不同模态子集之间的层次关系，并量化了学习映射与真实数据之间的差异。通过分析多模态框架中的配对复杂性，作者推导出新的泛化误差界限，揭示了模态数量和粒度对模型性能的联合影响。理论结果表明，引入细粒度模态特征可以降低假设空间的复杂性，提高模态互补性。该研究为提升多模态学习系统的收敛速度和准确性提供了理论支持和实践意义。

item.title: 基础模型的分布外泛化问题与代理系统解决方案
item.content:encoded:
基础模型面临新型分布外挑战，需引入代理系统解决。



摘要： 文章探讨了基础模型在开放世界环境中面临的分布外（OOD）问题，指出传统方法难以应对知识边界、能力上限等新型挑战。作者提出代理系统作为新的研究范式，通过感知、策略选择、外部行动和闭环验证等结构特性，突破现有模型的参数覆盖限制。文章还回应了七项反论点，并强调代理方法与模型中心方法应互补发展，以推动基础模型在分布外场景中的进步。

item.title: Flexi-LoRA：基于输入复杂度的动态参数调整方法
item.content:encoded:
Flexi-LoRA通过动态调整LoRA秩提升模型适应性。



摘要： Flexi-LoRA是一种新型框架，能够在训练和推理过程中根据输入复杂度动态调整LoRA的秩。实验表明，保持训练与推理阶段的动态一致性对有效适配至关重要，特别是在需要序列推理的任务中。输入依赖的参数分配在减少参数数量的同时提升了性能，尤其在数学推理任务中表现更优。Flexi-LoRA不仅提高了正确性，还增强了推理质量和指令遵循能力，相比静态LoRA表现更佳，且实现了类似专家混合框架的优势，同时减少了参数冗余。

item.title: Mira：一种基于样本的条件分布准确性评估方法
item.content:encoded:
Mira通过联合样本评估条件分布的准确性，实现贝叶斯模型比较。



摘要： 本文介绍了Mira，一种基于样本的评分方法，用于评估候选条件分布与真实数据生成过程的匹配程度。该方法基于分布一致性的原则，推导出Mira统计量的解析表达式，并定义了Mira分数。当候选分布与真实分布一致时，可计算理论参考值和不确定性估计。该框架通过量化候选模型条件分布与真实数据生成过程的对齐程度，实现了贝叶斯模型比较，无需进行复杂的证据计算。实验表明，Mira在多个简单问题和贝叶斯推断任务中表现有效。

item.title: 印尼Spotify评论三分类情感分析的机器学习与深度学习方法比较
item.content:encoded:
对比ML和DL方法在印尼Spotify评论情感分类中的表现。



摘要： 本文对印尼Spotify评论进行三分类情感分析，比较了传统机器学习方法（支持向量机、朴素贝叶斯、决策树）与深度学习方法（BiLSTM）的性能。研究使用了10万条爬取的评论数据，并经过清洗后得到70,155个样本。所有模型均采用相同的预处理流程，包括俚语标准化、停用词去除和词干提取。结果显示，决策树在传统模型中表现最佳，而BiLSTM在整体加权F1分数上最高，但在少数类中性情感识别上表现不佳。论文结论指出，BiLSTM在整体情感检测上更优，而结合SMOTE的机器学习方法在三分类任务中更具平衡性。

item.title: Aes3D：首个3D神经渲染场景美学评估框架
item.content:encoded:
提出Aes3D框架及数据集，用于评估3D场景美学质量。



摘要： 随着3D高斯点云（3DGS）在沉浸式媒体和数字内容创作中的应用增加，对3D场景的美学评估变得愈发重要。然而，现有方法主要关注重建精度和感知真实感，忽略了构图、和谐与视觉吸引力等高层次美学属性。为此，本文提出了Aes3D，这是首个系统性的3D神经渲染场景美学评估框架，包含Aesthetic3D数据集和Aes3DGSNet模型。Aesthetic3D通过新的注释策略构建，而Aes3DGSNet直接从3DGS表示中预测场景级美学评分，无需渲染多视角图像，降低了计算成本。实验表明该方法在保持轻量化的同时表现出色，为3D场景美学评估树立了新基准。

item.title: 基于对抗训练的机器生成文本检测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
REACT框架提升机器生成文本检测性能与抗攻击能力。



摘要： 本文针对机器生成文本检测在少量样本设置下的性能不足及易受对抗攻击的问题，提出了一种基于威胁建模的对抗训练框架REACT。该框架通过结合人类化攻击者和目标检测器，利用检索增强生成技术生成高度类人的对抗样本，同时使检测器通过对比学习提高少样本下的表示稳定性与鲁棒性。实验表明，REACT在四个数据集上的平均检测F1值提升了4.95点，并降低了3.66个百分点的攻击成功率。

item.title: Training Non-Differentiable Networks via Optimal Transport
item.content:encoded: Neural networks increasingly embed non-differentiable components (spiking neurons, quantized layers, discrete routing, blackbox simulators, etc.) where backpropagation is inapplicable and surrogate gradients introduce bias. We present PolyStep, a gradient-free optimizer that updates parameters using only forward passes. Each step evaluates the loss at structured polytope vertices in a compressed subspace, computes softmax-weighted assignments over the resulting cost matrix, and displaces particles toward low-cost vertices via barycentric projection. This update corresponds to the one-sided limit of a regularized optimal-transport problem, inheriting its geometric structure without Sinkhorn iterations. PolyStep trains genuinely non-differentiable models where existing gradient-free methods collapse to near-random accuracy. On hard-LIF spiking networks we reach 93.4% test accuracy, outperforming all gradient-free baselines by over 60~pp and closing to within 4.4~pp of a surrogate-gradient Adam ceiling. Across four additional non-differentiable architectures (int8 quantization, argmax attention, staircase activations, hard MoE routing) we lead every gradient-free competitor. On MAX-SAT scaling from 100 to 1M variables, we sustain above 92% clause satisfaction while evolution strategies drop 8--12~pp. On RL policy search, we match OpenAI-ES on classical control and retain performance under integer and binary quantization that collapses gradient-based methods. We prove convergence to conservative-stationary points at rate $O(\log T/\sqrt{T})$ on piecewise-smooth losses, upgraded to Clarke-stationary on the headline architectures and extended to the piecewise-constant regime via a hitting-time bound. These rates match the known zeroth-order query-complexity lower bounds that all forward-only methods inherit. Code is available at https://github.com/anindex/polystep.

item.title: 基于图结构的ACOPF与SCUC联合学习框架
item.content:encoded:
提出联合建模ACOPF与SCUC的图结构学习框架，提升电网优化性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于图结构的联合学习框架，用于同时建模交流最优潮流（ACOPF）和安全约束机组组合（SCUC）。该框架通过共享的图结构捕捉电网拓扑和物理交互，并结合任务特定解码器处理静态和时序决策。训练过程中引入物理信息目标以确保AC可行性及跨时段操作约束。实验表明，该模型在不同电网规模下均优于现有方法，具备良好的泛化能力，可支持跨异构电力系统优化问题的学习。

item.title: 基于自监督学习的脑肿瘤分类方法研究
item.content:encoded:
自监督学习在脑肿瘤MRI分类中表现优异，提升诊断准确性。



摘要： 本文研究了利用自监督学习（SSL）进行多类脑肿瘤分类的方法，采用ResNet-50作为主干网络，评估了SimCLR、BYOL、DINO和Moco v3四种SSL框架。在包含4,448张MRI图像的公开数据集上，SimCLR取得了99.64%的准确率、精确率、召回率和F1分数。实验表明，在标签有限的情况下，SSL预训练模型在多个指标上优于监督基线。同时，结合Grad-CAM等可解释AI技术，提升了模型决策的可解释性，证明了SSL在医学影像分析中的可行性和有效性。

item.title: BioTool提升大语言模型在生物医学领域的工具调用能力
item.content:encoded:
BioTool数据集显著提升LLM在生物医学工具调用中的表现。



摘要： 尽管大语言模型在通用任务中表现出色，但在生物医学等专业领域仍存在不足。本文提出BioTool，一个包含34个常用生物医学工具和7,040个高质量查询-API调用对的数据集，用于微调大语言模型。实验表明，使用BioTool微调的模型在生物医学工具调用任务中表现优于当前最先进的商业模型，且专家评估也证明其能有效提升下游答案质量。该数据集和评估代码已开源。

item.title: 基于RC-Koopman框架的非线性动力系统可学习表示
item.content:encoded:
RC-Koopman框架提升非线性系统动态建模精度与稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出RC-Koopman框架，通过将RC中的储备池视为有限维Koopman字典，结合谱半径控制时间深度，解决非线性动力系统建模中的字典选择、时间记忆编码和数值病态问题。研究显示，ESP保证了提升后的Koopman近似良好定义和数值稳定性，相关谱半径选择算法使储备池记忆与系统主要时间尺度对齐。分析表明，储备池的有限记忆决定了哪些Koopman特征函数可从提升特征中观测到。在合成基准测试中，RC-Koopman在重建精度与动态稳定性之间取得良好平衡，优于EDMD和基于Hankel的提升方法。

item.title: 基于大模型的藏语文本转语音系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出首个工业级藏语TTS系统，提升低资源条件下的语音合成效果。



摘要： 本文针对藏语文本转语音（TTS）面临的资源稀缺、方言差异大及书面与口语映射复杂等问题，提出了基于大模型的藏语TTS系统。该系统通过数据质量增强、面向藏语的文本表示与分词器适配以及跨语言适应训练，在低资源条件下实现了稳定、自然且可理解的藏语语音合成。实验结果表明，该系统在主观评价中取得较高的MOS分数和发音准确率，优于外部商业接口，为未来统一多方言藏语语音合成奠定了技术基础。

item.title: 改进的置信度校准评估方法：CSR与cwA的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出CSR和cwA作为更可靠的置信度校准评估指标。



摘要： 本文指出传统期望校准误差（ECE）在评估置信度校准时存在局限性，即使存在极端高估风险也可能保持较小值。因此，作者提出了校准比例（CSR）作为替代指标，并结合置信度加权准确率（cwA）来全面评估模型的置信度校准与区分能力。研究还表明，置信度加权AUC（cwAUC）能更好地捕捉校准信息，而传统AUC无法做到这一点。通过多个合成数据集和真实数据集的测试，验证了CSR能有效区分高风险与低风险的置信度分配，同时指出标准校准方法可能仍存在风险。

item.title: 提升多模态大模型推理的LIME方法
item.content:encoded:
LIME增强多模态模型推理中的感知输入使用，减少幻觉。



摘要： 本文提出LIME（Learning Inference-time Modality Enhancement），一种无需训练的框架，用于增强多模态大语言模型在推理过程中的感知输入利用，从而减少幻觉现象。LIME通过Layer-wise Relevance Propagation（LRP）量化每个token的贡献，并定义基于相关性的目标，以提高对感知输入的依赖性。该方法在解码过程中更新模型的关键值表示，不改变模型参数或需要额外数据。实验表明，LIME在多个视觉和音频领域的基准测试中有效减少了幻觉并提升了模型的多模态对齐能力。

item.title: AdaGATE：一种用于多跳RAG的高效证据控制器
item.content:encoded:
AdaGATE提升多跳RAG在噪声和冗余下的性能。



摘要： 本文提出AdaGATE，一种无需训练的多跳检索增强生成（RAG）证据控制器。该方法将证据选择建模为一个受约束的修复问题，结合实体中心的差距追踪、定向微查询生成和基于效用的选择机制，平衡了差距覆盖、佐证、新颖性、冗余性和直接相关性。在HotpotQA数据集上，AdaGATE在干净、冗余和噪声注入的检索条件下均取得最佳证据F1分数，分别达到62.3%和71.2%，同时比Adaptive-k少使用2.6倍的输入token。实验表明，显式的差距感知修复与高效的证据选择相结合，提升了多跳RAG在不完美检索环境下的鲁棒性。

item.title: 基于可执行世界模型的ARC-AGI-3编码代理系统评估
item.content:encoded:
评估一种基于可执行世界模型的编码代理系统在ARC-AGI-3中的表现。



摘要： 本文评估了一个用于ARC-AGI-3的初始编码代理系统，该系统通过维护一个可执行的Python世界模型进行推理和规划。系统使用脚本控制器、预定义接口、验证程序和计划执行器，但不包含任何特定游戏的逻辑代码。在25个公开的ARC-AGI-3游戏中，该代理成功解决了7个游戏，并在6个游戏中实现了超过75%的相对人类行动效率。该系统的性能表明，基于验证器驱动的可执行世界模型是ARC-AGI-3代理的一种有前景的方法。

item.title: 高熵玻尔兹曼机在人工蛋白设计中的优势研究
item.content:encoded:
高熵模型生成的序列空间更大且更接近自然蛋白。



摘要： 本文研究了基于进化序列数据训练的玻尔兹曼机在人工蛋白设计中的表现，比较了不同架构（如bmDCA、eaDCA、edDCA和meDCA）的性能。结果表明，尽管所有模型都能生成功能酶，但最大熵模型meDCA在序列空间的多样性上显著优于其他模型，其采样范围比低熵模型大超过15个数量级。此外，高熵模型能更好地捕捉自然蛋白周围的中性空间，减少过拟合问题。这说明高熵玻尔兹曼机在表征进化适应度景观方面具有优势。

item.title: 提升早期退出神经网络性能的稳定-可塑性方法
item.content:encoded:
通过稳定-可塑性机制优化早期退出网络性能。



摘要： 本文研究了早期退出神经网络在适应性推理中的应用，提出两种方法以解决顺序训练中新增退出层对已有分类器性能的干扰问题。一种方法通过保护关键参数来维持已有退出层的知识，另一种方法则通过保留早期输出分布来实现模型适应。这两种方法分别借鉴了持续学习中的弹性权重整合和无遗忘学习策略。实验表明，所提方法在标准基准测试中显著提升了早期退出性能，提高了准确率并实现了计算资源有限下的性能加速。

item.title: 基于动态适应再随机化的高效因果推断方法
item.content:encoded:
DARTS方法通过优化协变量选择提升因果推断效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为DARTS的新型随机对照试验设计方法，将协变量获取视为一个嵌入在因果推断任务中的序列优化问题。该方法利用预算约束的组合Thompson采样器，逐步识别最具预后价值的协变量，并通过再随机化和回归调整降低批次层面平均处理效应的方差。理论分析表明，基于历史批次的自适应协变量选择不会破坏批次层面的随机化有效性，且累积逆方差加权估计器可达到名义渐近覆盖。实验结果显示，DARTS能有效集中预算于高信息量特征，显著缩小与理想设计的效率差距，同时保持严格的统计推断有效性。

item.title: 基于细粒度特征的专利新颖性评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出FiNE-Patents数据集，提升专利新颖性评估精度。



摘要： 本文探讨了专利新颖性评估的复杂性，指出传统二分类方法存在缺陷。为此，作者引入FiNE-Patents数据集，包含3,658个专利权利要求，并标注了细粒度的先有技术引用。研究提出将新颖性评估从二分类任务转向特征级别的联合检索与抽象推理任务，要求模型识别具体先有技术段落并判断哪些特征使专利具有新颖性。实验表明，基于大语言模型的工作流在段落检索和新特征识别方面优于嵌入基基线模型，且对虚假相关性更具鲁棒性。研究释放了数据集和代码，以促进透明、细粒度的专利分析研究。

item.title: 在线对抗模仿学习的理论与实践框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出OPT-AIL框架，实现高效模仿学习。



摘要： 本文针对对抗模仿学习（AIL）中理论与实践脱节的问题，提出了基于优化的在线AIL框架（OPT-AIL）。该框架通过奖励学习的在线优化和策略学习的乐观正则化优化，实现了近专家策略的学习。文中开发了两种具体方法：无模型OPT-AIL和有模型OPT-AIL，并证明两者在一般函数逼近下具有多项式专家样本复杂度和交互复杂度。这是首个在一般函数逼近下被证明高效的AIL方法。从实践角度看，OPT-AIL只需近似优化两个目标，便于实施。实验表明，OPT-AIL在多个挑战性任务中优于现有最先进的深度AIL方法。

item.title: 基于流程图的科学可重复性评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种结构化可重复性评估方法，提升科研成果可验证性。



摘要： 本文提出Agentic Reproducibility Assessment (ARA)方法，将科学可重复性评估转化为结构化推理任务。通过从论文中提取包含来源、方法、实验和输出的有向流程图，并利用结构和内容评分进行可重建性评估。在213篇ReScience C文章上的实验表明，ARA具有跨领域通用性和稳定性，准确率高达61%，在多个基准测试中表现优于现有方法，为大规模科学同行评审提供了有效补充。代码与数据已公开。

item.title: FedQueue: Queue-Aware Federated Learning for Cross-Facility HPC Training
item.content:encoded: Federated learning (FL) across multiple HPC facilities faces stochastic admission delays from batch schedulers that dominate wall-clock time. Synchronous FL suffers from severe stragglers, while asynchronous FL accumulates stale updates when queues spike. We propose FedQueue, a queue-aware FL protocol that incorporates scheduler delays directly into training and aggregation, which (i) predicts per-facility queue delays online to budget local work, (ii) applies cutoff-based admission that buffers late arrivals to bound staleness, and (iii) performs staleness-aware aggregation to stabilize heterogeneous local workloads. We prove the convergence for non-convex objectives at rate $\mathcal{O}(1/\sqrt{R})$ under bounded staleness, and show that the admission controls yield bounded staleness with high probability under queue-prediction error. Real-world cross-facility deployment of FedQueue shows 20.5% improvement over baseline algorithms. Controlled queue simulations demonstrate robust improvement over the baselines; in particular, about 34% reduction in time to reach a target accuracy level under high queue variance and non-IID partitions.

item.title: Why Global LLM Leaderboards Are Misleading: Small Portfolios for Heterogeneous Supervised ML
item.content:encoded: Ranking LLMs via pairwise human feedback underpins current leaderboards for open-ended tasks, such as creative writing and problem-solving. We analyze ~89K comparisons in 116 languages from 52 LLMs from Arena, and show that the best-fit global Bradley-Terry (BT) ranking is misleading. Nearly 2/3 of the decisive votes cancel out, and even the top 50 models according to the global BT ranking are statistically indistinguishable (pairwise win probabilities are at most 0.53 within the top 50 models). We trace this failure to strong, structured heterogeneity of opinions across language, task, and time. Moreover, we find an important characteristic - *language* plays a key role. Grouping by language (and families) increases the agreement of votes massively, resulting in two orders of magnitude higher spread in the ELO scores (i.e., very consistent rankings). What appears as global noise is in fact a mixture of coherent but conflicting subpopulations. To address such heterogeneity in supervised machine learning, we introduce the framework of $(λ, ν)$-portfolios, which are small sets of models that achieve a prediction error at most $λ$, "covering" at least a $ν$ fraction of users. We formulate this as a variant of the set cover problem and provide guarantees using the VC dimension of the underlying set system. On the Arena data, our algorithms recover just 5 distinct BT rankings that cover over 96% of votes at a modest $λ$, compared to the 21% coverage by the global ranking. We also provide a portfolio of 6 LLMs that cover twice as many votes as the top-6 LLMs from a global ranking. We further construct portfolios for a classification problem on the COMPAS dataset using an ensemble of fairness-regularized classification models and show that these portfolios can be used to detect blind spots in the data, which might be of independent interest to policymakers.

item.title: From Concept to Capability: Evaluating 3D Gaussian Splatting for Synthetic Scene Editing in Autonomous Driving
item.content:encoded: The perception of an Autonomous Driving System (ADS) critically depends on relevant, comprehensive, and diverse datasets to ensure its safety while operating in the environment. Field data collection lacks completeness with respect to the list of rare but still possible safety-related scenarios needed for the development, verification, and validation of the ADS. 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) has shown promising capabilities for the reconstruction and editing of scenes based on data collected by cameras and LiDAR sensors. However, the industrial fidelity evaluation of reconstructions is underexplored, which is crucial when employing such methods in safety-related systems, especially for ADS. This becomes more challenging as ADS operates in a dynamic, uncontrolled environment with limited viewpoints and often partially occluded objects. This paper addresses this gap by proposing and implementing a framework (Fig. 1) to systematically analyze the capabilities and limitations of 3DGS for use in the reconstruction of safety-related scenes. It focuses on the quality of reconstruction for vehicles and pedestrians, which are the two most critical object classes for ADS. Our findings provide industry insights into the fidelity degradation of reconstructions from multiple novel viewpoints, both lateral and longitudinal, enabling the integration of these methods into real-world industrial AD software development and testing pipelines.

item.title: LatentRAG: Latent Reasoning and Retrieval for Efficient Agentic RAG
item.content:encoded: Single-step retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) provides an efficient way to incorporate external information for simple question answering tasks but struggles with complex questions. Agentic RAG extends this paradigm by replacing single-step retrieval with a multi-step process, in which the large language model (LLM) acts as a search agent that generates intermediate thoughts and subqueries to iteratively interact with the retrieval system. This iterative process incurs substantial latency due to the autoregressive generation of lengthy thoughts and subqueries. To address this limitation, we propose LatentRAG, a novel framework that shifts both reasoning and retrieval from discrete language space to continuous latent space. Unlike existing explicit methods that generate natural language thoughts or subqueries token-by-token, LatentRAG produces latent tokens for thoughts and subqueries directly from the hidden states in a single forward pass. We align LLMs with dense retrieval models in the latent space, enabling retrieval over latent subquery tokens and supporting end-to-end joint optimization. To improve transparency and encourage semantically meaningful latent representations, we incorporate a parallel latent decoding mechanism that translates latent tokens back into natural language. Extensive experiments on seven benchmark datasets show that LatentRAG achieves performance comparable to explicit agentic RAG methods while reducing inference latency by approximately 90%, substantially narrowing the latency gap with traditional single-step RAG.

item.title: Distilling Bayesian Belief States into Language Models for Auditable Negotiation
item.content:encoded: Negotiation agents must infer what their counterpart values, update those beliefs over dialogue turns, and choose actions under uncertainty. End-to-end large language models (LLMs) can imitate negotiation dialogue, but their opponent beliefs are usually implicit and difficult to inspect. We propose BOND (Bayesian Opponent-belief Negotiation Distillation), a framework for auditable negotiation. BOND consists of an LLM-based Bayesian teacher that scores dialogue contexts against the six possible opponent priority orderings, updates a posterior over those orderings, and uses the posterior for menu-based decision making, as well as a smaller 8B student language model that emits both negotiation actions and normalized posterior beliefs as tagged text. In the CaSiNo negotiation dataset, BOND outperforms the state-of-the-art and achieves mean Brier score 0.085 over opponent-priority posteriors. The distilled student preserves much of this belief signal, achieving Brier 0.114, below the uniform six-ordering reference of 5/36, approximately 0.139. Compared with a 70B structured-CoT baseline, the significantly smaller 8B student model yields substantially better elicited posterior calibration. We further showcase auditability through posterior trajectories, belief-versus-policy error decomposition, and posterior-prefix interventions. These diagnostics reveal that distillation preserves a scoreable belief report more strongly than causal belief-conditioned control, making weak belief-action coupling visible, not hidden.

item.title: EditPropBench: Measuring Factual Edit Propagation in Scientific Manuscripts
item.content:encoded: Local factual edits in scientific manuscripts often create non-local revision obligations. If a dataset changes from 215 to 80 documents, claims such as 'medium-scale' or 'a few hundred items' may also become stale, even though they do not repeat the edited number. In an audit of recent arXiv cs.CL benchmark and dataset papers, we find fact-dependent qualitative claims in 37.2% of papers, suggesting that this dependency pattern is common in the target genre. We introduce EditPropBench, a benchmark for measuring whether LLM editors propagate factual edits through dependent manuscript claims. Each item contains an ML/NLP-style synthetic manuscript, a targeted edit, and a controlled fact graph with sentence-level labels for direct targets, required downstream updates, and unrelated text that should remain unchanged. We summarize cascade success with Edit-Ripple Adherence (ERA), the fraction of required downstream updates correctly revised, and validate the metric with adversarial probes and stress-test variants. On the hardest cases, where dependent claims use implicit or free-form wording rather than repeating the edited value, five LLM editing systems span ERA 0.148-0.705. Even the strongest misses roughly 30% of required cascade updates. This advantage persists in a mixed evaluation that includes easy cases solvable by deterministic substitution. EditPropBench shows that current LLM editors can repair many implicit consequences of factual edits, but reliable scientific revision still requires cascade-aware checking.

item.title: PepSpecBench: A Unified Evaluation Benchmark for Peptide Tandem Mass Spectrometry Prediction
item.content:encoded: Tandem mass spectrometry provides a high-throughput framework for identifying and quantifying proteins in complex biological samples. In computational proteomics, predicting peptide MS/MS spectra is a critical task, enabling downstream applications such as large-scale peptide identification and quantification. While deep learning architectures have substantially improved prediction accuracy, three evaluation challenges obscure the true progress of the field. First, inconsistent data preprocessing and incompatible model output spaces hinder fair model comparison. Second, flawed data splitting strategies can permit hidden sequence leakage and inflate reported performance. Third, existing evaluations typically lack comprehensive cross-species benchmarking and systematic assessment of model robustness to influential experimental conditions. To address these challenges, we propose PepSpecBench, a unified benchmark for peptide MS/MS spectrum prediction. PepSpecBench standardizes data preprocessing across complementary public datasets, enforces a strict backbone-disjoint splitting strategy to eliminate sequence leakage, and evaluates diverse architectures within a shared fragment-ion representation space. It further introduces a comprehensive multi-species evaluation suite and physically grounded metadata perturbation probes to assess model robustness and instrument awareness. We uncover previously unrecognized performance discrepancies and robustness limitations across six representative models, providing actionable insights for future model design, evaluation and practical deployment.

item.title: Efficient Temporal Datalog Materialisation for Composite Event Recognition
item.content:encoded: Several applications demand the timely detection of critical situations, such as threats to safety and transparency, over high-velocity streams of symbolic events. This demand has motivated the development of (i) event specification languages, which define composite events via temporal patterns over simpler events, and (ii) stream reasoning frameworks, evaluating patterns expressed in these languages. However, event specification languages are typically studied in isolation, complicating their comparison in terms of expressivity and obscuring the scope of their associated stream reasoners. To mitigate this issue, we map practical fragments of prominent event specification languages into Temporal Datalog->-, a temporal Datalog with stratified negation and no future dependencies. To support efficient stream reasoning over Temporal Datalog->-, we propose Streaming Trigger Graphs, an extension of a state-of-the-art technique for Datalog materialisation. Our approach yields a uniform composite event recognition mechanism that has the potential to generalise across a wide range of practical event specification languages.

item.title: DGPO: Distribution Guided Policy Optimization for Fine Grained Credit Assignment
item.content:encoded: Reinforcement learning is crucial for aligning large language models to perform complex reasoning tasks. However, current algorithms such as Group Relative Policy Optimization suffer from coarse grained, sequence level credit assignment, which severely struggles to isolate pivotal reasoning steps within long Chain of Thought generations. Furthermore, the standard unbounded Kullback Leibler divergence penalty induces severe gradient instability and mode seeking conservatism, ultimately stifling the discovery of novel reasoning trajectories. To overcome these limitations, we introduce Distribution Guided Policy Optimization, a novel critic free reinforcement learning framework that reinterprets distribution deviation as a guiding signal rather than a rigid penalty.

item.title: OpenSeeker-v2: Pushing the Limits of Search Agents with Informative and High-Difficulty Trajectories
item.content:encoded: Deep search capabilities have become an indispensable competency for frontier Large Language Model (LLM) agents, yet their development remains dominated by industrial giants. The typical industry recipe involves a highly resource-intensive pipeline spanning pre-training, continual pre-training (CPT), supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and reinforcement learning (RL). In this report, we show that when fueled with informative and high-difficulty trajectories, a simple SFT approach could be surprisingly powerful for training frontier search agents. By introducing three simple data synthesis modifications: scaling knowledge graph size for richer exploration, expanding the tool set size for broader functionality, and strict low-step filtering, we establish a stronger baseline. Trained on merely 10.6k data points, our OpenSeeker-v2 achieves state-of-the-art performance across 4 benchmarks (30B-sized agents with ReAct paradigm): 46.0% on BrowseComp, 58.1% on BrowseComp-ZH, 34.6% on Humanity's Last Exam, and 78.0% on xbench, surpassing even Tongyi DeepResearch trained with heavy CPT+SFT+RL pipeline, which achieves 43.4%, 46.7%, 32.9%, and 75.0%, respectively. Notably, OpenSeeker-v2 represents the first state-of-the-art search agent within its model scale and paradigm to be developed by a purely academic team using only SFT. We are excited to open-source the OpenSeeker-v2 model weights and share our simple yet effective findings to make frontier search agent research more accessible to the community.

item.title: Strat-LLM: Stratified Strategy Alignment for LLM-based Stock Trading with Real-time Multi-Source Signals
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) are evolving into autonomous trading agents, yet existing benchmarks often overlook the interplay between architectural reasoning and strategy consistency. We propose Strat-LLM, a framework grounded in Stratified Strategy Alignment. Operating in a live-forward setting throughout 2025, it integrates heterogeneous data including sequential prices, real-time news, and annual reports to eliminate look-ahead bias. Extensive stress tests on A-share and U.S. markets reveal: (1) reasoning-heavy models achieve peak utility in Free Mode via internal logic, whereas standard models require Strict Mode as a vital risk anchor; (2) alignment utility is regime-dependent, with Free and Guided modes capturing momentum in uptrending markets, while Strict Mode mitigates drawdowns in downtrends; (3) mid-scale models (35B) show optimal fidelity under strict constraints, whereas ultra-large models (122B) suffer an alignment tax under rigid rules but gain a performance premium in Guided Mode; (4) standard LLMs often fall into a high win-rate trap, optimizing for small gains at the expense of total returns, which can only be mitigated through deep reasoning or strict external guardrails. Project details are available at https://Strat-LLM.github.io.

item.title: Bucketing the Good Apples: A Method for Diagnosing and Improving Causal Abstraction
item.content:encoded: We present a method for diagnosing interpretation in neural networks by identifying an input subspace where a proposed interpretation is highly faithful. Our method is particularly useful for causal-abstraction-style interpretability, where a high-level causal hypothesis is evaluated by interchange interventions. Rather than treating interchange intervention accuracy as a single global summary, we refine this framework by partitioning the input space into well-interpreted and under-interpreted regions according to pairwise interchange-intervention behavior. This turns causal abstraction from a purely global evaluation into a more diagnostic tool: it not only measures whether an interpretation works, but also reveals where it works, where it fails, and what distinguishes the two cases. This diagnostic view also provides practical heuristics for improving interpretations. By analyzing the structure of the well-interpreted and under-interpreted regions, we can identify missing distinctions in a high-level hypothesis, discover previously unmodeled intermediate variables, and combine complementary partial interpretations into a stronger one. We instantiate this idea as a simple four-step recipe and show that it yields informative error analyses across multiple causal abstraction settings. In a toy logic task, recursively applying the recipe recovers a high-level hypothesis from scratch. More broadly, our results suggest that partitioning the input space is a useful step toward more precise, constructive, and scalable mechanistic interpretability.

item.title: Linear Semantic Segmentation for Low-Resource Spoken Dialects
item.content:encoded: Semantic segmentation is a core component of discourse analysis, yet existing models are primarily developed and evaluated on high-resource written text, limiting their effectiveness on low-resource spoken varieties. In particular, dialectal Arabic exhibits informal syntax, code-switching, and weakly marked discourse structure that challenge standard segmentation approaches. In this paper, we introduce a new multi-genre benchmark (more than 1000 samples) for semantic segmentation in conversational Arabic, focusing on dialectal discourse. The benchmark covers transcribed casual telephone conversations, code-switched podcasts, broadcast news, and expressive dialogue from novels, and was annotated and validated by native Arabic annotators. Using this benchmark, we show that segmentation models performing well on MSA news genres degrade on dialectal transcribed speech. We further propose a segmentation model that targets local semantic coherence and robustness to discourse discontinuities, consistently outperforming strong baselines on dialectal non-news genres. The benchmark and approach generalize to other low-resource spoken languages.

item.title: Manifold Steering Reveals the Shared Geometry of Neural Network Representation and Behavior
item.content:encoded: Neural representations carry rich geometric structure; but does that structure causally shape behavior? To address this question, we intervene along paths through activation space defined by different geometries, and measure the behavioral trajectories they induce. In particular, we test whether interventions that respect the geometry of activation space will yield behaviors close to those the model exhibits naturally. Concretely, we first fit an activation manifold $M_h$ to representations and a behavior manifold $M_y$ to output probability distributions. We then test the link $M_h \leftrightarrow M_y$ via interventions: we find that steering along $M_h$, which we term manifold steering, yields behavioral trajectories that follow $M_y$, while linear steering -- which assumes a Euclidean geometry -- cuts through off-manifold regions and hence produces unnatural outputs. Moreover, optimizing interventions in activation space to produce paths along $M_y$ recovers activation trajectories that trace the curvature of $M_h$. We demonstrate this bidirectional relationship between the geometry of representation and behavior across tasks and modalities. In language models, we use reasoning tasks with cyclic and sequential geometries as well as in-context learning tasks with more complex graph geometries. In a video world model, we use a task with geometry corresponding to physical dynamics. Overall, our work shows that geometry in neural representation is not merely incidental, but is in fact the proper object for enabling principled control via intervention on internals. This recasts the core problem of steering from finding the right direction to finding the right geometry.

item.title: Causal discovery under mean independence and linearity
item.content:encoded: Causal discovery methods such as LiNGAM identify causal structure from observational data by assuming mutually independent disturbances. This assumption is fragile: shared volatility, common scale effects, or other forms of dependence can cause the methods to recover the wrong causal order, even with infinite data. We introduce the Linear Mean-Independent Acyclic Model (LiMIAM), which replaces full independence with weaker one-sided mean-independence restrictions on the disturbances. Under finite-order consequences of these restrictions, source nodes are generically identifiable, and hence a compatible causal order can be recovered recursively. Our proof is constructive and leads to DirectLiMIAM, a sequential residual-based algorithm for causal discovery under dependent noise. In simulations with mean-independent but dependent disturbances, DirectLiMIAM outperforms LiNGAM methods. A large-scale empirical application to the oil market highlights the implausibility of the independence assumption and the ability of DirectLiMIAM to recover a realistic causal ordering, from policy to production and from prices to inflation.

item.title: Self-Supervised Spatial And Zero-Shot Angular Super-Resolution by Spatial-Angular Implicit Representation For Rotating-View SNR-Efficient Diffusion MRI
item.content:encoded: Rotating-view thick-slice acquisition is highly SNR-efficient for mesoscale diffusion MRI (dMRI) but requires numerous rotating views to satisfy Nyquist sampling, resulting in long scan time. We propose a self-supervised Spatial-Angular Implicit Neural Representation (SA-INR) that reconstructs high-resolution dMRI from a single view per diffusion direction, representing a massive acceleration. Our model, an MLP conditioned on a b=0 structural prior and the b-direction via FiLM, is trained end-to-end on the anisotropic input. The framework not only accurately reconstructs the trained b-directions (spatial SR) but also learns a continuous q-space representation, enabling high-fidelity "zero-shot" synthesis of unseen b-directions (angular SR). On simulated data, our method achieved high fidelity for both trained (34.82 dB) and unseen (33.08 dB) directions. Most importantly, the synthesized angular data also improved the quantitative accuracy of downstream DTI model fitting. Our SA-INR framework breaks the classical sampling limits, paving the way for fast, quantitative high-resolution dMRI.

item.title: Adaptive Negative Scheduling for Graph Contrastive Learning
item.content:encoded: Graph contrastive learning (GCL) has become a central paradigm for self-supervised representation learning in computational intelligence, with applications spanning recommendation, anomaly detection, and personalization. A key limitation of existing methods is their reliance on static negative sampling, which fails to account for the dynamic informativeness and computational cost of negatives during training. We propose AdNGCL, an adaptive negative scheduling framework with a hardness-aware scheduler (HANS) that formulates negative selection as a loss-gated, budget-constrained process across hard, intermediate, and easy strata. The scheduler dynamically adjusts step sizes based on contrastive loss trends under both global and per-category budgets, while periodically refreshing samples to maintain diversity without exceeding compute constraints. Experiments on nine benchmark graph datasets demonstrate that AdNGCL consistently advances state-of-the-art performance, achieving the best accuracy on seven datasets and second-best on the remaining two, while offering explicit control over computational cost. These results highlight the value of budget-aware, loss-sensitive scheduling as a general strategy for improving the robustness and efficiency of representation learning in emerging computational intelligence applications.

item.title: One Sequence to Segment Them All: Efficient Data Augmentation for CT and MRI Cross-Domain 3D Spine Segmentation
item.content:encoded: Deep learning-based medical image segmentation is increasingly used to support clinical diagnosis and develop new treatment strategies. However, model performance remains limited by the scarcity of high-quality annotated data and insufficient generalization across imaging protocols. This limitation is particularly evident in MRI and CT, where models are typically trained on a single acquisition sequence and exhibit reduced robustness when applied to unseen sequences or contrasts. Although data augmentation is widely used to improve general robustness on medical images, its impact on cross-modality generalization has not been quantitatively explored. In this work, we study a targeted set of data augmentation techniques designed to improve cross-modality transfer. We train three spine segmentation models, each on a single-modality/sequence dataset, and evaluate them across seven out-of-distribution datasets (spanning CT and MRI), reflecting a realistic single-sequence training and multi-sequence/contrast/modality deployment scenario. Our results demonstrate substantial performance gains on unseen domains (average Dice gain of 155 %) while preserving in-domain accuracy (average Dice decrease of 0.008 %), including effective transfer between CT and MRI. To mitigate the computational cost typically associated with strong data augmentation, we implement GPU-optimized augmentations that maintain, and even improve, training efficiency by approximately 10 %. We release our approach as an open-source toolbox, enabling seamless integration into commonly used frameworks such as nnUNet and MONAI. These augmentations significantly enhance robustness to heterogeneous clinical imaging scenarios without compromising training speed.

item.title: X-Voice: Enabling Everyone to Speak 30 Languages via Zero-Shot Cross-Lingual Voice Cloning
item.content:encoded: In this paper, we present X-Voice, a 0.4B multilingual zero-shot voice cloning model that clones arbitrary voices and enables everyone to speak 30 languages. X-Voice is trained on a 420K-hour multilingual corpus using the International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) as a unified representation. To eliminate the reliance on prompt text without complex preprocessing like forced alignment, we design a two-stage training paradigm. In Stage 1, we establish X-Voice$_{\text{s1}}$ through standard conditional flow-matching training and use it to synthesize 10K hours of speaker-consistent segments as audio prompts. In Stage 2, we fine-tune on these audio pairs with prompt text masked to derive X-Voice$_{\text{s2}}$, which enables zero-shot voice cloning without requiring transcripts of audio prompts. Architecturally, we extend F5-TTS by implementing a dual-level injection of language identifiers and decoupling and scheduling of Classifier-Free Guidance to facilitate multilingual speech synthesis. Subjective and objective evaluation results demonstrate that X-Voice outperforms existing flow-matching based multilingual systems like LEMAS-TTS and achieves zero-shot cross-lingual cloning capabilities comparable to billion-scale models such as Qwen3-TTS. To facilitate research transparency and community advancement, we open-source all related resources.

item.title: ExpoCM: Exposure-Aware One-Step Generative Single-Image HDR Reconstruction
item.content:encoded: Single-image HDR reconstruction aims to recover high dynamic range radiance from a single low dynamic range (LDR) input, but remains highly ill-posed due to detail saturation in over-exposed regions and noise amplification in under-exposed areas. While recent diffusion-based approaches offer powerful generative priors, they often overlook the exposure-dependent nature of the degradation and incur substantial computational costs from iterative sampling. To address these challenges, we propose ExpoCM, a novel one-step generative HDR reconstruction framework that reformulates HDR reconstruction as a Probability Flow ODE (PF-ODE) and constructs exposure-aware consistency trajectories via exposure-dependent perturbations. Specifically, a soft exposure mask is first constructed to separate the LDR image into over-, under-, and well-exposed regions. Based on this partition, region-conditioned consistency trajectories are designed to hallucinate saturated details, suppress noise in dark regions, and preserve reliable structures within a single, distillation-free inference step. To further enhance perceptual quality, we introduce an Exposure-guided Luminance-Chromaticity Loss in the CIE~$\text{L}^*\text{a}^*\text{b}^*$ space, which assigns exposure-aware weights to luminance and chromaticity components, effectively mitigating brightness bias and color drift. Extensive experiments on the HDR-REAL, HDR-EYE, and AIM2025 benchmarks demonstrate that ExpoCM achieves state-of-the-art fidelity and perceptual accuracy, while enabling over 400$\times$ and 20$\times$ faster inference compared to DDPM (1000 steps) and DDIM (50 steps), respectively.

item.title: EFGPP: Exploratory framework for genotype-phenotype prediction
item.content:encoded: Predicting complex human traits from genetic data is challenging because different genetic, clinical, and molecular data sources often contain different parts of the signal. Here, we present EFGPP, a reproducible framework for generating, ranking, and combining multiple types of data for genotype-to-phenotype prediction. We applied EFGPP to migraine prediction using UK Biobank data from 733 individuals. The framework combined genotype-derived features, principal components, clinical and metabolomic covariates, and polygenic risk scores generated from migraine and depression GWAS using PLINK, PRSice-2, AnnoPred, and LDAK-GWAS. The best single data type achieved a test AUC of 0.644, while combining multiple data types improved performance to 0.688 using migraine-focused inputs and 0.663 using cross-trait depression-derived inputs. Genetic features alone did not outperform the covariates-only baseline, but genotype-derived features performed better than PRS alone, and depression-derived PRS showed useful predictive signal. Overall, EFGPP provides a practical proof-of-concept framework for prioritising and integrating heterogeneous genetic data sources for complex phenotype prediction.

item.title: 基于样本梯度裁剪的鲁棒梯度估计方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于样本梯度裁剪的优化方法，提升非凸优化性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于样本梯度裁剪的鲁棒梯度估计方法，并对其理论和实验性质进行了分析。该方法在重尾梯度噪声下实现了非凸优化问题的最优期望收敛率，同时在高概率下也表现出与期望收敛率相当的性能。实验表明，在CIFAR-100数据集上训练AlexNet时，该方法优于传统SGD和标准梯度裁剪，即使考虑额外计算时间仍表现更优。此外，研究还发现，在梯度累积情况下，采用小批量级别的裁剪可显著提升训练性能，且计算成本几乎不变，这一结果挑战了常见的裁剪实践。

item.title: 利用深度学习模型监测撒哈拉以南非洲的分散垃圾
item.content:encoded:
深度学习模型可有效检测非洲城市分散垃圾，支持精准管理。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的开放模型，用于通过众包无人机图像自动检测撒哈拉以南非洲地区分散的固体废物。该模型在10个国家的29个区域进行训练和评估，表现出色。研究揭示了垃圾分布的异质性，包括沿水道的热点区域和城市中的广泛散落垃圾。垃圾积累与人口密度和基础设施缺乏密切相关，而与区域发展指标关联较弱。该模型为地方政府和社区提供了无需复杂技术即可监测垃圾的工具，有助于提升废物管理效率。

item.title: 匈牙利语反思写作自动分类研究
item.content:encoded:
研究匈牙利语学生作文的反思水平自动分类方法。



摘要： 本文首次对匈牙利语学生作文的反思水平进行自动化分类研究。基于包含1954篇专家标注作文的大规模数据集，比较了传统机器学习模型与匈牙利语特定的Transformer模型在文档级反思分类中的表现。研究还探讨了处理类别不平衡的不同策略，如类别加权、过采样和数据增强。实验结果显示，传统模型在特征工程优化后表现优异，准确率、F1分数和ROC AUC平均达到71%，而Transformer模型虽然整体表现稍逊，但在少数类别的泛化能力更强。研究结果为低资源环境下的反思分析提供了支持，并为其他形态丰富的语言研究奠定了基础。

item.title: SpectraDINO：跨光谱域的视觉基础模型
item.content:encoded:
SpectraDINO提升多光谱图像感知性能。



摘要： 本文提出SpectraDINO，一种适用于近红外（NIR）、短波红外（SWIR）和长波红外（LWIR）等多光谱成像的视觉基础模型。该模型通过轻量级的模态适配器扩展了DINOv2 ViT架构，同时保留了RGB预训练模型的丰富表征。采用多阶段师生训练策略，包括余弦蒸馏、对称对比损失、块级对齐和新型邻域结构保持损失，实现跨模态对齐并避免RGB先验的灾难性遗忘。在多个多光谱目标检测和语义分割基准测试中，SpectraDINO表现优异，验证了其作为通用光谱泛化模型的有效性。

item.title: 基于连续情感值的高效情感图像编辑框架MooD
item.content:encoded:
MooD通过连续VA值实现高效且精细的情感图像编辑。



摘要： 本文提出MooD，首个直接利用连续情感值（Valence-Arousal）进行精细且高效的AIE框架。MooD引入VA感知检索策略，结合视觉迁移和语义引导，实现可控情感编辑。同时构建了VA标注数据集AffectSet以支持模型优化与评估。实验表明MooD在情感控制性和视觉保真度上表现优异，且具有高效率。消融实验进一步验证了设计关键因素。

item.title: 自动化规划中的反事实推理研究综述
item.content:encoded:
本文综述了自动化规划中的反事实推理方法。



摘要： 本文回顾了自动化规划中反事实推理的相关研究，按变化元素、触发时机及改变原因和方式对现有工作进行了分类。文章探讨了在现实世界规划中应对意外情况和优化结果所需的灵活性，并总结了关键发现，同时提出了未来研究的方向。

item.title: 基于视觉-语言模型的无源域自适应方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出VODA框架，无需依赖源域数据实现高效域自适应。



摘要： 本文研究了无源域自适应（SFDA）问题，指出现有方法仍依赖源域预训练模型，无法真正实现源域无关。为此，作者提出ViL-Only Domain Adaptation（VODA），完全不依赖源域数据，仅使用随机初始化模型、视觉-语言模型和无标签目标域数据进行域自适应。通过分析VODA的适应过程，引入Two-Stage Denoised-Region Distillation（TS-DRD）框架，在Office-Home、VisDA和DomainNet-126数据集上验证了其性能优于现有SFDA方法，证明了VODA的有效性。

item.title: 高能物理文献的AI协同探索框架HEP-CoPilot
item.content:encoded:
HEP-CoPilot整合多源信息，提升新物理搜索效率。



摘要： 本文介绍HEP-CoPilot，一个用于高能物理文献探索和解释的检索增强型多智能体AI框架。该系统整合了出版物中的文本信息、HEPData中的结构化实验数据以及重构的物理图表，通过结合检索增强语言模型与协调代理工作流程，实现了基于证据的推理和对对撞机结果的结构化解释。在CMS近期的超越标准模型的物理搜索评估中，HEP-CoPilot能够检索相关测量、直接从HEPData记录中重建排除限，并进行跨论文的实验约束比较，从而实现无需人工数据集成的一致性、物理感知比较。这表明检索增强型AI系统可以作为粒子物理的科学协作者，帮助导航复杂文献、组织异构证据并加速新物理搜索的解释流程。

item.title: STABLEVAL：一种考虑分歧的AI系统评估框架
item.content:encoded:
STABLEVAL提升AI系统评估的稳定性与可靠性。



摘要： 文章提出STABLEVAL，一种考虑标注者分歧的AI系统评估框架。与传统多数投票方法不同，STABLEVAL通过建模项目正确性和标注者混淆模式，生成更稳定的系统排名。实验表明，在面对标注者异质性和对抗性噪声时，STABLEVAL比多数投票方法更具稳定性和统计可靠性，证明了建模分歧对实现稳健和可重复AI评估的重要性。

item.title: 基于VV-VH交叉极化融合的SAR洪水监测方法研究
item.content:encoded:
交叉极化SAR数据融合提升复杂环境下的洪水监测精度。



摘要： 本文研究了利用VV和VH极化SAR数据交叉融合的方法，以提高复杂环境下洪水监测的准确性。通过深度学习分割框架，对比了仅使用VV、仅使用VH以及融合VV-VH三种配置的效果。实验结果表明，融合方法在植被覆盖和异质区域表现更优，提升了洪水边界识别的准确性，对灾害监测具有重要意义。

item.title: 动态检索框架FitText提升API工具搜索效率
item.content:encoded:
FitText通过动态检索提升API工具搜索效果。



摘要： 文章提出了一种无需训练的动态检索框架FitText，将检索过程嵌入到智能体的推理循环中。该方法生成自然语言伪工具描述作为检索探针，并通过反馈迭代优化，同时利用随机生成探索多种可能性。Memetic Retrieval通过工具记忆引入进化选择压力，避免重复搜索。实验显示，在ToolRet和StableToolBench数据集上，FitText显著提升了检索排名和通过率，证明其在大规模API生态系统中的有效性。

item.title: 基于时空学习的LEO卫星网络分布式路由框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种融合GAT与LSTM的DQN路由框架，提升LEO网络性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于时空学习的分布式路由框架，用于动态低地球轨道（LEO）卫星网络。该框架将图注意力网络（GAT）和长短期记忆网络（LSTM）集成到深度Q网络（DQN）中，以实现基于局部观测的分布式自适应路由决策。路由问题被建模为部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP），以应对动态拓扑和时变流量带来的部分可观测性。仿真结果表明，该方法在吞吐量、丢包率、队列长度和端到端延迟方面均优于传统及学习型路由方案，同时实现了高达23.26%的队列减少，具有良好的拥塞预防能力。此外，该方法计算开销低且碳排放极少，展现出绿色AI的高效性。

item.title: 临床信息对失语症语音识别影响的实证研究
item.content:encoded:
临床信息对ASR性能提升有限，但结合微调可显著改善。



摘要： 本文通过构建基于Speech Accessibility Project数据集的基准测试，评估诊断标签、临床评分和更丰富的临床描述是否能提升失语症语音识别的准确性。实验结果显示，当前模型无法有效利用这些临床信息，反而可能导致词错误率上升。研究还尝试了结合临床提示格式的LoRA微调方法，在无上下文时保持性能的同时，将词错误率降低52%。子组分析显示，唐氏综合征和轻度严重程度的说话者受益显著。该研究明确了现有模型的不足，并为更包容的语音识别系统提供了测试平台。

item.title: 基于图神经网络与强化学习的中转物流优化方法
item.content:encoded:
文章提出一种结合图神经网络和强化学习的中转物流优化方法。



摘要： 文章探讨了中转物流问题，即通过具有有限容量的卡车网络对包裹进行路由。作者将该问题重新表述为一个目标条件化的多目标马尔可夫决策过程（MDP）。所提出的方法结合了图神经网络和无模型强化学习，从环境状态中提取小型特征图进行处理，以提升物流路径规划的效率和准确性。

item.title: 基于AI的屋顶光伏检测与城市太阳能分析框架
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种可扩展的太阳能检测框架，用于生成城市级光伏分布数据。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种基于开源数据和基础视觉AI模型的太阳能光伏板检测框架，能够从卫星图像中自动识别屋顶光伏系统，并生成具有地理坐标的光伏分布数据。该方法无需人工标注或特定模型训练，适用于多种影像数据，提高了光伏信息获取的效率和透明度。通过结合天气数据，该框架还能生成区域性的太阳能发电概况，支持分布式能源整合、电力优化等应用。研究团队已发布相关数据和API，使用户能通过指定建筑位置获取光伏地图和发电数据，助力太阳能规划与分析。

item.title: 基于多轮强化学习的高效探索框架T²PO
item.content:encoded:
提出T²PO框架提升多轮RL训练稳定性与探索效率。



摘要： 文章介绍了多轮强化学习（RL）在复杂交互任务中的最新进展，指出尽管已有稳定化技术如细粒度信用分配和轨迹过滤，但训练不稳定问题仍然存在，常导致训练崩溃。作者认为这是由于多轮设置中探索效率低下，策略生成的信息量不足。为此，提出了T²PO框架，通过在token和turn两个层级上控制探索，提升模型的不确定性感知能力。实验表明，T²PO在WebShop、ALFWorld和Search QA等环境中显著提升了训练稳定性和任务性能。代码已开源。

item.title: 基于不确定性主动校准的实时裂缝分割方法UnGAP
item.content:encoded:
提出UnGAP框架，通过不确定性主动校准提升裂缝分割精度与速度。



摘要： 实时裂缝分割在结构健康监测中至关重要，但受光照、模糊和纹理歧义等随机不确定性影响。现有方法将不确定性估计作为被动终点，未能将其反馈用于优化特征表示。本文提出UnGAP框架，通过不确定性提示特征调制器（UPFM）将不确定性作为主动视觉提示，动态校准特征分布，缓解高方差导致的梯度抑制问题，并引入边界感知检测头提升分割精度。实验表明，UnGAP在保持实时推理速度的同时显著提升了分割性能。

item.title: 因果软件工程：提升软件决策的因果建模与推理
item.content:encoded:
文章提出因果软件工程概念，强调因果推理在软件开发中的应用。



摘要： 随着软件工程中不确定性增加，传统基于相关性的方法在应对干预性或反事实问题时存在局限。文章提出因果软件工程（CSE）作为未来范式，通过因果模型和推理系统地支持软件生命周期中的决策。CSE旨在增强现有实践，提供明确假设、不确定性的效果估计和反事实诊断。文章提出了一个以因果为核心的流程视图、工具和组织采用的阶段性路线图，以及衡量进展的评估和基准计划。

item.title: 基于联邦深度强化学习的移动众包感知策略研究
item.content:encoded:
提出FDRL-PPO算法提升移动众包感知任务完成效率。



摘要： 本文研究了移动众包感知（MCS）系统中用户如何在信息不完全的情况下优化任务参与策略。针对 MCS 系统的动态特性，作者提出了一种去中心化的联邦深度强化学习算法 FDRL-PPO，使每个移动单元（MU）能够基于自身经验、资源和偏好自主学习任务参与策略。该方法通过联邦学习实现模型共享，避免数据泄露，提升整体任务完成率和公平性。实验表明，FDRL-PPO 在合成和真实数据集上均优于现有基准算法。

用户定义约束提升LLM任务规划的可靠性与灵活性。

item.title: 基于安全约束的扩散轨迹生成方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出SDGD方法提升安全轨迹生成的可靠性与效率。



摘要： 本文研究了离线安全强化学习中策略在部署时适应不同安全预算的问题。针对现有引导方案在奖励优化和约束满足之间的冲突，提出一种将安全约束视为轨迹区域限制的新视角，并设计Safe Decoupled Guidance Diffusion (SDGD)方法，通过条件引导和奖励梯度优化提高轨迹安全性与回报。同时引入Feasible Trajectory Relabeling (FTR)来避免奖励引导导致的代价漂移问题。实验表明，SDGD在DSRL基准测试中表现出最佳的安全合规性，成功满足94.7%的任务约束，同时在21项任务中获得最高安全回报。

item.title: 多智能体系统中的信念修正与动态认识推理框架
item.content:encoded:
研究多智能体系统中信念修正问题，提出广义AGM信念修正框架。



摘要： 本文探讨了多智能体系统中信念修正问题，即在某个智能体获得某一状态属性的信念后，所有智能体的信念将如何变化。基于标准的多智能体Kripke模型表示方法，作者将经典的AGM信念修正公理推广到多智能体场景，旨在为评估动态认识推理框架提供形式化基础。文章以广义全交多智能体信念修正是一个满足所有广义AGM公理的简单算子为例进行说明，并进一步定义了迭代修正的标准公理，提出了基于事件模型的更复杂的修正算子，同时讨论了在Kripke模型上构建满足所有广义迭代修正公理的意识算子可能遇到的问题。

item.title: 基于DDQN的LEO卫星网络自适应多目标切换框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于DDQN的LEO卫星网络多目标切换方法，提升性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于对弈双深度Q网络（DDQN）的自适应多目标切换框架，用于低轨卫星网络。该方法能够在动态变化的网络环境下，实现吞吐量、阻塞概率和切换成本之间的动态权衡。仿真结果表明，该方法在典型运行条件下可提升10.3%的吞吐量，并接近零阻塞，优于传统基线方法。

item.title: 基于医疗理赔数据的生成式Transformer模型ReClaim在疾病预测与医疗支出分析中的应用
item.content:encoded:
ReClaim模型利用43.8亿医疗事件提升疾病预测与医疗支出分析效果。



摘要： 本文介绍了ReClaim，一个从2008年至2022年MarketScan理赔数据中训练的生成式Transformer模型，涵盖2亿多参保人。该模型在1000多个疾病发病预测任务中表现出色，平均AUC达75.6%，优于LightGBM和Delphi模型。ReClaim不仅能预测疾病，还能捕捉财务结果，提高真实世界证据分析效果。其性能随规模增加而提升，并在外部数据集上验证有效，展示了理赔数据作为医疗基础模型的潜力。

item.title: 因果层级中的描述长度差异分析
item.content:encoded:
研究因果查询在不同层级间的描述长度差异。



摘要： 本文探讨了Pearl因果层级中，从观察性到干预性再到反事实查询的描述长度差异。通过引入查询类描述长度的概念，分析了在给定观测分布的情况下，更高层级因果答案所需的额外信息量。研究发现，在某些二元无环结构因果模型中，观测分布的描述长度是常数，而单变量干预答案的描述长度为Θ(n²)。文章还给出了有限门模式因果模型的上界，并证明了该二次分离在固定ε下的总变分描述中仍然成立。进一步分析显示，反事实描述长度仍存在Θ(n)的差距，这与剩余高阶模糊度的对数相关。

item.title: BYOK架构中的后对齐篡改攻击与防御研究
item.content:encoded:
BYOK架构中存在后对齐篡改威胁，可导致LLM响应被恶意中继修改。



摘要： 本文研究了BYOK代理架构中的一种新型安全威胁——后对齐篡改攻击（RTA）。该攻击通过第三方中继在LLM生成响应后、执行前进行篡改，使即使对齐的LLM也失效。实验表明，RTA在多个LLM和基准测试中成功率高达99.1%，优于传统提示注入攻击。案例研究表明其在现实场景中的可行性，并验证现有防御措施无法完全阻止该攻击。最后，作者提出一种基于时间的检测防御方法，在不影响代理功能的前提下有效缓解RTA。

item.title: 基于Schema的累积过程奖励模型提升知识图谱推理性能
item.content:encoded:
SCPRM-MCTS提升知识图谱问答任务性能。



摘要： 本文针对大语言模型在知识图谱推理中的中间步骤评估难题，提出一种基于Schema的累积过程奖励模型（SCPRM），通过结合推理前缀和查询中隐含目标的Schema距离，提供累积和未来奖励以优化路径探索。该模型被集成到蒙特卡洛树搜索（MCTS）中，用于多跳知识图谱问答任务。实验表明，SCPRM-MCTS在医疗和法律知识图谱问答任务中，平均提升了Hits@k指标1.18%，表现出更准确和风险敏感的推理评估能力。

item.title: 强化学习中可验证奖励的多样性崩溃问题研究
item.content:encoded:
RLVR导致语言模型多样性下降，KL控制可缓解此问题。



摘要： 本文研究了基于可验证奖励的强化学习（RLVR）在提升语言模型推理能力时出现的多样性崩溃现象。尽管单样本准确性提高，但多样本覆盖范围却下降，甚至低于基础模型。作者通过分析二元奖励的性质，指出政策梯度方法在此背景下存在本质退化问题。KL控制通过选择基模型条件下的有效性分布来解决这一问题，但在实际应用中，降低超参数β会导致优化器趋向于少数有效输出，进一步加剧多样性崩溃。实验表明，直接针对该有效性分布进行优化可以避免此问题。

item.title: 定量双极论证框架的新渐进语义
item.content:encoded:
提出新的渐进语义以改进QBAF的论证可接受性计算。



摘要： 本文介绍了用于定量双极论证框架（QBAFs）的新渐进语义，旨在解决现有语义在简单无环情况下仍产生分歧或反直觉结果的问题。该方法通过考虑攻击者和支持者的综合影响，更新论证的初始强度为最终强度，使结果更符合直觉，并满足文献中已有的合理性公理。此外，研究还证明了该语义在无环和更广泛的循环框架中均能收敛。
