item.title: Beyond Specialization: Robust Reinforcement Learning Navigation via Procedural Map Generators
item.content:encoded: Deep reinforcement learning (DRL) navigation policies often overfit to the structure of their training environments, as environmental diversity is typically constrained by the manual effort required to design diverse scenarios. While procedural map generation offers scalable diversity, no prior work systematically compares how different generator types affect policy generalization. We integrate four generators (sparse, maze, graph, and Wave Function Collapse) with guaranteed navigability into MuRoSim, a 2D simulator focusing on training efficiency for LiDAR-based navigation. We cross-evaluate five navigation policies on 1000 seeded maps per generator across three training seeds. Results show a strongly asymmetric cross-generator transfer: a specialist trained on sparse layouts falls to 3.3% success on mazes, whereas a policy trained on the combined generator set achieves 91.5 +/- 1.1% mean success. We further demonstrate that A* path-planner subgoal inputs are the dominant factor for robustness, raising success from the 90.2 +/- 1.4% feedforward baseline to 98.9 +/- 0.4% and outperforming GRU recurrence, which only improves the reactive baseline. The DRL policies outperform a classical Carrot+A* controller, which matches their success only at low speeds (1.0 m/s) but collapses to 24.9% at 2.0 m/s. This highlights learned speed adaptation as the decisive advantage of the learned approach. Real-world experiments on a RoboMaster confirm sim-to-real transfer in a cluttered arena, while a maze-like layout exposes remaining failure modes that recurrence helps mitigate.

item.title: Entanglement is Half the Story: Post-Selection vs. Partial Traces
item.content:encoded: While tensor networks have their traditional application in simulating quantum systems, in the recent decade they have gathered interest as machine learning models. We combine the experience from both fields and derive how quantum constraints placed on a tensor network manifest a change in capabilities. To this end, we employ a method of inference of classical tensor networks on a quantum computer to define a hybrid architecture. This hybrid tensor network is a practical unified framework for it's classical and quantum tensor network edge cases. We identify post-selection as the important property on which this interpolation hinges. The amount of post-selection corresponds to the level to which quantum constraints are enforced on the tensor network. On this basis, we propose a new hyperparameter which controls the transition between the hybrid and the quantum tensor network. In the comparison of classical and quantum tensor networks it complements the bond dimension. Quantum machine learning is improved by using the hyperparameter to allocate the practically limited post-selection to the quantum model in a trainable manner.

item.title: Position: How can Graphs Help Large Language Models?
item.content:encoded: With the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), classic graph learning tasks have greatly benefited from LLMs, including improved encoding of textual features, more efficient construction of graphs from text, and enhanced reasoning over knowledge graphs. In this paper, we ask a complementary question: How can graphs help LLMs? We address this question from three perspectives: 1) graphs provide an up-to-date knowledge source that helps reduce LLM hallucinations, 2) graph-based prompting techniques-such as Chain-of-Thought (CoT), Tree-of-Thought (ToT), and Graph-of-Thought (GoT)-enhance LLM reasoning capabilities, and 3) integrating graphs into LLMs improves their understanding of structured data, expanding their applicability to domains such as e-commerce, code, and relational databases (RDBs). We further outlook some future directions including designing sparse LLM architectures based on graphs and brain-inspired memory systems.

item.title: Beyond Known Objects: A Novel Framework for Open-Set Object Detection using Negative-Aware Norm
item.content:encoded: Open-Set Object Detection (OSOD) is crucial for autonomous driving, where perception systems must recognize and localize both known and previously unseen objects in complex, dynamic environments. While recent approaches deliver promising results, they often require retraining the detector extensively to learn objectness, which describes the likelihood that a bounding box tightly encloses a valid object, regardless of whether its category was learned during training. Deviating from existing work, we hypothesize that standard off-the-shelf detectors may already contain helpful cues for objectness, owing to their training on numerous and diverse known categories. Building on this idea, we propose NAN-SPOT, a training-light framework that does not require to retrain the base object detector and estimates objectness by leveraging a hidden layer metric called Negative-Aware Norm (NAN), requiring only minutes of training on just hundreds of images. To support comprehensive evaluation, we introduce COCO-Open, an expanded version of the existing COCO-Mixed dataset, increasing unknown object annotations from 433 to 1853, making it the most exhaustively labeled dataset for OSOD to the best of our knowledge. Experimental results demonstrate that NAN-SPOT achieves even better performance on unknown object detection than methods requiring heavy training, without compromising performance on known objects. This efficiency and robustness make NAN-SPOT a promising step towards open-world perception in autonomous driving.

item.title: CNNs for Vis-NIR Chemometrics: From Contradiction to Conditional Design
item.content:encoded: Near-infrared (NIR; a.k.a.\ NIRS) deep-learning studies in chemometrics increasingly report mutually inconsistent conclusions regarding convolutional neural network (CNN) design, including small versus large kernels, shallow versus deep architectures, raw spectra versus preprocessing, and single-domain training versus transfer learning. As a result, the same architecture can appear superior in one study and inferior in another, creating a practical impasse for chemometric practitioners. In this review, we argue that these contradictions are not evidence of irreconcilable methods but a structurally expected consequence of uncontrolled moderating variables. Specifically, we trace recurring disagreements to (i) the indirect nature of Vis--NIR measurement in water-dominated matrices, (ii) mismatch between effective receptive field (ERF) and the width of informative spectral structure, and (iii) validation design (including split strategy, hyperparameter tuning budget, and exposure to deployment-like shifts) acting as a hidden hyperparameter that can dominate model ranking. Building on evidence from published chemometrics and spectroscopy studies, we propose a conditional design framework that links architecture and preprocessing choices to spectral physics, dataset regime, and intended deployment scenario. Overall, the proposed perspective moves DL Chemometrics from template-driven architecture selection toward reproducible, physics-aware, and deployment-aligned model comparison.

item.title: ATLAS: Article Tracking, Linking, and Analysis of Swedish Encyclopedias
item.content:encoded: The digitization of old encyclopedias represents an important step to improve access to historically structured knowledge. Often, however, this process does not go beyond an optical character recognition, leaving all the underlying structure unexploited. In addition, many encyclopedias had multiple editions reflecting the evolution of knowledge. The lack of structure in the raw text makes it difficult to track changes across these editions. In this work, we built a pipeline to restore the text structure, where we extract the headwords and identify entries; categorize the entities; match entries across editions; and link entries to a Wikidata item. We applied this pipeline to the four major editions of \textit{Nordisk familjebok}, an authoritative Swedish encyclopedia published between 1876 and 1951. We could extract the headwords with an F1 score of 97.8\% and we obtained an F1 score of 93.4\% on the headword classification. On a small-scale evaluation, we reached a 93\% precision on the cross-edition matching, 85\% precision and 16.5\% recall on the Wikidata linking. This shows that an automated approach to digitized historical knowledge is possible. This should facilitate the preservation of general knowledge and the understanding of knowledge transmission. The datasets and programs are available online.

item.title: Does it Really Count? Assessing Semantic Grounding in Text-Guided Class-Agnostic Counting
item.content:encoded: Open-world text-guided class-agnostic counting (CAC) has emerged as a flexible paradigm for counting arbitrary object classes by using natural language prompts. However, current evaluation protocols primarily focus on standard counting errors within single-category images, overlooking a fundamental requirement: the ability to correctly ground the textual prompt in the visual scene. In this paper, we show that several state-of-the-art CAC models often struggle to determine which object class should be counted based on the given prompt, revealing a misalignment between textual semantics and visual object representations. This limitation leads to spurious counting responses and reduced reliability in real-world scenarios. To systematically address these limitations, we propose a new evaluation framework focused on model robustness and trustworthiness. Our contribution is two-fold: (i) we introduce PrACo++ (Prompt-Aware Counting++), a novel test suite featuring two dedicated evaluation protocols -- the negative-label test and the distractor test -- paired with new specialized metrics; and (ii) we present the MUCCA (MUlti-Category Class-Agnostic counting) evaluation dataset, a new collection of real-world images featuring multiple annotated object categories per scene, unlike existing CAC benchmarks that typically include a single category per image. Our extensive experimental evaluation of 10 state-of-the-art methods shows that, despite strong performance under standard counting metrics, current models exhibit significant weaknesses in understanding and grounding object class descriptions. Finally, we provide a quantitative analysis of how semantic similarity between prompts influences these failures. Overall, our results underscore the need for more semantically grounded architectures and offer a reliable framework for future assessment in open-world text-guided CAC methods.

item.title: Recurrent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Chemotherapy Control under Partial Observability
item.content:encoded: Chemotherapy dose optimization can be formulated as a dynamic treatment regime, requiring sequential decisions under uncertainty that must balance tumor suppression against toxicity. However, most reinforcement learning approaches assume full observability of the patient state, a condition rarely met in clinical practice. We investigate whether memory-augmented policies can improve chemotherapy control under partial observability. To this end, we employ a recurrent TD3-based approach with separate LSTM actor-critic networks and evaluate it on the AhnChemoEnv benchmark from DTR-Bench, considering both off-policy and on-policy recurrent architectures against feed-forward TD3 and Soft Actor-Critic. Pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic variability are held fixed to isolate hidden-state uncertainty and observation noise and to avoid confounding effects from inter-patient variability. Across ten random seeds, recurrence yields modest benefit under full observability but substantially stronger and more stable performance under partial observability, with more consistent tumor suppression and improved normal-cell preservation. These findings indicate that memory-based policies are particularly beneficial when clinically relevant state information is incomplete or noisy.

item.title: A second-order method on the Stiefel manifold via Newton$\unicode{x2013}$Schulz
item.content:encoded: Retraction-free approaches offer attractive low-cost alternatives to Riemannian methods on the Stiefel manifold, but they are often first-order, which may limit the efficiency under high-accuracy requirements. To this end, we propose a second-order method landing on the Stiefel manifold without invoking retractions, which is proved to enjoy local quadratic (or superlinear for its inexact variant) convergence. The update consists of the sum of (i) a component tangent to the level set of the constraint-defining function that aims to reduce the objective and (ii) a component normal to the same level set that reduces the infeasibility. Specifically, we construct the normal component via Newton$\unicode{x2013}$Schulz, a fixed-point iteration for orthogonalization. Moreover, we establish a geometric connection between the Newton$\unicode{x2013}$Schulz iteration and Stiefel manifolds, in which Newton$\unicode{x2013}$Schulz moves along the normal space. For the tangent component, we formulate a modified Newton equation that incorporates Newton$\unicode{x2013}$Schulz. Numerical experiments on the orthogonal Procrustes problem, principal component analysis, and real-data independent component analysis illustrate that the proposed method performs better than the existing methods.

item.title: DirectEdit: Step-Level Accurate Inversion for Flow-Based Image Editing
item.content:encoded: With recent advancements in large-scale pre-trained text-to-image (T2I) models, training-free image editing methods have demonstrated remarkable success. Typically, these methods involve adding noise to a clean image via an inversion process, followed by separate denoising steps for the reconstruction and editing paths during the forward process. However, since the reconstruction path is approximated using noisy latents from mismatched timesteps, existing methods inevitably suffer from accumulated drift, which fundamentally limits reconstruction fidelity. To address this challenge, we systematically analyze the inversion process within the flow transformer and propose DirectEdit, a simple yet effective editing method that eliminates the inherent reconstruction error without introducing additional neural function evaluations (NFEs). Unlike most prior works that attempt to rectify the inversion path, DirectEdit focuses on directly aligning the forward paths, enabling precise reconstruction and reliable feature sharing. Furthermore, we introduce a preservation mechanism based on attention feature injection and multi-branch mask-guided noise blending, which effectively balances fidelity and editability. Extensive experiments across diverse scenarios demonstrate that DirectEdit achieves efficient and accurate image editing, delivering superior performance that outperforms state-of-the-art methods. Code and examples are available at https://desongyang.github.io/Directedit.

item.title: Measuring Differences between Conditional Distributions using Kernel Embeddings
item.content:encoded: Comparing conditional distributions is a fundamental challenge in statistics and machine learning, with applications across a wide range of domains. While proposed methods for measuring discrepancies using kernel embeddings of distributions in a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS) provide powerful non-parametric techniques, the existing literature remains fragmented and lacks a unified theoretical treatment. This paper addresses this gap by establishing a coherent framework for studying kernel-based methods to measure divergence between conditional distributions through what we refer to as conditional maximum mean discrepancy (CMMD). The CMMD consists of a family of metrics which we call levels, with three special cases each using a different type of RKHS embedding: CMMD$_0$ (conditional mean operators), CMMD$_1$ (conditional mean embeddings), and CMMD$_2$ (joint mean embeddings). We additionally introduce a general level $s$ CMMD, clarifying the required assumptions, and establishing mathematical connections between the levels through the lens of operator-based smoothing. In addition to reviewing previously proposed estimators, we introduce a novel doubly robust estimator for the CMMD that maintains consistency provided at least one of the underlying models is correctly specified. We provide numerical experiments demonstrating that the CMMD effectively captures complex conditional dependencies for statistical testing.

item.title: Foundation-Model-Based Agents in Industrial Automation: Purposes, Capabilities, and Open Challenges
item.content:encoded: Foundation models, particularly large language models, are increasingly integrated into agent architectures for industrial tasks such as decision support, process monitoring, and engineering automation. Yet evidence on their purposes, capabilities, and limitations remains fragmented across domains. This work examines how mature foundation-model-based agent systems are in industrial contexts, how their functional profile differs from conventional agent systems, and which limitations persist. A systematic literature survey following the PRISMA 2020 guideline is presented, screening 2,341 publications and synthesising a corpus of 88 publications through a structured coding scheme. The results show that reported systems are predominantly at prototype and early validation stages (75.0% at TRL 4-6), with deployment-oriented evidence remaining rare (9.1%). Operational goals are most frequently positioned in user assistance, monitoring, and process optimisation, while conventional production-control purposes such as planning and scheduling are less prominent. Compared with an established baseline for industrial agent systems, the capability profile reveals substantial gains in human interaction (+37%) and dealing with uncertainty (+35%), but a pronounced deficit in negotiation (-39%). The most widely reported limitations concern lack of generalization, hallucination and output instability, data scarcity, and inference latency. A working definition of foundation-model-based industrial agents is also proposed, bridging conventional agent theory, automation-engineering standards, and the foundation-model paradigm.

item.title: (POSTER) From Sensors to Insight: Rapid, Edge-to-Core Application Development for Sensor-Driven Applications
item.content:encoded: Scientists increasingly rely on sensor-based data; however transforming raw streams into insights across the edge-to-cloud continuum remains difficult due to the breadth of expertise required to coordinate the necessary data and computation flow. This paper introduces a pattern-based, AI-assisted methodology for rapid development of sensor-driven applications. Using Pegasus workflows executing on the FABRIC testbed, we demonstrate a 5-step development loop that shifts workflow construction and deployment from code-first to intent-first design. Starting from an existing Orcasound hydrophone workflow as a reusable template, we generate and refine workflows for air quality, earthquake, and soil moisture monitoring applications. We further show how these workflows extend to edge resources-including BlueField-3 DPUs and Raspberry Pis-through configuration and placement rather than workflow redesign. Our evaluation, from the perspective of a novice Pegasus user, shows that AI-assisted pattern reuse compresses multi-stage workflow development to 1-1.5 days per workflow while preserving the rigor and portability of workflow-based execution.

item.title: Global-Local Feature Decoding with Adapter-Guided SAMv2 for Salient Object Detection
item.content:encoded: Salient Object Detection (SOD) remains an essential yet underexplored task in the era of large-scale vision models. Although foundation models like SAM exhibit strong generalization, their potential for SOD is not fully realized, and training or fully fine-tuning them is computationally expensive and prone to overfitting under limited data. To overcome these challenges, we introduce GLASSNet, a Global-Local feature decoding framework that uses SAMv2 as a frozen encoder paired with a lightweight, spatially aware convolutional adapter-reducing learnable encoder parameters by over 97%. To enhance saliency quality, GLASSNet employs a dual-decoder architecture: one decoder captures global, long-range semantics with an expanded receptive field, while the other captures fine local details such as edges and textures. Fusing these complementary cues yields saliency maps that combine global coherence with local precision, producing accurate final masks. Extensive experiments on standard SOD and camouflaged object detection benchmarks show that GLASSNet surpasses state-of-the-art methods, demonstrating the power of frozen foundation models combined with targeted adaptation and global-local decoding.

item.title: Validation of an AI-based end-to-end model for prostate pathology using long-term archived routine samples
item.content:encoded: Artificial intelligence (AI) is becoming a clinical tool for prostate pathology, but generalization across variations in sample preparation and preservation over prolonged time periods remains poorly understood. We evaluated GleasonAI, an end-to-end attention-based multiple instance learning model, on an independent validation cohort comprising 10,366 biopsy cores from 1,028 patients across 14 Swedish regions, using archival diagnostic specimens from the ProMort cohorts collected between 1998-2015. The model achieved an overall quadratic-weighted kappa of 0.86 for core-level ISUP grading, comparable to several experienced pathologists and consistent across geographic regions. Notably, performance remained stable across the 17-year collection period, demonstrating robustness to time-related variation in archival material, a property not consistently observed with foundation model-based approaches, with exploratory analysis demonstrating a significant prognostic gradient across AI-assigned grade groups for prostate cancer-specific mortality. These findings support the generalizability of the AI grading model and demonstrate the potential of pathology archives as a large-scale resource for AI development, validation, and retrospective prognostic research.

item.title: LLM-enabled Social Agents
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) have transformed agent-agent and human-agent interaction by enabling software, physical, and simulation agents to communicate and deliberate through natural language. Yet fluent language use does not by itself yield socially intelligible behaviour. Most current systems remain weakly grounded in roles, norms, intentions, and contextual constraints, limiting their capacity for meaningful participation in social environments. This paper develops a conceptual baseline for LLM-enabled social agents by arguing that they should be grounded in role definitions operationalized through persona descriptions. On this basis, we outline research directions for representation, hybrid control, and evaluation. The paper concludes that persona-based role definitions are a necessary foundation for turning language competence into social behaviour.

item.title: Revisiting Semantic Role Labeling: Efficient Structured Inference with Dependency-Informed Analysis
item.content:encoded: Semantic Role Labeling (SRL) provides an explicit representation of predicate-argument structure, capturing linguistically grounded relations such as who did what to whom. While recent NLP progress has been dominated by large language models (LLMs), these systems often rely on implicit semantic representations, often lacking explicit structural constraints and systematic explanatory mechanisms. Traditionally, SRL systems have often relied on AllenNLP; however, the framework entered maintenance mode in December 2022, limiting compatibility with evolving encoder architectures and modern inference requirements. We revisit structured SRL modeling, introducing a modernized encoder-based framework that preserves explicit predicate-argument structure while enabling inference 10 times faster. Using BERT-base, the model attains comparable predictive performance, and RoBERTa and DeBERTa further improve F1 performance within the same framework. We adopt a dependency-informed diagnostic methodology to characterize span-level inconsistencies and conduct a representation-level analysis of LLM behavior under dependency-informed structural signals. Results indicate that dependency cues primarily improve structural stability. Finally, we illustrate how the framework's explicit predicate-argument structure can support multilingual SRL projection as a downstream application.

item.title: Statistically-Lossless Quantization of Large Language Models
item.content:encoded: Model quantization has become essential for efficient large language model deployment, yet existing approaches involve clear trade-offs: methods such as GPTQ and AWQ achieve practical compression but are lossy, while lossless techniques preserve fidelity but typically do not accelerate inference. This paper explores the middle ground of statistically-lossless compression through three complementary notions of losslessness for quantized LLMs. First, task-lossless compression preserves zero-shot benchmark accuracy within natural sampling variance and remains achievable at aggressive bitwidths. Second, we formalize the stricter notion of distribution-lossless compression, requiring the quantized model's next-token distribution to be practically indistinguishable from the original, and propose the Expected Acceptance Rate (EAR), the maximum token-agreement probability under optimal coupling, as a directly interpretable fidelity metric (for example, EAR >= 0.99 indicates 99% agreement). Third, we prove a gamma-squared variance law showing that symmetric quantization inflates noise variance by gamma squared relative to asymmetric quantization, making asymmetry necessary for distribution-lossless fidelity but not for task-level preservation. Using SLQ, a layer-wise non-uniform method with asymmetric quantization and wide bitwidth search, we achieve task-lossless compression at well below 4 bits per parameter (as low as 3.3 bits depending on the model), distribution-lossless compression at 5 to 6 bits per parameter on average, and inference speedups of 1.7 to 3.6x relative to FP16 with optimized kernels. Source code is available at https://github.com/IST-DASLab/SLQ.

item.title: IConFace: Identity-Structure Asymmetric Conditioning for Unified Reference-Aware Face Restoration
item.content:encoded: Blind face restoration is highly ill-posed under severe degradation, where identity-critical details may be missing from the degraded input. Same-identity references reduce this ambiguity, but mismatched pose, expression, illumination, age, makeup, or local facial states can lead to overuse of reference appearance. We propose \textbf{IConFace}, a unified reference-aware and no-reference framework with identity--structure asymmetric conditioning. References are distilled into a norm-weighted global AdaFace identity anchor for image-only modulation, while the degraded image is reinforced as the spatial structure anchor through low-rank residuals and block-wise degraded cross-attention with two-route memory. The resulting single checkpoint exploits references when available and falls back to no-reference restoration when absent, improving identity consistency, fine-detail recovery, and degraded-only restoration quality in a unified model.

item.title: Heterogeneous Model Fusion for Privacy-Aware Multi-Camera Surveillance via Synthetic Domain Adaptation
item.content:encoded: We propose HeroCrystal, a novel privacy-preserving framework for multi-camera domain-adaptive object detection, addressing challenges such as data privacy, class imbalance, and heterogeneous architectures. Our framework consists of three key stages. In the Generated Stage, we introduce a one-shot, target-aware diffusion-based generation module that learns visual style from a single target-domain image while leveraging prompt-based control to synthesize specific object instances. Unlike conventional style transfer-based methods that require large target datasets and ignore semantic-level discrepancies, our approach enables privacy-preserving augmentation to reduce ethical concerns, and introduces controllable rare object generation to mitigate long-tailed category degradation. In the Federated Stage, we employ probabilistic Faster R-CNN on the client side to improve localization accuracy, and a dynamic model contrastive strategy to suppress domain-specific bias. The server side performs model fusion across heterogeneous architectures without accessing raw data. Finally, in the Distilled Stage, we propose an inconsistent categories integration algorithm to resolve label inconsistency and architecture heterogeneity across clients. Extensive experiments on multiple cross-domain detection benchmarks demonstrate that our method outperforms existing multi-source domain adaptation and federated learning baselines under multi-class, privacy-preserving settings. Our method improves mAP by +2.1% over prior privacy-preserving approaches and achieves a new state-of-the-art mAP of 33.4%, highlighting the effectiveness of HeroCrystal in enabling practical multi-camera AI surveillance systems.

item.title: 提升RAG中检索质量以优化推理性能
item.content:encoded:
研究提出RCR指标，发现检索质量影响推理效果，提出NeocorRAG框架提升性能。



摘要： 本文针对Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)中检索性能提升与推理准确率不匹配的问题，提出新的评估指标Recall Conversion Rate (RCR)，揭示了检索质量对推理的影响。研究发现，随着Recall@5的提升，RCR呈现线性下降趋势，表明现有方法忽视了检索质量的优化。为此，作者提出NeocorRAG框架，通过证据链挖掘和约束解码实现全面的检索质量优化，显著提升了模型表现，并在多个基准测试中取得SOTA结果，同时减少约80%的token消耗。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的简单四边形网格生成方法
item.content:encoded:
SQuadGen通过CDF技术生成高效四边形网格，提升3D建模效率。



摘要： 文章提出SQuadGen，一种基于扩散模型的生成框架，用于在3D形状上生成简单的四边形网格布局。现有四边形重网格化方法常产生复杂且不规则的结构，导致手动清理和算法调整耗时。SQuadGen利用基于图表距离场（CDF）的连续表面表示，解决网格连通性的离散性问题，并构建大规模高质量四边形网格数据集以应对数据稀缺问题。实验表明，该方法在多种3D输入下均优于现有技术，生成更鲁棒、适合艺术家使用的四边形布局。

item.title: 基于多模态检索增强生成的隐私保护框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种去身份化的多模态检索增强生成系统，提升图像隐私保护。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为Identity-Decoupled MRAG的框架，用于在多模态检索增强生成系统中保护图像中人脸的隐私。该框架通过生成式匿名模块，在检索与生成之间进行干预，包含三个组件：解耦变分编码器用于分离人脸身份码和属性码；基于流形的拒绝采样器替换身份码以确保真实性与差异性；条件潜在扩散生成器则根据替换的身份码和保留属性合成匿名人脸。隐私通过多Oracle集成的面部识别模型和基于铰链损失的优化机制来保障。

item.title: AdaptEdit：一种精准局部编辑的适配器框架
item.content:encoded:
AdaptEdit实现精确局部编辑，无需用户掩码。



摘要： 本文提出AdaptEdit，一种基于冻结扩散Transformer的局部编辑框架。通过引入轻量级块适配器和空间门控机制，使模型能区分编辑内容与位置，并通过区域感知损失提升编辑精度。该方法无需用户提供掩码，在多个基准测试中表现优于现有方法，验证了其在像素级保真和指令遵循方面的有效性。

item.title: 基于小数据集的浅层纳米压痕硬度校正方法研究
item.content:encoded:
利用小数据集预测参考硬度，提升浅层纳米压痕精度。



摘要： 本研究探讨了如何利用少量实验数据通过物理引导的特征工程和数据增强来预测参考硬度，以解决浅层纳米压痕中因尺寸效应、接触面积误差和探针几何误差导致的硬度高估问题。实验收集了约700个来自三种钢参考样品的压痕数据，并通过物理驱动的变化进行增强，包括仪器噪声、会话漂移和局部多相边界混合。输入特征结合了Oliver-Pharr参数与力学描述符，如压痕功分配、H/E_r 和面积不变合规代理。评估了多种机器学习模型，其中受限神经网络表现最佳，在隔离测试中达到RMSE=0.470 GPa，MAPE=5.4%。结果表明该方法在浅层区域具有稳定性，且依赖于面积不变和能量相关描述符，为体积受限材料的数据高效表征提供了新路径。

item.title: 基于层分离策略的深度学习优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出层分离策略优化深度网络训练过程。



摘要： 本文研究了使用softmax交叉熵损失函数的深度学习优化问题，提出了一种层分离策略以缓解训练过程中遇到的强非凸性。针对全连接和卷积神经网络的交叉熵模型，引入与隐藏层输出相关的辅助变量，并构建对应的层分离模型，将原问题分解为一系列更易处理的子问题。理论分析证明新提出的层分离损失函数对原始交叉熵损失具有上界。同时设计了交替最小化算法，并在适当条件下证明其损失函数具有下降性质。数值实验验证了所提方法的有效性，表明其在优化行为上有所提升。

item.title: LOCA：局部因果解释模型拒绝的 jailbreak 攻击
item.content:encoded:
LOCA通过局部因果分析提升模型对有害请求的拒绝能力。



摘要： 本文提出 LOCA，一种用于解释 LLMs 在 jailbreak 攻击中为何成功拒绝有害请求的局部因果方法。LOCA 通过识别少量可解释的中间表示变化，有效诱导模型拒绝。实验表明，LOCA 平均只需六次变化即可成功触发拒绝，优于以往方法。该研究为理解 LLMs 的安全漏洞提供了更精细的机制解释。

item.title: AI代理市场协调能力评估研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨AI代理在市场中的自我评估能力与协调效果。



摘要： 文章提出MarketBench，用于评估AI代理是否具备参与市场的基本能力，如任务完成能力和成本估算。通过93个软件工程任务测试六种大型语言模型，发现它们在成功率和资源消耗上存在偏差，导致市场机制失效。引入历史实验数据后，校准有所改善，但差距仍显著。研究指出，自我评估是AI代理市场协调的关键瓶颈。

item.title: 基于证据深度学习的多组学融合框架CMGL提升癌症亚型分类
item.content:encoded:
CMGL通过可靠性估计提升多组学融合，提高癌症亚型分类准确率。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为CMGL的两阶段框架，利用证据深度学习评估每样本的多组学可靠性，并使用固定的置信度分数指导跨组学融合和图构建。在四个MLOmics癌症亚型任务及32类泛癌任务中，CMGL表现优于最强基线模型，平均准确率提升4.03%。其生成的表征能够恢复乳腺浸润癌的PAM50内在亚型，并且BRCA训练模型无需微调即可应用于肾透明细胞癌，有效区分预后不同的患者群体。

item.title: HealthFormer：基于Transformer的生理轨迹建模与临床预测模型
item.content:encoded:
HealthFormer通过生成式建模预测个体生理轨迹，提升疾病风险预测和干预效果模拟。



摘要： 本文介绍了HealthFormer，一个基于Transformer架构的模型，用于生成性地建模人类生理轨迹。该模型利用来自Human Phenotype Project的15,000多名个体的多维度健康数据进行训练，涵盖血液生物标志物、身体成分、睡眠生理等七个领域。HealthFormer能够预测个体在多个生理领域的轨迹，并无需任务特定训练即可迁移到其他队列，提高27种疾病和死亡结局的预测准确率。此外，它还能模拟干预效果，在个性化营养试验中成功预测6个月后的生物标志物变化，并在41项随机干预结果比较中显示出高度一致性。HealthFormer被视为一种初步的健康世界模型，可用于预测、风险分层和干预模拟，为临床数字孪生提供基础。

item.title: 基于语言模型的代理在混合动机环境中的协作与竞争行为研究
item.content:encoded:
研究AI与人类在协作竞争环境中的谈判行为差异。



摘要： 本文介绍了C2C环境，这是一个多代理系统，玩家在其中可以进行私人谈判并竞争达成秘密目标。研究发现，人类在谈判中更倾向于简单协议且合作可靠性较低，而基于语言模型的代理表现更稳定。通过针对性提示优化，代理的胜率有所提升。实验包含超过1,100场游戏和大量对话数据，为真实世界部署提供了测试平台。

item.title: GeoSR-Bench：面向地球观测任务的超分辨率基准数据集
item.content:encoded:
GeoSR-Bench用于评估超分辨率模型在地球监测任务中的表现。



摘要： 本文介绍了GeoSR-Bench，一个将超分辨率（SR）技术与下游地球观测任务相结合的基准数据集。该数据集包含来自36,000个地点的高质量图像对，覆盖多种地表类型和分辨率范围。通过评估GAN、Transformer、神经算子和扩散模型等SR方法，研究发现传统SR指标如PSNR或SSIM与任务性能之间关联性有限，甚至可能呈负相关，表明需要将下游任务纳入SR模型开发与评估中。

item.title: 跨被试脑电信号解码中的深度学习方法综述
item.content:encoded:
本文综述了用于解决跨被试脑电解码的深度学习方法。



摘要： 本文针对跨被试脑电信号解码中因个体差异导致的领域偏移问题，系统回顾了相关深度学习方法。文章将跨被试场景建模为多源领域问题，并提出了严格的评估协议。通过对现有文献的分类，文章总结了特征对齐、对抗学习、特征解耦和对比学习等主要方法。最后，文章探讨了提升实际解码性能的三个关键方向：当前方法的理论局限性、被试身份结构的价值以及脑电基础模型的发展。

item.title: 基于神经元-星形胶质细胞动态的自注意力联想记忆模型
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型联想记忆模型，提升高负载下的记忆检索准确率。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于神经元与星形胶质细胞相互作用的霍普菲尔德型联想记忆模型。该模型通过熵正则化复制方程调节星形胶质细胞增益，实现对模式相似性得分的softmax归一化分配，从而在固定点上实现类似注意力的路由机制。实验表明，在高记忆负载和干扰条件下，该模型比传统霍普菲尔德动力学和近期神经元-星形胶质细胞基线模型具有更高的检索准确性。研究建立了连接胶质调节、竞争性资源分配和类注意力计算的动力系统框架。

item.title: 基于流图驱动的多智能体框架在代码错误检测与修复中的应用
item.content:encoded:
FGDM框架提升代码错误检测与修复效果。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于流图驱动的多智能体框架（FGDM），用于自动化软件错误检测与修复。该框架通过将代码转换为流图，识别错误段并生成修复代码，结合Chain-of-Thought和Tree-of-Thought提示方法，并集成FAISS向量数据库以检索类似历史错误及修复方案。实验表明，FGDM在多个项目中表现优于现有方法，在Python和C语言中分别实现了平均24.33和8.37的Levenshtein距离降低，以及0.951和0.974的余弦相似度提升。

item.title: ClassEval-Pro：面向类级代码生成的基准测试与评估
item.content:encoded:
ClassEval-Pro基准测试揭示LLM在类级代码生成中的挑战与提升空间。



摘要： 本文介绍了ClassEval-Pro，一个包含300个类级任务的基准测试，覆盖11个领域，通过自动化流程构建，涵盖复杂性增强、跨领域类组合和真实GitHub代码集成。每个任务由LLM评委组验证，并需通过覆盖率超过90%的测试套件。评估显示最强模型仅达到45.6%的Pass@1分数，且不同生成策略对模型表现影响显著，逻辑错误和依赖错误是主要问题，表明跨方法协调是核心瓶颈。

item.title: PND框架有效减少视觉语言模型中的对象幻觉
item.content:encoded:
PND通过双路径对比提升视觉真实性，显著降低对象幻觉。



摘要： 本文提出一种无需训练的推理框架PND，用于解决视觉语言模型（VLMs）中因过度依赖语言先验而导致的对象幻觉问题。PND通过双路径对比机制，分别增强视觉特征的权重并削弱不相关特征，从而提升生成内容的视觉真实性。实验表明，PND在多个基准测试中表现优异，准确率提升达6.5%，同时增强了描述细节，且适用于多种VLM架构。

item.title: SIEVES提升多模态大模型在分布外场景下的选择性预测性能
item.content:encoded:
SIEVES通过视觉证据评分提升多模态模型在分布外任务中的覆盖度。



摘要： 本文提出SIEVES方法，旨在提高多模态大语言模型在真实世界分布外场景下的选择性预测能力。该方法通过生成局部视觉证据并评估其质量，显著提升了模型在多个挑战性基准测试（如V* Bench、HR-Bench-8k等）上的覆盖度，比非定位基线模型提升高达三倍。此外，SIEVES设计可适用于不同推理模型，无需访问其权重或logits，表现出良好的泛化能力和跨模型迁移性。

item.title: 跨模态编码器中的hub嵌入问题研究
item.content:encoded:
研究跨模态编码器中的hub嵌入问题及其对信息检索的影响。



摘要： 本文探讨了高维嵌入空间中常见的hubness问题，即某些嵌入点与大量不相关样本接近，可能影响信息检索和自动评估指标。针对文本与图像之间的跨模态相似性计算难题，研究提出了一种识别hub嵌入及其对应文本的方法。实验在MSCOCO和Nocaps数据集上的图像描述评估任务，以及MSCOCO和Flickr30k的数据集上的图像到文本检索任务中进行，结果表明该方法能够识别出不合理地获得较高相似度分数的单个hub文本，揭示了跨模态编码器的潜在缺陷。

item.title: 基于结构化剪枝的大型视觉语言模型压缩方法研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出结构化剪枝与轻量恢复训练方法提升LVLM部署效率。



摘要： 本文探讨了在资源受限设备上部署大型视觉语言模型（LVLM）的挑战，提出通过结构化剪枝对现有LVLM进行压缩，并结合监督微调和知识蒸馏进行恢复训练。研究对比了层级剪枝和宽度剪枝两种方法，发现宽度剪枝在低资源场景下表现更优。实验表明，在仅使用5%原始数据的情况下，仍可保持超过95%的原始性能，且在小压缩比例下仅需微调多模态投影器即可实现有效恢复。该方法为LVLM在边缘设备上的应用提供了可行方案。

item.title: 基于ILR框架的Claude多语言评估研究
item.content:encoded:
Claude在六种语言中的表现差异显著，评估方法结合量化与专家分析。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于ILR技能水平描述的系统评估框架，并应用于Claude（Sonnet 4.6）在六种语言中的表现评估。通过12个语义等价提示集群，收集了216份响应，采用定量和定性相结合的方法进行分析。结果显示，法语回应比德语长30%，创意和情感类提示表现出最大的跨语言差异。专家分析发现五种跨语言变化模式，包括语用消歧策略、文学传统差异、术语规范、文化校准缺口及情感支持中的机构行为差异。研究认为，ILR指导的专家判断可作为补充计算基准的新评估方法，对多语言AI的公平部署具有重要意义。

item.title: AI原生测试驱动开发框架提升大模型软件开发稳定性
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于大语言模型的AI原生TDD框架，提升开发稳定性与可重复性。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种AI原生的测试驱动开发（TDD）框架，旨在解决大语言模型在非约束工作流中出现的不稳定性和非确定性问题。该框架将传统TDD原则转化为结构化的提示层和工作流级治理机制，通过可机器读取的宣言文档，在规划、生成、修复和验证阶段进行分层架构设计，实现模型提议与确定性引擎权威的分离。系统通过强制阶段顺序、有限修复循环、验证门控和原子突变控制来提高稳定性和可重复性。文章还探讨了将软件工程规范直接编码到提示编排中的可能性，认为这是实现可靠LLM辅助开发的有前景方向。

item.title: Evergreen：提升语义聚合验证效率的系统
item.content:encoded:
Evergreen系统优化语义聚合验证，提升准确率并降低计算成本。



摘要： 本文介绍了Evergreen系统，该系统将语义聚合验证转化为语义查询处理任务，并通过多种优化手段（如早期停止、相关性排序、置信度序列估计等）降低计算成本和延迟。Evergreen在真实餐厅评论数据集上表现出色，验证质量达到F1=1.00，同时相比未优化方案显著降低了成本和延迟。即使使用较弱的LLM，Evergreen仍能以更低的成本和延迟获得更好的验证效果，优于基于检索增强的代理系统。

item.title: 多语言情感分析模型评估与德国数据集构建
item.content:encoded:
多语言情感分析模型在不同语言和任务中表现差异显著。



摘要： 本文对七种语言（英语、德语、法语、荷兰语、俄语、西班牙语和捷克语）的最新基于Transformer和指令调优的情感分析方法进行了全面评估，涵盖四个子任务。研究比较了不同模型架构在零资源、仅数据和全资源设置下的表现，并探索了跨语言迁移、代码切换和机器翻译的效果。结果表明，微调的大语言模型在复杂生成任务中表现最佳，而少样本模型在简单任务中接近其性能。跨语言训练对微调大模型的迁移效果最强，而小型编码器或序列到序列模型则从代码切换中获益最多。此外，本文还发布了两个新的德语数据集，以推动多语言情感分析研究。

item.title: 基于Bohm轨迹的时变薛定谔方程求解方法
item.content:encoded:
通过学习得分函数求解时变薛定谔方程。



摘要： 本文提出一种新方法，通过在Bohm轨迹上学习得分函数（对数概率密度梯度）来求解时变薛定谔方程。在Bohm量子力学框架中，粒子沿受经典势和量子势共同影响的确定性路径运动，这些非交叉轨迹构成由得分函数控制的连续归一化流。研究使用神经网络参数化得分函数，并最小化自洽Fisher散度以匹配实际密度的得分。理论证明，在无节点波函数条件下，零损失最小化可恢复薛定谔动力学，适用于原子振动场景。实验展示了双势阱中的波包分裂和Morse链的非谐振动。该框架将实时量子动力学转化为自洽得分驱动的归一化流，为现代生成建模工具提供了新的应用方向。

item.title: 基于流匹配的自由能表面采样方法FES-FM
item.content:encoded:
提出FES-FM方法，高效采样自由能表面。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为FES-FM的新型自由能表面采样方法，通过在集体变量空间中训练动态传输映射，实现对自由能分布的直接采样。该方法相比传统方法显著降低了计算成本，同时提高了单位采样时间内的准确性。针对多粒子系统，研究者构建了一个基于势能局部极小点Hessian矩阵的先验分布，确保了旋转平移不变性和物理合理性。实验结果表明，该方法在多种势函数和集体变量下均表现优异。

item.title: 基于变分期望最大化算法的非线性混合效应模型拟合研究
item.content:encoded:
VEM算法用于高效拟合大规模NLME模型。



摘要： 本文探讨了变分期望最大化（VEM）算法在非线性混合效应模型（NLME）中的应用，作为一种可扩展的替代方法，以应对传统方法在参数和随机效应增加时计算成本过高的问题。通过灵活的变分族和反向模式自动微分，VEM能够高效最大化边际似然，适用于包含超过15,000个群体参数的模型。研究对比了VEM与其他NLME拟合算法，并通过Pumas软件对两个测试模型进行了验证：一个标准华法林模型和一个具有15,410个群体参数的DeepNLME Friberg模型。实验结果展示了VEM的正确性和可扩展性。

item.title: 多轮智能体设置下的策略蒸馏方法改进
item.content:encoded:
提出TCOD解决多轮策略蒸馏中的KL不稳定问题。



摘要： 本文研究了在多轮智能体设置下，传统On-policy distillation（OPD）方法存在的轨迹级KL不稳定性问题。实验发现，随着训练进行，KL散度增加且成功率下降，导致训练不稳定。该问题源于多轮任务中误差累积，使学生模型超出教师模型的监督范围。为解决此问题，作者提出了TCOD（Temporal Curriculum On-Policy Distillation），通过逐步扩展轨迹深度的方式提升训练稳定性。实验表明，TCOD在多个基准任务上显著提升了性能，甚至超越了教师模型的表现。代码已开源。

item.title: 异步联邦遗忘机制在医疗影像中的应用
item.content:encoded:
提出AFU-IC框架实现高效联邦遗忘，提升医疗FL效率与合规性。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为AFU-IC的异步联邦遗忘框架，用于医疗影像领域，以解决传统方法依赖同步协调导致的延迟问题。该框架将遗忘过程与全局训练解耦，允许目标客户端异步执行遗忘而不中断整体训练。同时，服务器端的不变性校准机制防止模型重新学习被删除的数据。实验表明，AFU-IC在三个医学基准上实现了与重训练相当的遗忘效果和模型精度，且显著降低了实际运行时间，提升了跨数据中心医疗联邦学习的效率与可靠性。

item.title: 基于边界调整的大型语言模型对齐方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新的对齐框架，提升LLM与人类意图的一致性。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）对齐问题，指出传统偏好学习方法如DPO在神经网络假设空间下存在理论不一致的问题。为解决此问题，作者提出一种基于边界调整的排名框架，并推导出依赖分离边界的H-一致性界。进一步引入结构感知的H-一致性方法（SA-DPO），根据响应语义距离动态调整边界以处理同义词和难例对。最后，通过分析边界容量曲线，证明重尾损失函数在容量受限模型中优于标准逻辑损失，提供更优的一致性保障。

item.title: Dont Stop Early: Scalable Enterprise Deep Research with Controlled Information Flow and Evidence-Aware Termination
item.content:encoded: Enterprise deep research often fails to produce decision-ready reports due to uneven information coverage, context explosion, and premature stopping. We propose a scalable Enterprise Deep Research (EDR) architecture to address these failures. Our system (i) decomposes requests into coverage-driven objectives via outline generation with reflection, (ii) localizes context with dependency-guided execution and explicit information sharing, and (iii) enforces evidence-based completion criteria so agents iteratively collect information until sufficiency conditions are met. We evaluate on an internal sales enablement task and the public DeepResearch Bench benchmark, where our proposed system design achieves the strongest overall performance compared with competitive deep-research baselines. The results show that dependency-controlled context and explicit evidence sufficiency criteria reduce premature stopping and improve the consistency and depth of enterprise research outputs.

item.title: XekRung Technical Report
item.content:encoded: We present XekRung, a frontier large language model for cybersecurity, designed to provide comprehensive security capabilities. To achieve this, we develop diverse data synthesis pipelines tailored to the cybersecurity domain, enabling the scalable construction of high-quality training data and providing a strong foundation for cybersecurity knowledge and understanding. Building on this foundation, we establish a complete training pipeline spanning continued pre-training (CPT), supervised fine-tuning (SFT), and reinforcement learning (RL) to further extend the model's capabilities. We further introduce a multi-dimensional evaluation system to guide the iterative improvement of both domain-specific and general-purpose abilities. Extensive experiments demonstrate that XekRung achieves state-of-the-art performance on cybersecurity-specific benchmarks among models of the same scale, while maintaining strong performance on general benchmarks.

item.title: Three Models of RLHF Annotation: Extension, Evidence, and Authority
item.content:encoded: Preference-based alignment methods, most prominently Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF), use the judgments of human annotators to shape large language model behaviour. However, the normative role of these judgments is rarely made explicit. I distinguish three conceptual models of that role. The first is extension: annotators extend the system designers' own judgments about what outputs should be. The second is evidence: annotators provide independent evidence about some facts, whether moral, social or otherwise. The third is authority: annotators have some independent authority (as representatives of the broader population) to determine system outputs. I argue that these models have implications for how RLHF pipelines should solicit, validate and aggregate annotations. I survey landmark papers in the literature on RLHF and related methods to illustrate how they implicitly draw on these models, describe failure modes that come from unintentionally or intentionally conflating them, and offer normative criteria for choosing among them. My central recommendation is that RLHF pipeline designers should decompose annotation into separable dimensions and tailor each pipeline to the model most appropriate for that dimension, rather than seeking a single unified pipeline.

item.title: TwinGate：一种针对分解式越狱攻击的高效防御框架
item.content:encoded:
TwinGate有效防御分解式越狱攻击，提升LLM安全性。



摘要： 文章提出TwinGate，一种基于双编码器的防御框架，用于对抗分解式越狱攻击。该方法通过非对称对比学习在共享潜在空间中聚类语义不同但意图匹配的恶意片段，并利用冻结编码器减少误报。TwinGate仅需一次轻量级前向传递，实现低延迟和高吞吐量。实验表明，TwinGate在大规模数据集上表现出色，具有高恶意意图召回率和低误报率，优于现有防御方法。

item.title: 基于几何扰动的神经网络形式化验证方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出Super-DeepG方法提升神经网络在几何扰动下的鲁棒性验证效率。



摘要： 本文针对图像处理中对几何扰动（如旋转、缩放、剪切和平移）不敏感的需求，提出了一种新的神经网络形式化验证方法Super-DeepG。该方法改进了线性松弛技术和Lipschitz优化的推理方式，并利用GPU硬件实现高效的鲁棒性认证。实验表明，Super-DeepG在精度和计算效率上均优于现有方法，并已作为开源工具发布于GitHub。

item.title: AI/ML模型在临床决策中的稳定性与更新风险分析
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨AI/ML模型更新对临床决策的影响及稳定性问题。



摘要： 本文研究了临床环境中人工智能和机器学习模型在数据过时后性能下降的问题，分析了模型更新可能带来的风险。通过四个美国1型糖尿病数据集，评估了不同更新策略对模型稳定性、预测准确性及子群体误差平衡的影响。研究以儿童严重高血糖事件预测为例，提出多维监控框架以确保临床决策支持系统的可靠性。

item.title: S2G-RAG：一种增强多跳问答的结构化检索生成框架
item.content:encoded:
S2G-RAG提升多跳问答性能，通过结构化控制减少冗余信息。



摘要： 本文提出S2G-RAG框架，通过引入S2G-Judge控制器，明确判断当前证据是否足以回答问题，并在不足时生成结构化的缺失信息项，用于指导后续检索。该方法通过维护句级证据上下文，减少噪声积累，提高多轮检索的稳定性与准确性。实验表明，S2G-RAG在TrivaQA、HotpotQA和2WikiMultiHopQA数据集上表现优异，且可作为轻量组件集成到现有RAG系统中，无需修改搜索引擎或重新训练生成器。

item.title: 多模态测试时适应方法MG-MTTA提升模型鲁棒性
item.content:encoded:
MG-MTTA通过控制模态可靠性提升多模态模型在部署时的性能。



摘要： 本文研究了多模态模型在部署时因视觉和文本分支不对称变化而导致的性能下降问题。作者提出MG-MTTA方法，通过更新轻量级门控或适配器，在不改变主干网络的情况下优化融合后验分布。该方法结合了后验熵最小化与基于锚点的模态一致性及跨模态冲突的可靠性先验，有效提升了模型在不同语义变化和联合视觉-文本变化下的准确率。实验表明，MG-MTTA在ImageNet基准上显著提升了模型性能，证明了控制模态可靠性比单纯减少预测熵更为重要。

item.title: AutoSP：自动优化长上下文大语言模型训练的解决方案
item.content:encoded:
AutoSP提升长上下文LLM训练效率，无需手动优化。



摘要： 本文提出AutoSP，一种自动优化长上下文大语言模型（LLM）训练的解决方案。传统训练库主要针对大规模参数模型进行优化，而忽视了长上下文任务的需求。AutoSP通过自动化序列并行和长上下文感知激活检查点技术，在不影响吞吐量的情况下显著提升训练可扩展性。实验表明，AutoSP在NVIDIA和AMD硬件上分别将训练上下文长度提升2.7倍和2.5倍，优于手动优化基线。

摘要： 本文针对人脸识别模型在不同场景下的性能问题，提出一种新的自适应边距损失函数FunFace。该方法结合生物特征效用评估，通过Certainty Ratio估计样本效用，优化模型训练效果。实验表明，FunFace在高质量数据集上表现优异，在低质量数据集上更具优势，有效提升了人脸识别的鲁棒性。

item.title: 3D虚假内容检测与Fake3DGS数据集的提出
item.content:encoded:
3D虚假内容检测面临挑战，Fake3DGS数据集提升识别能力。



摘要： 本文针对3D重建和神经渲染技术带来的内容真实性问题，提出3D虚假检测的概念，并构建了Fake3DGS数据集。该数据集包含通过几何、外观和空间布局操控生成的高真实感3D场景图像。研究发现，现有2D检测方法难以区分真实与3D篡改图像，因此引入一种基于多视角一致性和高斯点云特征的3D感知检测方法，实验表明该方法显著提升了3D内容的识别效果，强调了超越2D证据的认证技术的重要性。相关代码和数据已公开。

item.title: Cognitive Digital Shadows：分析大型语言模型生成话语的合成语料库
item.content:encoded:
CDS是用于研究LLM在不同社会情境下输出变化的合成语料库。



摘要： Cognitive Digital Shadows (CDS) 是一个包含19万个记录的合成语料库，用于分析大型语言模型（LLMs）在不同社会和情境提示下的生成话语。每个记录由19个LLMs生成，分别模拟人类角色或AI助手。CDS涵盖疫苗/医疗、社交媒体虚假信息、科学领域的性别差距及STEM刻板印象等四个争议性社会议题。每条记录包含17种社会人口和心理属性，支持对LLM语言、立场和推理的分析。语料经过主题锚定验证，并可通过可解释的NLP技术进行情感分析。CDS还提供交互式平台，便于群体层面的情感与语义框架比较，有助于未来对LLM偏见、社会敏感性和对齐性的审计。

item.title: InvariRank：一种具有排列不变性的大语言模型推荐重排序框架
item.content:encoded:
InvariRank解决LLM推荐重排序中的顺序敏感问题，提升排名稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出InvariRank，一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的推荐重排序框架，旨在解决LLM在推荐系统中因候选集顺序变化导致的评分不稳定问题。通过引入结构化注意力掩码和共享位置框架，InvariRank实现了排列不变性，避免了依赖多组候选集排列的训练目标。实验表明，该方法在保持推荐效果的同时，显著提升了排名的稳定性，为高效可靠的LLM推荐系统提供了新路径。

item.title: 机器集体智能在科学方程发现中的应用
item.content:encoded:
机器集体智能可自主发现科学方程，提升可解释性和泛化能力。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为机器集体智能的统一范式，结合符号主义和元启发式方法，实现科学方程的自主演化发现。该方法通过多个推理代理协同生成、评估、批判和整合符号假设，无需依赖人工领域知识即可自动恢复确定性、随机或未知动力系统的控制方程。相比深度神经网络，其方程将外推误差降低了六个数量级，并将数百万个模型参数压缩为5至40个可解释参数，标志着AI在自主发现科学原理方面的重要进展。

item.title: 长周期大模型任务中的知识状态协调架构研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出ADEMA架构解决长周期任务中知识状态漂移问题。



摘要： 本文针对长周期大模型任务中知识状态漂移、中间承诺不明确和中断导致证据链断裂的问题，提出ADEMA知识状态协调架构。该架构融合显式知识管理、异构双评估治理、自适应任务模式切换、声誉驱动资源分配等机制，强调显式知识状态转换、证据承载的成果进展和可恢复的连续性。实验基于四个场景展示包、固定60次运行机制矩阵及修复后的协议级基准进行验证，结果表明检查点恢复机制在中断敏感情况下至关重要，而双评估、段合成和动态治理则作为支持控制机制，促进轨迹纪律和成本质量行为。

item.title: 基于流图的生成模型引导方法
item.content:encoded:
提出FMRG方法，提升生成模型效率与效果。



摘要： 本文将生成模型中的引导问题重新定义为确定性最优控制问题，提出了一种无需训练、单轨迹的Flow Map Reward Guidance（FMRG）方法。该方法利用流图实现对生成过程的集成与引导，在文本到图像任务中，FMRG在仅需3个数值积分步骤的情况下，表现优于或等于现有基准，速度提升至少一个数量级。此方法简化了传统多粒子、多步骤方案，为高效生成提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于视频的事件数据提取方法SnapLog
item.content:encoded:
SnapLog通过视频帧特征向量和时间分割提取事件数据。



摘要： 本文提出SnapLog方法，用于从视频中提取可分析的事件数据。该方法利用图像嵌入将视频帧转换为特征向量，并通过帧间相似性矩阵进行时间分割。随后使用广义少样本分类对视频片段进行标签分配，生成带有时间戳的事件序列。传统流程挖掘技术可用于分析这些数据，实验表明该方法能准确反映视频中的流程。

item.title: 基于脑活动基础模型的逆向解码与刺激生成研究
item.content:encoded:
通过脑活动模型逆向恢复刺激参数，验证神经编码质量。



摘要： 本文探讨了基础脑活动模型在逆向任务中的应用，即从合成脑活动中恢复刺激属性。研究使用TRIBEv2模型与大型语言模型结合，通过模拟推断方法建立脑图到潜在刺激参数的映射。实验结果表明，可以从中恢复出语言参数如情感、唤醒度和支配性，证明了神经编码的质量，并展示了语言模型作为可控刺激生成器的潜力。该研究为基于基础脑模型的解码与逆向设计提供了重要进展。

item.title: 大型语言模型对边缘科学材料的易受影响性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究显示大语言模型易受边缘科学内容影响，可能产生误导性回答。



摘要： 本研究探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在回答科学问题时的可靠性，并展示了它们如何容易受到边缘科学材料的影响。研究人员通过调整自定义LLMs，使其优先参考特定的边缘论文，涉及精细结构常数和引力波。与领域专家和标准LLMs相比，修改后的模型生成了流畅且有说服力但与科学共识相悖的回答，且不易被非专家识别为错误信息。研究结果表明，LLMs存在被操控的脆弱性，无法替代专家判断，这可能对公众科学理解及错误信息传播构成风险。

item.title: 基于多源知识的诊断推理框架MultiDx研究
item.content:encoded:
MultiDx通过多源知识实现更准确的诊断推理。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为MultiDx的两阶段诊断推理框架，旨在解决大型语言模型在医疗诊断中因领域知识不足而表现不佳的问题。该框架通过从网络搜索、SOAP格式病历和临床病例数据库等多个知识源中收集证据，生成疑似诊断和推理路径，并通过匹配、投票和差异诊断整合多视角证据，最终得出诊断结果。实验表明，该方法在两个公开基准数据集上均表现出色，提升了诊断推理的准确性与适应性。

item.title: PAD-Rec：面向生成式推荐的感知位置草案解码方法
item.content:encoded:
PAD-Rec提升生成式推荐的解码速度，保持推荐质量。



摘要： 本文提出PAD-Rec，一种用于生成式推荐的感知位置草案解码方法。该方法通过引入位置嵌入和门控机制，增强草案模型对物品内位置和推测深度的感知能力，从而在不改变目标分布的前提下提高解码效率。实验表明，PAD-Rec在四个真实数据集上实现了最高3.1倍的运行时加速，并在保持推荐质量的同时提升了约5%的速度增益。

item.title: 基于图神经网络与Koopman理论的非地面网络SDN框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种新型SDN框架提升非地面网络管理效率。



摘要： 文章探讨了由于地面网络限制，非地面网络（如低轨卫星星座）在大规模部署中面临的管理瓶颈。为解决这一问题，作者提出了一种可扩展的分层软件定义网络（SDN）框架，结合图神经网络（GNN）和Koopman理论，用于压缩星座拓扑并预测时空行为。通过引入图Koop曼自编码器（GKAE），该框架能够在每个轨道层内进行线性化预测，并由中央SDN控制器进行全局协调。实验结果表明，该方法在星链星座上的空间压缩和时间预测性能分别提升了42.8%和10.81%，且模型规模更小。

item.title: 基于强化学习的电动车网约车调度优化研究
item.content:encoded:
通过强化学习实现电动车网约车在充电限制下的高效调度。



摘要： 本文研究了城市规模下电动车网约车车队的控制问题，考虑了调度、重新定位和充电决策在不确定需求和交通时间条件下的约束。文章将问题建模为一个六边形网格半马尔可夫决策过程（semi-MDP），结合离散动作和连续充电功率，并引入时间限制的混合整数线性规划确保物理可行性。通过优化Soft Actor-Critic（SAC）代理对抗Wasserstein-1模糊集，提升策略鲁棒性。实验表明，该方法在纽约市出租车数据模拟中实现了最高净收益，达到122万美元，优于其他基准方法。

item.content:encoded:
提出统一理论框架，简化概念提取的可识别性证明。



摘要： 本文提出一种统一的理论框架，用于无监督概念提取，将概念提取任务建模为生成模型的识别过程。作者提出一个通用的元定理，将可识别性保证的建立问题转化为两个集合交集的表征问题。该方法在多种常用方法上得到验证，显著简化了证明过程，为开发新的、基于原理的概念提取方法奠定了基础。

item.title: 基于可观测性理论的连续时间因果推断方法
item.content:encoded:
研究连续时间决策中的因果推断，解决隐藏混杂因素问题。



摘要： 本文探讨了在存在隐藏混杂因素的情况下，如何通过可观测性理论实现连续时间序列决策中的因果推断。作者提出了一种基于观测数据重构潜在动态的方法，并引入了可观察神经微分方程（ObsNODEs），用于学习可从观测中重建的状态动态，从而预测不同治疗路径下的结果。实验表明，该方法在合成癌症数据、半合成MIMIC-IV数据和真实脓毒症数据上均表现出色。

item.title: 多领域零售账单OCR智能处理系统研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种多领域零售账单OCR处理系统，提升识别准确率与效率。



摘要： 本文针对多领域零售账单数字化的挑战，提出了一种智能、质量感知的OCR处理流程。该系统包括基于CNN的图像增强模块、图像质量分析器、自适应反馈循环和NLP后处理层。实验在5个商业领域的360张真实账单图像上进行，采用OCR集成多数投票生成真实数据。结果表明，该系统在字符错误率（CER）和词错误率（WER）上分别优于原始Tesseract 26.4%和31.2%，并具备更高的文本密度、更低的噪声比和更快的处理速度。图像质量分析显示，增强后的中等和低质量图像PSNR平均达到28.7 dB，验证了系统的有效性。

item.title: 基于往返验证的自然语言形式化忠实性评估方法
item.content:encoded:
通过往返验证提升自然语言形式化的忠实性。



摘要： 本文提出一种无需真实标注数据的往返验证方法，用于评估大语言模型在将自然语言形式化时的忠实性。该方法包括将陈述形式化、翻译回自然语言、再形式化，并使用形式化工具检查逻辑等价性。若两次形式化结果一致，则表明形式化过程是忠实的；若不一致，则通过诊断步骤识别失败阶段并进行针对性修复。实验在150条交通规则上进行，结果显示诊断引导的修复显著提升了形式等价率，优于随机修复基线。独立的NLI分析也表明形式等价与语义漂移密切相关。

item.content:encoded:

item.content:encoded:
提出C-Bench和O-Bench评估布局引导图像生成模型性能。

item.title: HuM-Eval: A Coarse-to-Fine Framework for Human-Centric Video Evaluation
item.content:encoded: Video generation models have developed rapidly in recent years, where generating natural human motion plays a pivotal role. However, accurately evaluating the quality of generated human motion video remains a significant challenge. Existing evaluation metrics primarily focus on global scene statistics, often overlooking fine-grained human details and consequently failing to align with human subjective preference. To bridge this gap, we propose HuM-Eval, a novel human-centric evaluation framework that adopts a coarse-to-fine strategy. Specifically, our framework first utilizes a Vision Language Model to perform a coarse assessment of global video quality. It then proceeds to a fine-grained analysis, using 2D pose to verify anatomical correctness and 3D human motion to evaluate motion stability. Extensive experiments demonstrate that HuM-Eval achieves an average human correlation of 58.2%, outperforming state-of-the-art baselines. Furthermore, we introduce HuM-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark comprising 1,000 diverse prompts, and conduct a detailed evaluation of existing text-to-video models, paving the way for next-generation human motion generation.

item.title: Ceci n'est pas une explication: Evaluating Explanation Failures as Explainability Pitfalls in Language Learning Systems
item.content:encoded: AI-powered language learning tools increasingly provide instant, personalised feedback to millions of learners worldwide. However, this feedback can fail in ways that are difficult for learners--and even teachers--to detect, potentially reinforcing misconceptions and eroding learning outcomes over extended use. We present a portion of L2-Bench, a benchmark for evaluating AI systems in language education that includes (but is not limited to) six critical dimensions of effective feedback: diagnostic accuracy, awareness of appropriacy, causes of error, prioritisation, guidance for improvement, and supporting self-regulation. We analyse how AI systems can fail with respect to these dimensions. These failures, which we argue are conducive to "explainability pitfalls," are AI-generated explanations that appear helpful on the surface but are fundamentally flawed, increasing the risk of attainment, human-AI interaction, and socioaffective harms. We discuss how the specific context of language learning amplifies these risks and outline open questions we believe merit more attention when designing evaluation frameworks specifically. Our analysis aims to expand the community's understanding of both the typology of explainability pitfalls and the contextual dynamics in which they may occur in order to encourage AI developers to better design safe, trustworthy, and effective AI explanations.

item.title: QEVA: A Reference-Free Evaluation Metric for Narrative Video Summarization with Multimodal Question Answering
item.content:encoded: Video-to-text summarization remains underexplored in terms of comprehensive evaluation methods. Traditional n-gram overlap-based metrics and recent large language model (LLM)-based approaches depend heavily on human-written reference summaries, limiting their practicality and sensitivity to nuanced semantic aspects. In this paper, we propose QEVA, a reference-free metric evaluating candidate summaries directly against source videos through multimodal question answering. QEVA assesses summaries along three clear dimensions: Coverage, Factuality, and Chronology. We also introduce MLVU(VS)-Eval, a new annotated benchmark derived from the MLVU dataset, comprising 800 summaries generated from 200 videos using state-of-the-art video-language multimodal models. This dataset establishes a transparent and consistent framework for evaluation. Experimental results demonstrate that QEVA shows higher correlation with human judgments compared to existing approaches, as measured by Kendall's $τ_b$, $τ_c$, and Spearman's $ρ$. We hope that our benchmark and metric will facilitate meaningful progress in video-to-text summarization research and provide valuable insights for the development of future evaluation methods.

item.title: A Quantitative Confirmation of the Currier Language Distinction
item.content:encoded: We present a quantitative analysis of character-pair substitution ratios in the Voynich manuscript, testing whether Currier's A/B language distinction (1976) reflects a genuine structural property of the text. A Beta-Binomial mixture model applied to raw character counts without access to labels recovers the Currier split with ARI = 0.383. A supervised Beta-Binomial classifier trained on a subset of folios predicts the A/B identity of held-out folios at 89.2% accuracy. The character pairs separate into three functional regimes that constrain any theory of the Voynich writing system.

item.title: Dynamic UGV-UAV Cooperative Path Planning in Uncertain Environments
item.content:encoded: This paper addresses the Dynamic UGV-UAV Cooperative Path Planning (DUCPP) problem involving one unmanned ground vehicle (UGV) assisted by one or more unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) operating on an uncertain road network with potentially impassable edges. DUCPP is particularly relevant for scenarios such as disaster response, emergency supply transport, and rescue operations, where a UGV must reach a specified destination in the presence of partially unknown road conditions. To enable the UGV to travel safely and efficiently to its destination, the UAV(s) dynamically inspect edges in the environment to identify and prune damaged or impassable edges from consideration. We present multiple strategies, including a bidirectional approach, to optimize UGV-UAV cooperation for finding a safe path in an uncertain road network. Furthermore, we explore the impact of using multiple UAVs on reducing the UGV's travel time, and evaluate the associated computation time. The proposed strategies are implemented and evaluated on 100 urban road networks. The results demonstrate that the bidirectional strategy achieves the best performance in most instances, and using multiple UAVs further reduces UGV travel time at the expense of increased computation time. This paper presents a robust framework for DUCPP to achieve efficient UGV-UAV cooperation for path planning and inspection, offering practical solutions for navigation in challenging and uncertain conditions.

item.title: Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Efficient and Open Multimodal Intelligence
item.content:encoded: We introduce Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, the latest model in the Nemotron multimodal series and the first to natively support audio inputs alongside text, images, and video. Nemotron 3 Nano Omni delivers consistent accuracy improvements over its predecessor, Nemotron Nano V2 VL, across all modalities, enabled by advances in architecture, training data and recipes. In particular, Nemotron 3 delivers leading results in real-world document understanding, long audio-video comprehension, and agentic computer use. Built on the highly efficient Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B backbone, Nemotron 3 Nano Omni further incorporates innovative multimodal token-reduction techniques to deliver substantially lower inference latency and higher throughput than other models of similar size. We are releasing model checkpoints in BF16, FP8, and FP4 formats, along with portions of the training data and codebase to facilitate further research and development.

item.title: Hyper-Dimensional Fingerprints as Molecular Representations
item.content:encoded: Computational molecular representations underpin virtual screening, property prediction, and materials discovery. Conventional fingerprints are efficient and deterministic but lose structural information through hash-based compression, particularly at low dimensionalities. Learned representations from graph neural networks recover this expressiveness but require task-specific training and substantial computational resources. Here we introduce hyperdimensional fingerprints (HDF), which replace the learned transformations of message-passing neural networks with algebraic operations on high-dimensional vectors, producing deterministic molecular representations without any training. Across diverse property prediction benchmarks, HDF outperforms conventional fingerprints in the majority of tasks while exhibiting greater consistency across datasets and models. Crucially, HDF embeddings preserve molecular similarity faithfully: at 32 dimensions, distances in HDF space achieve a 0.9 Pearson correlation with graph edit distance, compared to 0.55 for Morgan fingerprints at equivalent size. This structural fidelity persists at low dimensions where hash-based methods degrade, allowing simple nearest-neighbor regression to remain predictive with as few as 64 components. We further demonstrate the practical impact in Bayesian molecular optimization, where HDF-based surrogate models achieve substantially improved sample efficiency in regimes where Morgan fingerprints perform comparably to random search. HDF thus provides a general-purpose, training-free alternative to conventional molecular fingerprints, suggesting that the information loss long accepted as inherent to fixed-length fingerprints is a limitation of the hash-based encoding scheme rather than the fingerprint paradigm itself.

item.title: On the Expressive Power of GNNs to Solve Linear SDPs
item.content:encoded: Semidefinite programs (SDPs) are a powerful framework for convex optimization and for constructing strong relaxations of hard combinatorial problems. However, solving large SDPs can be computationally expensive, motivating the use of machine learning models as fast computational surrogates. Graph neural networks (GNNs) are a natural candidate in this setting due to their sparsity-awareness and ability to model variable-constraint interactions. In this work, we study what expressive power is sufficient to recover optimal SDP solutions. We first prove negative results showing that standard GNN architectures fail on recovering linear SDP solutions. We then identify a more expressive architecture that captures the key structure of SDPs and can, in particular, emulate the updates of a standard first-order solver. Empirically, on both synthetic and \textsc{SdpLib} benchmarks of various classes of SDPs, this more expressive architecture achieves consistently lower prediction error and objective gap than theoretically weaker baselines. Finally, using the learned high-quality predictions to warm-start the first-order solver yields practical speedups of up to 80%.

item.title: 工业环境中LLM驱动的自动化代码审查评估研究
item.content:encoded:
评估工业场景中LLM驱动的自动化代码审查工具的有效性。



摘要： 本文研究了在工业软件开发中，如何可靠且大规模地评估基于大语言模型（LLM）的自动化代码审查（ACR）工具的效果。通过分析Beko公司提供的2,604条机器人生成的PR评论数据，并由软件工程师标记为“已修复”或“不修复”，作者比较了两种自动化评估方法：G-Eval和LLM-as-a-Judge。结果显示，不同模型（如Gemini-2.5-pro、GPT-4.1-mini、GPT-5.2）与人工标签的对齐度仅达到中等水平，说明自动化评估仍存在局限性。此外，开发者的行为不仅反映评论质量，还受到上下文约束、优先级决策和工作流动态的影响，使得这些信号难以作为客观基准。访谈进一步证实了工作流压力和组织限制对开发者标注行为的显著影响。

item.title: 多平面HyperX网络在AI数据中心中的性能研究
item.content:encoded:
多平面HyperX网络相比现有拓扑具有更小的网络直径和更高的成本效益。



摘要： 本文研究了多平面HyperX网络在AI数据中心的应用，指出其相较于多平面Fat-Tree、Dragonfly及Dragonfly+等现有网络拓扑，具有更小的网络直径和更好的成本效益。多平面架构通过利用单个网卡或多个网卡的多个端口，为系统提供多个独立网络平面，从而提升整体性能。此前未有研究探讨多平面技术在直接网络如HyperX中的应用，本文填补了这一空白，并验证了其优越性。

item.title: 无需调优的运行时安全监控方法LCF在大语言模型中的应用
item.content:encoded:
LCF通过隐藏状态轨迹检测模型异常，提升运行时安全性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种无需调优的运行时监控方法Layerwise Convergence Fingerprinting (LCF)，用于检测大语言模型在运行时可能发生的异常行为，如后门、越狱和提示注入。LCF通过分析模型各层之间的隐藏状态轨迹，计算马氏距离并进行收缩聚合，以12-16%的后门误报率和小于0.1%的推理开销有效降低了攻击成功率，同时适用于多种模型架构和威胁类型，具备良好的通用性和实用性。

item.title: LAVA：一种鲁棒的音视频防篡改水印框架
item.content:encoded:
LAVA通过音视频融合提升深度伪造检测与定位的可靠性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为LAVA的音视频防篡改水印框架，旨在解决现有方法在压缩和多模态错位下的脆弱性问题。LAVA通过跨模态水印融合和校准感知对齐，确保在压缩和音频视频不同步情况下仍能保持可靠的篡改证据，从而实现更鲁棒的篡改定位。实验表明，LAVA在检测性能（AP=0.999）和抗压缩能力方面均优于现有方法，显著提升了多模态融合基线的可靠性。

item.title: GSAR：一种基于证据类型的多智能体系统可信评估框架
item.content:encoded:
GSAR 提出一种新的多智能体系统可信评估方法，提升诊断报告的可靠性。



摘要： GSAR 是一种用于评估多智能体大语言模型系统可信度的框架，通过将声明分类为四类（有依据、无依据、矛盾、互补），赋予不同证据类型权重，并计算加权可信度分数。该框架结合三阶段决策机制，控制后续操作，在有限计算预算内优化系统表现。实验表明，GSAR 在 FEVER 数据集上表现出色，优于现有方法，是首个将证据类型评分与分层恢复机制结合的框架。

item.title: 基于大语言模型的自主强化学习框架研究
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨基于LLM的自主强化学习框架及其未来发展方向。



摘要： 本文探讨了强化学习（RL）从传统模式向基于大语言模型（LLMs）的自主代理范式的转变。这种新框架强调构建具备目标设定、长期规划、动态策略适应和交互推理能力的自主代理，以应对复杂、开放的任务环境。与传统RL依赖静态目标和周期性交互不同，LLM-based Agentic RL将元推理、自我反思和多步骤决策等认知能力融入学习过程。文章深入分析了该趋势的概念基础、方法创新及有效设计，并指出了关键挑战与未来研究方向。

item.title: 基于记忆图的Transformer解码器结构研究
item.content:encoded:
通过记忆图替代FFN子层构建新型解码器模型。



摘要： 本文提出Graph Memory Transformer (GMT)，在保持自回归架构不变的前提下，用显式学习的记忆图替代传统的FFN子层。该模型利用记忆单元对token表示进行路由，通过学习的中心点和转移矩阵实现信息传递。实验显示，GMT具有82.2M参数，无密集FFN结构，在验证损失和困惑度上略逊于基准模型，但在零样本任务中表现接近。研究旨在验证图结构在语言模型中的可行性与可解释性，未来工作将聚焦于扩展规模和优化性能。

item.title: 基于迁移学习的量子设备噪声建模与误差缓解研究
item.content:encoded:
利用迁移学习实现跨量子设备的噪声建模与误差缓解。



摘要： 本文研究在NISQ阶段下，通过迁移学习方法将一个量子设备上学习到的噪声模型应用到另一个设备上。研究使用了两个IBM量子设备的数据集，训练了一个残差神经网络以映射噪声输出到理想结果。实验表明，零样本迁移的KL散度较高，但通过少量微调样本可显著降低散度，提升误差缓解效果。分析发现，CX门错误是跨设备不匹配的主要原因。研究证明量子噪声可通过少量样本进行学习和优化，为跨设备量子误差缓解提供了可行方案。

item.title: STELLAR-E：一种自动化生成高质量合成数据集的系统
item.content:encoded:
STELLAR-E可自动生成高质量合成数据集，用于LLM评估。



摘要： 文章介绍了STELLAR-E，一个完全自动化的系统，用于生成定制大小的高质量合成数据集，无需依赖现有数据集。该系统分为两个阶段：首先通过修改TGRT Self-Instruct框架生成可控的合成数据；其次通过统计和LLM指标评估数据集的适用性。实验表明，STELLAR-E生成的数据集在LLM评分上比现有基准高出5.7%，具有与真实数据相当的质量，适用于大、小规模LLM的全面评估。该方法提供了比人工更高效、可扩展的评估框架。

item.title: 图世界模型：统一框架与分类研究
item.content:encoded:
本文系统梳理图世界模型，提出基于关系归纳偏置的分类方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了传统世界模型在噪声敏感性、误差累积和推理能力方面的不足，并提出使用图结构来构建更高效的虚拟环境表示。文章首次将基于图的世界模型（GWMs）作为一个统一的研究范式进行系统化分析，提出了基于关系归纳偏置（RIB）的分类体系，包括空间、物理和逻辑三类结构先验。针对每种类别，文章总结了关键设计原则、代表性模型并进行了对比分析，同时指出了未来研究方向，如动态图适应、概率关系动力学等。

item.title: 面向自主代理的政策执行授权架构研究
item.content:encoded:
提出PEA架构以系统级方式确保AI代理安全。



摘要： 本文探讨了前沿AI系统可能出现的代理对齐问题，即系统在无用户指令的情况下生成并执行有害行为。现有方法如RLHF和宪法提示主要在模型层面操作，仅提供概率性安全保障。为此，作者提出了Policy-Execution-Authorization（PEA）架构，通过分权设计在系统层面强制安全机制。PEA将意图生成、授权和执行分离为独立层，并通过加密能力令牌连接。文章提出了五个核心贡献：意图验证层、意图溯源追踪、目标漂移检测、输出语义门以及形式化验证框架，证明即使在对抗性模型攻击下，目标完整性仍可保持。该架构将代理对齐从行为属性转变为结构约束，为自主代理治理提供了坚实基础。

item.title: 分布匹配中漂移场的可识别性与稳定性分析
item.content:encoded:
研究生成漂移框架中漂移场的可识别性与稳定性，揭示其与概率测度的关系。



摘要： 本文分析了Deng等人提出的生成漂移框架中分布匹配的漂移场的可识别性和稳定性。作者引入了一类伴随椭圆核，包括拉普拉斯核，并证明了在该类核和任意两个Borel概率测度下，漂移场为零当且仅当这两个测度相等。进一步证明该类核恰好是高斯核和Matérn核（ν≥1/2）。通过构造反例，展示了质量可能逃逸到无穷远但场趋于零的情况，表明仅控制场范数不足以保证弱收敛。然而，唯一可能的失败模式被限制在一维射线{c p:0≤c≤1}，通过施加内在重叠标量的渐近下界可恢复弱收敛。

item.title: 企业文档AI系统评估与基准测试
item.content:encoded:
评估企业文档AI系统的整体性能及关键指标。



摘要： 本文介绍了EnterpriseDocBench，一个用于评估企业文档AI系统整体性能的基准测试工具。该工具涵盖解析、索引、检索和生成四个阶段，并在六个企业领域的真实数据集上进行测试。实验对比了BM25、密集嵌入和混合检索三种方法，结果显示混合检索表现最佳。研究还发现，幻觉现象与文档长度呈非线性关系，且各阶段间相关性较弱，表明系统质量并非如预期那样层层递进。此外，系统在事实准确性方面表现良好，但答案完整性较低，这对实际部署具有重要意义。最后，文章描述了三种参考架构，并计划开源相关框架和工具。

item.title: 基于视频语言框架的意图识别方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出IntentVLM框架，提升人机交互中的意图理解效果。



摘要： 本文提出IntentVLM，一种用于开放词汇人类意图识别的两阶段视频语言框架。该方法受认知科学中前向-逆向建模启发，将意图理解分解为目标候选生成和结构化推理，有效减少隐式推理中的幻觉问题。在IntentQA和Inst-IT Bench数据集上的实验表明，IntentVLM达到80%的准确率，比基线模型高出30%，并接近人类表现。研究表明，该结构化推理方法在不产生灾难性遗忘的情况下提升了开放词汇意图理解能力，为人机协作提供了可靠基础。

item.title: 基于偏好反馈的贝叶斯优化方法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于偏好反馈的贝叶斯优化方法，性能与传统方法相当。



摘要： 本文提出一种利用偏好反馈（如成对比较）的贝叶斯优化方法，采用汤普森采样（TS）策略，通过单调链接函数建模潜在效用差异，并利用基础核函数生成对抗核。理论分析表明，该方法在有限时间内表现与传统基于标量反馈的TS方法相当。研究还引入了双TS配对变体，并在合成和真实数据集上验证了方法的有效性。

item.title: ScaleBox：提升大规模代码训练效率与准确性的系统
item.content:encoded:
ScaleBox提升代码验证准确性与效率，增强大模型训练效果。



摘要： 文章介绍了ScaleBox，一个高保真、可扩展的代码沙盒系统，旨在解决现有系统在高并发负载下验证不准确和效率低的问题。ScaleBox通过自动化特殊判断生成、细粒度并行执行以及配置驱动的评估套件，显著提升了代码验证的准确性和效率。实验表明，ScaleBox在LiveCodeBench上的性能和训练稳定性优于传统方法，为大规模代码训练提供了可靠且高效的基础设施。

item.title: LLM版本比较中的可靠变化分析
item.content:encoded:
LLM版本间性能变化存在显著差异，需关注个体项级变化。



摘要： 本文将临床心理学中的可靠变化指数（RCI）应用于2000个MMLU-Pro项目的LLM版本比较中，分析了Llama 3到3.1和Qwen 2.5到3的性能变化。结果显示，大多数项目没有显著变化，但部分项目出现双向变化，且效果量较大。低准确度项目有所提升，高准确度项目则下降。不同领域表现不一，如Llama在物理领域退步，Qwen在法律领域退步。单次评估可能遗漏真实变化，建议报告变化率以补充整体准确率。

item.title: 基于扩散模型的3D人体姿态预测方法SnapPose3D
item.content:encoded:
SnapPose3D通过生成多假设并聚合实现更准确的3D姿态预测。



摘要： 文章提出了一种名为SnapPose3D的3D人体姿态预测框架，旨在解决2D到3D姿态提升中的深度模糊和联合不确定性问题。该方法利用扩散模型生成多个可能的3D姿态假设，并通过聚合得到最终准确结果。与以往依赖时间序列的方法不同，SnapPose3D仅使用单帧图像作为输入，降低了计算成本和数据获取复杂度。实验表明，SnapPose3D在多个基准测试中表现优异，能够有效生成并整合高精度姿态假设。

item.title: 基于物理信息神经算子的相对论磁流体动力学模拟研究
item.content:encoded:
神经算子用于高精度相对论磁流体模拟，提升计算效率。



摘要： 本文研究了利用物理信息傅里叶神经算子（PINO）和OFormer风格的Transformer神经算子对相对论磁流体动力学（MHD）进行建模的可能性。通过在Orszag-Tang涡旋中训练PINO模型，能够有效捕捉等离子体形成过程，优于仅依赖数据的基线模型。此外，在脊鞘相对论喷流的模拟中，神经算子直接应用于自适应网格，展示了其在高分辨率下的有效性。这是首次将神经算子应用于相对论MHD的自适应网格模拟。

item.title: EgoMAGIC：用于医疗任务指导的增强现实数据集
item.content:encoded:
EgoMAGIC是用于医疗任务指导的增强现实数据集，包含3,355段视频。



摘要： 本文介绍了EgoMAGIC数据集，该数据集是DARPA PTG项目的一部分，旨在开发集成于增强现实眼镜中的虚拟助手，以帮助用户完成复杂任务。数据集包含3,355段视频，涵盖50项医疗任务，每项任务至少有50段标注视频。数据集已发布，并附带针对8项医疗任务的动作检测挑战。视频使用头戴式立体相机录制，包含音频。基于此数据集，训练了40个YOLO模型，检测124种医疗物体。本文还提供了三种模型在动作检测任务上的基线结果，最佳方法的mAP为0.526。该数据集还可用于动作识别、物体检测、错误检测等计算机视觉任务。

item.title: 基于机器学习的涡轮发动机剩余寿命估计比较研究
item.content:encoded:
本文比较了多种机器学习方法在NASA C-MAPSS数据集上的剩余寿命预测性能。



摘要： 本文对多种机器学习方法在NASA C-MAPSS涡轮发动机数据集上的剩余寿命（RUL）估计性能进行了比较研究，包括经典模型（岭回归、多项式岭回归和XGBoost）、1D卷积神经网络（CNN）和长短期记忆网络（LSTM）。所有模型在FD001和FD003子集上进行评估，采用相同的预处理流程以确保公平性。实验结果显示，LSTM在两个子集上的RMSE分别为14.93和14.20，优于之前报道的深层LSTM模型。1D CNN在FD003上表现良好，但预测结果较为保守。XGBoost在FD003上表现出色，显示出非线性建模的优势。

item.title: 基于开源工作流的幻灯片语音头像教学实践研究
item.content:encoded:
研究提出一种用于教学的语音头像生成方法，提升在线教学互动性。



摘要： 本文探讨了在在线、混合和异步教学环境中，如何通过开源工作流创建语音幻灯片头像来增强教学效果。该方法结合了文本转语音和音频驱动的图像合成技术，使教师能够将脚本和静态照片转化为可嵌入课件的短篇叙述视频。研究不仅关注技术实现，还从数字教学、审美教育和艺术科技交叉的角度分析了语音头像作为多模态传播工具的价值。文章详细描述了制作流程，评估其沟通与美学潜力，并提出了关于脚本长度、图像选择、节奏控制等方面的实用指南。研究贡献包括一个面向教育者的开源模型、对语音头像作为教学设计问题的重新定义，以及负责任地引入生成式合成媒体的教学路径。

item.title: 文化感知机器翻译评估与CanMT数据集研究
item.content:encoded:
研究文化感知机器翻译性能及CanMT数据集的应用。



摘要： 本文介绍了CanMT，一个用于机器翻译的文化感知平行语料库，并提出了一种多维度的评估框架来衡量文化翻译质量。通过CanMT对多种大型语言模型和翻译系统进行系统评估，发现不同模型在文化相关翻译任务中表现差异显著，翻译策略对模型行为有系统性影响。研究还表明，不同类型的文化特定内容翻译难度不同，且模型在识别文化知识与实际翻译应用之间仍存在差距。此外，参考译文的引入显著提升了LLM作为评估者的可靠性，凸显其在文化感知翻译评估中的关键作用。

item.title: 临床场景下视觉语言模型的可靠性审计与改进研究
item.content:encoded:
视觉语言模型在医学VQA任务中表现不佳，需提升感知能力。



摘要： 本文对五种前沿视觉语言模型在医学视觉问答任务中的表现进行了审计，发现其在解剖和病理目标定位上表现较差，最佳模型仅达到0.23平均IoU和19.1%的Acc@0.5。同时存在严重的左右混淆问题。自接地管道导致所有模型的VQA准确率下降，主要由于定位不准确和格式合规失败。使用真实标注可恢复并提升准确率，表明问题主要出在感知模块。对Qwen 2.5 VL进行微调后，在Med-VQA任务中取得了85.5%的最高开放召回率，显示VQA差距可通过领域适配解决，但感知和信任瓶颈仍需进一步研究。

item.title: Libra-VLA：一种分层的视觉-语言-动作模型用于机器人操作
item.content:encoded:
提出分层VLA模型提升机器人操作能力。



摘要： 本文提出Libra-VLA，一种基于粗到细双系统架构的视觉-语言-动作模型，旨在解决传统模型在机器人操作中语义与执行之间存在的巨大差距。该模型将复杂动作分解为离散的宏观方向和连续的微观对齐，通过分离学习复杂度实现训练平衡，并采用异步执行策略提升系统的可扩展性、鲁棒性和响应性。实验表明，当两个子系统的学习难度平衡时，性能达到最佳。

item.title: 面向LLM代理的分层长期语义记忆框架HLTM
item.content:encoded:
HLTM提升LLM代理的长期记忆能力，优化个性化交互。



摘要： 本文介绍了面向大型语言模型（LLM）代理的分层长期语义记忆（HLTM）框架，旨在解决工业级长期记忆系统的五个关键挑战：可扩展性、低延迟检索、隐私约束、跨领域泛化性和可观测性。HLTM通过结构化的记忆树组织文本数据，实现多粒度语义知识存储，并支持隐私保护和透明溯源。此外，该框架还包含适应机制以增强跨场景泛化能力。在LinkedIn招聘助手上的评估显示，HLTM显著提升了回答准确性和检索F1值，同时优化了查询与索引延迟之间的平衡。目前，HLTM已部署于LinkedIn招聘流程中，用于支持核心个性化功能。

item.title: DKnownAI Guard在AI代理安全场景中的性能评估
item.content:encoded:
DKnownAI Guard在AI安全检测中表现最佳。



摘要： 本报告对DKnownAI Guard在AI代理安全场景中的表现进行了评估，并与AWS Bedrock Guardrails、Azure Content Safety和Lakera Guard进行比较。通过人工标注作为基准，评估各防护系统在检测代理自身风险（如指令覆盖、间接注入、工具滥用）以及有害内容请求（如仇恨言论、色情、暴力）方面的效果。结果显示，DKnownAI Guard在召回率上达到96.5%，真负率（TNR）为90.4%，整体性能最优。

item.title: 知识蒸馏需关注损失：评估学生模型应考虑教师能力的保留
item.content:encoded:
知识蒸馏应关注教师能力的保留，而不仅是任务性能。



摘要： 本文主张知识蒸馏过程中应关注模型在训练中丢失的能力，而不仅仅是保留的任务性能指标。随着大型模型被广泛用于部署系统，仅依赖于任务得分可能掩盖了不确定性、边界行为、可靠性、隐私、安全等方面的损失。文章指出当前评估方法基于一个假设，即蒸馏是教师行为的忠实复制，但实际上应视为一种有损投影。作者提出了一种分类体系，总结了蒸馏过程中的非指标性损失，并建议设定特定场景下的保留目标及蒸馏损失声明，以实现可问责的知识蒸馏。

item.title: SymphonyGen：基于3D分层框架的交响乐生成模型
item.content:encoded:
SymphonyGen通过分层架构提升交响乐生成效率与表现力。



摘要： 本文介绍了SymphonyGen，一种用于当代电影配乐生成的3D分层框架。该模型采用级联解码器架构，将小节、轨道和事件轴分解，提高了计算效率和可扩展性。通过“短谱”条件输入，实现结构控制同时保持音色多样性，并引入GRPO算法优化符号输出与现代听觉期望的一致性。此外，还设计了避免不和谐的采样算法，提升和声清晰度。实验表明，SymphonyGen在音乐性和管弦乐生成方面优于基线模型。

item.title: DataPRM：提升动态数据分析任务中LLM推理能力的环境感知奖励模型
item.content:encoded:
DataPRM增强LLM在动态数据分析中的推理能力，有效检测错误并优化训练。



摘要： 本文提出DataPRM，一种环境感知的生成式过程奖励模型，旨在提升大型语言模型在动态数据分析任务中的表现。研究发现，通用领域奖励模型难以有效监督数据分析师代理，容易忽略隐性错误并误罚探索性行为。DataPRM通过主动验证和反思感知的三元奖励策略，能够识别隐性错误并区分可修正错误与不可恢复错误。通过多样化轨迹生成和知识增强的步骤标注，构建了超过8000个高质量训练实例。实验表明，DataPRM在多个基准测试中显著提升下游策略LLM性能，且参数规模较小仍表现优异，具备良好的泛化能力。结合强化学习后，其表现优于基于结果奖励的基线方法。
