item.title: Optimally Auditing Adversarial Agents
item.content:encoded: Fraud can pose a challenge in many resource allocation domains, including social service delivery and credit provision. For example, agents may misreport private information in order to gain benefits or access to credit. To mitigate this, a principal can design strategic audits to verify claims and penalize misreporting. In this paper, we introduce a general model of audit policy design as a principal-agent game with multiple agents, where the principal commits to an audit policy, and agents collectively choose an equilibrium that minimizes the principal's utility. We examine both adaptive and non-adaptive settings, depending on whether the principal's policy can be responsive to the distribution of agent reports. Our work provides efficient algorithms for computing optimal audit policies in both settings and extends these results to a setting with limited audit budgets.

item.title: Agentic AI for Remote Sensing: Technical Challenges and Research Directions
item.content:encoded: Earth Observation (EO) is moving beyond static prediction toward multi-step analytical workflows that require coordinated reasoning over data, tools, and geospatial state. While foundation models and vision-language models have expanded representation learning and language-grounded interaction for remote sensing, and agentic AI has demonstrated long-horizon reasoning and external tool use, EO is not a straightforward extension of generic agentic AI. EO workflows operate over georeferenced, multi-modal, and temporally structured data, where operations such as reprojection, resampling, compositing, and aggregation actively transform the underlying state and can constrain subsequent analysis. As a result, errors may propagate silently across steps, and correctness depends not only on internal coherence, but also on geospatial consistency, temporally valid comparisons, and physical validity. This position paper argues that these challenges are structural rather than incidental. We identify the implicit assumptions commonly made in generic agentic models, analyze how they break in geospatial workflows, and characterize the resulting failure modes in multi-step EO pipelines. We then outline design principles for EO-native agents centered on structured geospatial state, tool-aware reasoning, verifier-guided execution, and learning objectives aligned with geospatial and physical validity. Finally, we present research directions spanning EO-specific benchmarks, hybrid supervised and reinforcement learning, constrained self-improvement, and trajectory-level evaluation beyond final-answer accuracy. Building reliable geospatial agents therefore requires rethinking agent design around the physical, geospatial, and workflow constraints that govern EO analysis.

item.title: AI as Consumer and Participant: A Co-Design Agenda for MBSE Substrates and Methodology
item.content:encoded: AI tools are being deployed over MBSE models today, and those models were not designed for this kind of consumption. The problem is not simply that tools hallucinate: well-prompted frontier models produce competent, useful output over a conformant SysML model, but the reasoning they produce is drawn from training rather than retrieved from the model itself, and different tools over the same model produce different results with nothing in the record to adjudicate between them. The model, in other words, is functioning as a prompt rather than as a knowledge base. Attaching better tools to the same model does not resolve this. The model and the methodology that governs its construction need to be designed together for AI participation, treating the model as a machine-queryable knowledge substrate rather than a structured artefact for human navigation, and that co-design has not yet happened in any systematic way. This paper works through a concrete workflow scenario to show what that gap looks like in practice, proposes three principles that jointly characterise what model and methodology must achieve together, and closes with a call to the community to begin this work before the architectural decisions about AI integration settle without the methodological foundation they require.

item.title: TopoMamba: Topology-Aware Scanning and Fusion for Segmenting Heterogeneous Medical Visual Media
item.content:encoded: Visual state-space models (SSMs) have shown strong potential for medical image segmentation, yet their effectiveness is often limited by two practical issues: axis-biased scan ordering weakens the modeling of oblique and curved structures, and naive multi-branch fusion tends to amplify redundant responses. We present TopoMamba, a topology-aware scan-and-fuse framework for segmenting heterogeneous medical visual media. The method combines a diagonal/anti-diagonal TopoA-Scan branch with the standard Cross-Scan branch to provide complementary structural priors, and introduces ScanCache, a device-aware caching mechanism that amortizes explicit scan-index construction across recurring resolutions. To fuse heterogeneous scan features efficiently, we further propose a lightweight HSIC Gate that regulates branch interaction using a dependence-aware scalar gating rule. We also instantiate a volumetric TopoMamba-3D for practical 3D clinical segmentation. Experiments on Synapse CT, ISIC 2017 dermoscopy, and CVC-ClinicDB endoscopy show that TopoMamba consistently improves segmentation quality over strong CNN, Transformer, and SSM baselines, with particularly clear gains on thin or curved targets such as the pancreas and gallbladder, while maintaining favorable deployment efficiency under dynamic input resolutions. These results suggest that topology-aware scan ordering and lightweight dependence-aware fusion form an effective and practical design for medical multimedia segmentation. The code will be made publicly available.

item.title: What Kind of Language is Easy to Language-Model Under Curriculum Learning?
item.content:encoded: Many of the thousands of attested languages share common configurations of features, creating a spectrum from typologically very rare (e.g., object-verb-subject word order) or impossible languages to very common combinations of features (e.g., subject-object-verb word order). One central question is under what conditions such typological tendencies can be predicted, and specifically whether the learning bias of language models (LMs) is sufficient to reproduce such patterns. In this study, we add one dimensionality to such analysis -- the learning scenario for LMs -- to explore its interaction with the inductive bias of LMs. Specifically, as a first study, we examine the effect of curriculum learning (CL), as a developmentally motivated learning scenario, i.e., starting with simpler sentences rather than randomly-ordered input. We expand existing LM-based exploration (El-Naggar et al., 2025a,b) with a simple CL variant and find that CL substantially impacts the apparent inductive bias of LMs.

item.title: MultiHedge: Adaptive Coordination via Retrieval-Augmented Control
item.content:encoded: Decision-making under changing conditions remains a fundamental challenge in many real-world systems. Existing approaches often fail to generalize across shifting regimes and exhibit unstable behavior under uncertainty. This raises the research question: can retrieval-augmented LLM coordination improve the robustness of modular decision pipelines? We propose MultiHedge, a hybrid architecture where an LLM produces structured allocation decisions conditioned on retrieved historical precedents, and execution is grounded in canonical option strategies. In a controlled evaluation using U.S. equities, we compare MultiHedge to rule-based and learning-based baselines. The key result is that memory-augmented retrieval confers greater robustness and stability than increasing model scale alone. Our paper contributes a controlled computational study showing that memory and architectural design play a central role in robustness in modular decision systems.

item.title: ViPO: Visual Preference Optimization at Scale
item.content:encoded: While preference optimization is crucial for improving visual generative models, how to effectively scale this paradigm remains largely unexplored. Current open-source preference datasets contain conflicting preference patterns, where winners excel in some dimensions but underperform in others. Naively optimizing on such noisy datasets fails to learn preferences, hindering effective scaling. To enhance robustness against noise, we propose Poly-DPO, which extends the DPO objective with an additional polynomial term that dynamically adjusts model confidence based on dataset characteristics, enabling effective learning across diverse data distributions. Beyond biased patterns, existing datasets suffer from low resolution, limited prompt diversity, and imbalanced distributions. To facilitate large-scale visual preference optimization by tackling data bottlenecks, we construct ViPO, a massive-scale preference dataset with 1M image pairs at 1024px across five categories and 300K video pairs at 720p+ across three categories. State-of-the-art generative models and diverse prompts ensure reliable preference signals with balanced distributions. Remarkably, when applying Poly-DPO to our high-quality dataset, the optimal configuration converges to standard DPO. This convergence validates dataset quality and Poly-DPO's adaptive nature: sophisticated optimization becomes unnecessary with sufficient data quality, yet remains valuable for imperfect datasets. We validate our approach across visual generation models. On noisy datasets like Pick-a-Pic V2, Poly-DPO achieves 6.87 and 2.32 gains over Diffusion-DPO on GenEval for SD1.5 and SDXL, respectively. For ViPO, models achieve performance far exceeding those trained on existing open-source preference datasets. These results confirm that addressing both algorithmic adaptability and data quality is essential for scaling visual preference optimization.

item.title: Spectral Dynamic Attention Network for Hyperspectral Image Super-Resolution
item.content:encoded: Hyperspectral image super-resolution is essential for enhancing the spatial fidelity of HSI data, yet existing deep learning methods often struggle with substantial spectral redundancy and the limited non-linear modeling capacity of standard feed-forward networks (FFNs). To address these challenges, we propose Spectral Dynamic Attention Network (SDANet), a framework designed to adaptively suppress redundant spectral interactions. SDANet integrates two key components: 1) Dynamic Channel Sparse Attention (DCSA) module that computes channel-wise correlations and selectively preserves the most informative attention responses through dynamic and data-dependent sparsification. 2) Frequency-Enhanced Feed-Forward Network (FE-FFN) that jointly models spatial and frequency-domain representations to enhance non-linear expressiveness. Extensive experiments on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that SDANet achieves state-of-the-art HISR performance while maintaining competitive efficiency. The code will be made publicly available at https://github.com/oucailab/SDANet.

item.title: PEPS: Positional Encoding Projected Sampling -- Extended
item.content:encoded: Implicit neural representations (INRs) are increasingly being used as tools to map coordinates to signals, encompassing applications from neural fields to texture compression, shape representations, and beyond. Most INR methods are based on using high-dimensional projections of the initial coordinates through encoders such as grid or positional encoding. Nevertheless, positional encoding is often insufficient and grids, as we show in this paper, require high resolution for being able to learn. In this paper, we demonstrate that positional encoding can be used not only as a high-dimensional embedding but also decomposed as a series of meaningful points. We propose the Positional Encoding Projected Sampling, where we treat the projection of the original coordinate at each frequency as a point of interest. We describe the motion of each point with respect to the frequencies and show that it follows a unique pattern. Finally, we use the unique motion of each point as a basis decomposition for doing learned positional encoding using grids. We prove, using three competitive applications; image representation, texture compression, and signed distance function; that the proposed approach outperforms the current state of the art methods, and often requires 25\% less parameters for equivalent reconstruction error or rendering.

item.title: AI时代下嵌入式安全关键系统的可靠性设计与挑战
item.content:encoded:
AI使能的嵌入式系统面临可靠性与安全性新挑战。



摘要： 本文探讨了在人工智能和机器学习技术广泛应用背景下，下一代汽车和自动驾驶平台等嵌入式安全关键系统的可靠性设计问题。随着系统复杂性和软硬件异构性的增加，传统方法难以应对AI和ML组件带来的动态不确定行为。尽管AI和ML提供了强大的预测与自优化能力，但其非确定性、数据依赖性和缺乏形式化保证给验证、测试和认证带来了新的难题。文章介绍了最新的方法、架构和框架，旨在提升AI驱动系统的可靠性，并推动其在实际应用中的可认证性。

item.title: 基于树状离散化与整数线性规划的因果推断方法
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种新型因果推断算法，提升估计精度与效率。



摘要： 因果推断在数据驱动决策中至关重要，但因混杂因素和相关性与因果性的区别而面临挑战。本文提出一种结合树状离散化技术和整数线性规划匹配算法的新方法，通过确保控制组内近似线性关系实现有效匹配，并优化全局平衡。该方法在计算效率和ATT估计偏差方面优于现有技术，实验证明其在因果推断场景中的优越性。

item.title: LLM与人类协作的业务流程建模研究
item.content:encoded:
LLM与人类协作实现可解释的流程建模。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型（LLMs）在业务流程管理中的应用，强调流程建模的复杂性不能仅依赖黑盒解决方案。作者提出一种交互式、迭代式的建模方法，将任务分解为多个步骤，并通过透明的决策过程逐步构建模型。为了弥补LLM在处理复杂依赖关系上的不足，系统结合了专门工具以结构化方式生成流程模型。该研究介绍了Pragmos原型系统，展示了LLM如何与人类专家合作，共同创建可解释且不断演进的流程模型。

item.title: 深度学习中学习率调度的演化与DALS优化框架
item.content:encoded:
文章系统分析学习率调度的发展，并提出DALS优化框架。



摘要： 文章回顾了学习率调度从早期固定全局学习率到分层自适应策略的演变过程，将其归纳为五个发展阶段。作者揭示了各阶段演进的根本动因，指出从统一策略向按层和时间定制的转变如何解决迁移学习中的矛盾。基于此分类，提出了统一的DALS框架，结合相位自适应余弦调度、深度感知梯度过滤和LARS信任比例。在多个数据集上的实验表明，DALS在合成数据上达到98.0%的准确率，且在微调任务中表现优异，证明其有效性。

item.title: 基于VLA的批评者框架CriticVLA在自动驾驶中的应用
item.content:encoded:
CriticVLA通过多模态评估提升自动驾驶性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为CriticVLA的两阶段框架，旨在通过利用视觉-语言-动作（VLA）模型的批评能力来优化自动驾驶决策。该方法首先生成粗略轨迹，再通过VLA驱动的多模态评估和单步优化进行精调，从而提高驾驶行为的质量。研究构建了一个包含1290万条标注轨迹的大规模合成数据集，以增强模型的推理与优化能力。实验结果表明，CriticVLA在Bench2Drive基准测试中表现优异，总成功率高达73.33%，并在复杂场景中提升了约30%。

item.title: 句法结构中依赖距离最小化的优化分析
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨了句法依赖距离最小化的优化难度与星型结构中的反向现象。



摘要： 本文研究了句法结构中依赖距离最小化的问题，特别是针对星型结构和路径结构的优化难度。研究发现，星型结构的优化景观是凸的，表明其优化问题比之前认为的更简单。此外，作者指出，星型结构中出现的反依赖距离最小化现象并非由于优化难度，而是由其他竞争原则所导致，同时依赖距离最小化在星型结构中的收益也低于其他结构。

item.title: 物理基础模型：下一代高效AI硬件的革命
item.content:encoded:
物理基础模型有望提升AI能效与性能。



摘要： 文章探讨了基础模型在人工智能领域的广泛应用及其对硬件设计的影响。传统数字电子硬件已难以满足大规模基础模型的需求，因此提出物理基础模型（PFMs）的概念。PFMs通过直接在物理层面实现神经网络，利用硬件自身的物理动态运行，可能带来能效、速度和参数密度的显著提升。文章以光学纳米结构玻璃为例，展示了PFMs的潜在规模和优势，并讨论了其在纳米电子学等领域的前景。最后指出，实现万亿参数以上的PFMs仍需解决多项关键研究挑战。

item.title: 基于语言模型的ASR系统重排序评估方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出新指标评估ASR系统重排序效果。



摘要： 本文探讨了自动语音识别（ASR）系统的评估问题，指出传统词错误率（WER）存在局限性。为更深入分析自动转录错误，文章引入了两种新指标：POSER（词性错误率）和EmbER（嵌入错误率）。POSER关注语法层面的错误，EmbER则根据语义距离对错误进行加权。这些指标有助于理解语言模型在后处理阶段对转录结果的影响，为ASR系统优化提供新思路。

item.title: 基于机器学习的Vicsek群体模型相图构建
item.content:encoded:
机器学习用于分类和插值Vicsek群体模型的相结构。



摘要： 本研究利用机器学习对Vicsek群体模型在三维参数空间(η, ρ, v₀)中的相结构进行分类和插值。通过构建模拟参数点的数据集，并使用长时间动力学可观测量作为输入，采用K-均值聚类方法将每个点归类为无序、有序或共存相。随后训练神经网络分类器，实现从模型参数到相行为的映射，准确率达0.92。该方法揭示了有序与无序相之间的狭窄共存区域，并扩展了推断的相边界，为集体运动模型提供了系统化的全局相图构建方法。

item.title: 多轮交互中大语言模型对约束的遵循性研究
item.content:encoded:
研究发现多轮交互中模型约束遵循性下降，存在知而犯之现象。



摘要： 本文引入DriftBench基准，评估多轮大语言模型辅助科学创意生成中的约束遵循性。实验涵盖2146次运行，涉及7个模型、4种交互条件和38个跨24个领域的研究简报。结果表明，迭代压力会增加结构复杂性并降低原始约束的遵循度。模型虽能准确重述约束，但常同时违反，称为“知而犯之”（KBV）现象，其发生率在8%至99%之间。结构检查点部分减少KBV，但无法消除矛盾，复杂性仍上升。人类验证显示模型评分保守，约束违规检测不足。敏感性分析确认结果在温度和压力类型上稳健。所有数据已公开。

item.title: Strait：提升双优先级推理任务的延迟满足度
item.content:encoded:
Strait系统提升高优先级推理任务的延迟满足度。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为Strait的推理服务系统，旨在提高在高GPU利用率下双优先级推理任务的截止时间满足率。Strait通过建模数据传输中的潜在竞争，并利用自适应预测模型考虑内核执行干扰，从而改进延迟估计。基于这些预测，系统进行优先级感知调度，实现差异化处理。评估结果显示，在高负载环境下，Strait可减少高优先级任务的截止时间违规率1.02至11.18个百分点，同时对低优先级任务的影响可接受。与软件定义的抢占方法相比，Strait表现出更公平的性能。

item.title: 基于TFM-S3的机器人策略学习方法研究
item.content:encoded:
TFM-S3提升机器人策略学习效率与性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种名为TFM-S3的混合局部-全局策略优化方法，用于解决高维连续控制中的机器人策略学习问题。该方法通过在高频局部更新与间歇性全局搜索之间交替进行，实现高效探索。在每次全局搜索中，利用SVD构建低维策略子空间，并在此空间内进行迭代优化。同时，使用预训练的表格基础模型从少量上下文数据中预测候选回报，从而在有限的采样成本下进行大规模筛选。实验表明，TFM-S3在连续控制基准任务中相比TD3和基于种群的基线方法具有更快的早期收敛速度和更高的最终性能，证明了基础模型在样本高效策略学习中的潜力。

item.title: 基于特征级对比学习的遥感表格数据表示学习方法ZAYAN
item.content:encoded:
ZAYAN通过特征级对比学习提升遥感表格数据的表示效果。



摘要： 本文提出ZAYAN，一种自监督的特征中心对比学习框架，用于从遥感和环境科学中的表格数据中学习信息表示。ZAYAN在特征层面进行对比学习，无需显式锚点或类别标签，减少冗余并获得解耦的嵌入空间。该框架包含ZAYAN-CL和ZAYAN-T两个模块，分别用于预训练特征嵌入和下游分类任务。实验表明，ZAYAN在多个遥感表格基准数据集上优于现有深度学习基线，尤其在标签稀缺和分布偏移情况下表现更优，验证了特征级对比学习与动态特征编码的有效性。

item.title: 基于采样器鲁棒优化的不确定性建模方法
item.content:encoded:
SRO优化决策以应对生成模型的采样器扰动，提升稳定性与性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于采样器鲁棒优化（Sampler-Robust Optimization, SRO）的方法，用于处理依赖于学习生成模型的不确定性建模问题。传统方法依赖蒙特卡洛情景评估下游决策，而SRO则通过对抗生成器扰动来优化决策，确保在不同采样器下的稳定性。该方法在覆盖性假设下提供了对真实目标函数的高概率上界，并有效吸收了有限模拟误差。实验表明，SRO在分布偏移情况下能提升投资组合优化的稳定性与外样本性能。

item.title: 消费者对医疗AI应用的态度与接受度研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨消费者对医疗AI的接受度与信任情况。



摘要： 本文通过混合方法调查研究了澳大利亚消费者对医疗AI的准备度、接受度、信任及风险感知，结合AI生成与医生撰写的咨询摘要场景评估。参与者表现出中等乐观和高度有用性与易用性的认知，但对准确性、安全性和数据使用有较大担忧。在场景任务中，AI生成的摘要在质量、同理心和整体实用性上更受青睐，但识别率接近随机。研究显示，消费者通过具体的沟通质量和可见的人类监管来评判AI，强调了技术性能之外的临床监督部署框架的重要性。

item.title: 基于概率电路的不规则多变量时间序列预测方法
item.content:encoded:
提出CircuITS模型提升不规则多变量时间序列预测准确性。



摘要： 本文提出了一种名为CircuITS的概率模型，用于不规则多变量时间序列（IMTS）的预测。该模型基于概率电路架构，能够灵活捕捉不同时间序列通道之间的复杂依赖关系，同时确保联合分布的有效性。实验在四个真实数据集上进行，结果表明CircuITS在联合和边缘密度估计方面优于现有最先进的基线模型。

item.title: 基于级联扩散框架的3D室内场景生成方法
item.content:encoded:
CasLayout提升3D室内场景生成的准确性和可控性。



摘要： 本文提出CasLayout，一种基于级联扩散的3D室内场景生成框架。该框架将场景生成任务分解为四个条件子阶段，包括预测家具数量和类别、优化对象尺寸和特征嵌入、建模空间关系以及生成定向边界框。通过显式建模建筑元素作为约束，并引入稀疏关系图以降低高熵问题，提升了生成场景的物理合理性和语义一致性。实验表明，CasLayout在保真度和多样性上均达到先进水平，且增强了实际应用中的可控性。

item.title: LLM+ASP框架提升逻辑推理性能与自修正能力
item.content:encoded:
LLM+ASP通过非单调逻辑提升推理能力，无需人工干预。



摘要： 本文提出LLM+ASP框架，将自然语言转化为答案集编程（ASP），以非单调逻辑提升大语言模型的推理能力。该框架无需任务特定工程，可统一应用于多种推理任务，并通过自动化自我修正循环提高性能。实验表明，稳定模型语义使LLM能更好地表达默认规则和例外情况，在非单调任务中显著优于基于SMT的方法；迭代自修正是性能提升的关键，可替代人工领域知识；简短的上下文参考指南比冗长文档更有效，揭示了‘上下文衰减’现象，即过多上下文会阻碍约束遵守。

item.title: 指数族中的KL散度恒等式及其应用
item.content:encoded:
文章揭示指数族中KL散度的恒等式及其广泛理论应用。



摘要： 本文探讨了指数族中KL散度的简化恒等式，该恒等式通过对数归一化函数和矩量表达KL差异。利用这一恒等式和KL非负性，可直接推导出一系列经典结果，包括三阶恒等式、毕达哥拉斯定理、对数归一化函数的凸性、熵正则化强化学习中的优化器等。此外，标准分析方法进一步推导出对数归一化函数的梯度公式、Bregman表示以及矩映射的满射性。文章内容自洽且理论完备。

item.title: 语言模型中意外性理论的单位分离与统一框架
item.content:encoded:
本文提出分离语言单位定义与预测区域的选择，建立统一的意外性分析框架。



摘要： 本文探讨了语言模型中意外性理论（surprisal theory）在实际应用中的问题，指出实验设计常将语言单位（如单词）作为分析基础，而预训练语言模型则基于固定词元（token）进行概率计算，导致两者不匹配。这种不一致使得基于意外性的预测依赖于人为设定的步骤，混淆了两个不同的建模选择。文章提出一种统一框架，使研究者能够明确区分单位定义和预测区域，并将分词视为实现细节而非科学基础，从而提升分析的清晰度和可解释性。

item.title: 基于AMGenC的非晶材料生成逆向设计方法
item.content:encoded:
提出AMGenC方法实现电荷平衡的非晶材料生成。



摘要： 本文介绍了一种名为AMGenC的新型生成逆向设计方法，用于生成具有电荷平衡特性的非晶材料。与传统方法相比，该方法在保持逆向设计准确性的同时，通过引入元素噪声和分步投影机制，有效解决了概率生成模型中因元素分配无约束而导致的电荷不平衡问题。实验表明，AMGenC能够在最小额外计算开销下，生成符合预期性质的非晶材料，为相关领域研究提供了新思路。

item.title: 基于多层防御机制的鲁棒联邦学习方法AdaBFL
item.content:encoded:
提出AdaBFL方法提升联邦学习抗攻击能力。



摘要： 本文针对联邦学习在去中心化环境下易受 poisoning 攻击的问题，提出一种基于三层防御机制的自适应聚合方法 AdaBFL。该方法通过动态调整防御算法权重，有效应对多种攻击类型，无需依赖服务器端数据。在非凸和非独立同分布数据条件下，分析了 AdaBFL 的收敛性，并通过多组实验验证其优于现有方法。

item.title: 基于3D语义占用预测的World2Minecraft环境构建与数据集研究
item.content:encoded:
提出World2Minecraft实现真实场景到Minecraft的转换，提升AI感知与决策能力。



摘要： 本文提出World2Minecraft框架，通过3D语义占用预测将真实场景转换为结构化的Minecraft环境，以支持更高效的感知与决策任务。研究指出，重建质量依赖于精确的占用预测，但现有模型受数据稀缺和泛化能力限制。为此，作者设计了一种低成本、自动化且可扩展的数据采集流程，并构建了MinecraftOcc数据集，包含156个室内场景的100,165张图像。实验表明该数据集对现有方法构成挑战，有助于提升占用预测性能，并为个性化具身AI研究提供可定制平台。

item.title: 非交叉口行人事故模式及影响因素研究
item.content:encoded:
研究显示50%非交叉口行人事故发生在距交叉口198英尺内。



摘要： 本研究基于路易斯安那州2017至2021年的交通事故数据，分析非交叉口行人事故的分布特征。研究发现，约50%的非交叉口行人事故发生在距离交叉口198英尺范围内，并将事故区域划分为三个距离区间：D1（0-150英尺）、D2（151-435英尺）和D3（435英尺以上）。通过关联规则挖掘技术，识别出60条关键关联规则，进一步揭示了不同距离区间的事故模式差异。研究结果为制定针对性的交通安全措施提供了重要依据。

item.title: MED-VRAG：基于医学文档图像的多模态检索增强生成系统
item.content:encoded:
MED-VRAG通过图像检索提升医学问答准确率，达78.6%。



摘要： 本文提出MED-VRAG，一种基于医学文献页面图像的多模态检索增强生成框架。与传统基于文本片段的方法不同，该系统直接处理原始文档的视觉内容，包括表格和图表。通过结合ColQwen2.5的图像嵌入与MapReduce过滤器，MED-VRAG实现了高效的检索，并在四个医学问答基准测试中达到78.6%的平均准确率。实验表明，相比无检索基线，检索模块提升了5.8个百分点；相较于MedRAG + GPT-4也略有优势。消融实验显示，图像检索、迭代机制和记忆库分别贡献了1.0、1.5和1.0的性能提升。

item.title: 大型语言模型在语言意识形态检测中的应用研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨了大语言模型在检测语言意识形态中的应用潜力。



摘要： 本文探讨了大型语言模型在 Luxembourg 多元文化社会中语言意识形态检测的应用。通过人工标注 Luxembourgish 用户评论并评估不同提示条件下的模型表现，研究发现尽管 LLM 在多类别意识形态标注任务上尚未完全优化，但已具备识别语言意识形态内容的实用性。由于 Luxembourgish 语言资源较少，研究还测试了机器翻译到高资源语言是否能提升检测效果。

item.title: 提升AI生成图像检测的FGINet方法研究
item.content:encoded:
FGINet提升AI图像检测通用性，解决频率偏差与跨域冲突问题。



摘要： 本文针对AI生成图像检测中普遍存在的泛化能力不足问题，提出一种基于频率感知的门控注入网络（FGINet）。该方法通过引入频域交叉带掩码编码器（BMFE）减少对特定生成器模式的依赖，增强表示多样性；同时设计逐层门控频率注入机制（LGFI），逐步融合频率特征并缓解高阶语义与低阶频率模式之间的冲突。此外，采用超球面紧凑学习框架（HCL）优化特征分布。实验表明，FGINet在多个数据集上均取得最佳性能，展现出强大的泛化能力。

item.title: 基于混合专家的半监督推断框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于MOE的半监督推断方法，提升预测性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于混合专家（MOE）的半监督推断框架，用于在标签数据稀缺的情况下提高预测准确性。该框架通过整合多个预测模型的优势，减少方差并提升整体表现。与传统方法相比，该方法能适应不同预测器的性能差异，并保证最优专家的预测效果。该框架适用于多种统计推断任务，如均值估计、线性回归等。作者建立了非渐近理论分析，并通过实验验证了其有效性。

item.title: 人机协作决策中的领导力谱系分析
item.content:encoded:
文章探讨了人机协作决策中领导力的五种配置及其影响。



摘要： 本文提出了一种人机协作决策的领导力谱系，包括纯人类、半人半AI（人类主导）、平等、AI主导和纯AI五种模式。该框架帮助领导者识别决策过程中权力、责任和信任的分布，并强调了配置变化可能带来的风险，如误判领导角色或无效的人类参与。文章还引入了‘协同适应性’概念，强调人与AI在复杂团队中需共同调整以提升决策质量。最终目标是为战略领导者提供工具，以识别并适应不同决策配置，确保其可控且有意义。

item.title: 基于扰动探测的LLM神经元因果假设与行为电路分析
item.content:encoded:
扰动探测揭示了LLM中FFN神经元的行为电路结构及其干预效果。



摘要： 本文通过扰动探测方法，利用两次前向传播和无反向传播的方式，生成任务特定的因果假设，识别出LLM中的两种行为电路结构：对抗电路和路由电路。对抗电路在RLHF抑制预训练倾向时出现，如安全拒绝场景中，约50个神经元控制拒绝模板，其消融可显著改变响应格式并减少有害合规。路由电路则用于预训练行为，通过残差流方向注入可实现语言切换。研究还发现FFN-to-skip信号比是区分电路结构的关键指标，并揭示了不同架构下电路拓扑的差异。实验表明，扰动探测为理解RLHF组织的行为提供了机制洞察，并为精准编辑模板层提供了工具。



使用 qwen-turbo 生成

查看原文

item.title: Revealing the Impact of Visual Text Style on Attribute-based Descriptions Produced by Large Visual Language Models
item.content:encoded: When the visual style of text is considered, a wide variety can be observed in font, color, and size. However, when a word is read, its meaning is independent of the style in which it has been written or rendered. In this paper, we investigate whether, and how, the style in which a word is visualized in an image impacts the description that a Large Visual Language Model (LVLM) provides for the concept to which that word refers. Specifically, we investigate how functional text styles (readability-oriented, e.g., black sans-serif) versus decorative styles (display-oriented, e.g., colored cursive/script) affect LVLMs' descriptions of a concept in terms of the attributes of that concept. Our experiments study the situation in which the LVLM is able to correctly identify the concept referred to by a visual text, i.e., by a word or words rendered as an image, and in which the visual text style should not influence the attribute-based description that the LVLM produces. Our experimental results reveal that even when the concept is correctly identified, text style influences the model's attribute-based descriptions of the concept. Our findings demonstrate non-trivial style leakage from text style into semantic inference and motivate style-aware evaluation and mitigation for LVLM-based multimedia systems.

item.title: Hyperspectral Image Classification via Efficient Global Spectral Supertoken Clustering
item.content:encoded: Hyperspectral image classification demands spatially coherent predictions and precise boundary delineation. Yet prevailing superpixel-based methods face an inherent contradiction: clustering aggregates similar pixels into regions, but the subsequent classifier operates pixel-wise, undermining regional consistency. Consequently, existing approaches do not guarantee region-level, boundary-aligned classification. To address this limitation, we propose the Dual-stage Spectrum-Constrained Clustering-based Classifier (DSCC), an end-to-end framework that explicitly decouples clustering from classification by first grouping spectral similar and spatially proximate pixels into spectral supertokens and then performing token-level prediction. At its core, DSCC computes an image-level multi-criteria feature distance between pixels and centers, followed by a locality-aware assignment regularization, enabling the generation of boundary-preserving spectral supertokens. A density-isolation based center selection further yields representative, well-separated centers, reducing redundancy and improving robustness to scale variation. To accommodate mixed land-cover compositions within each token, we introduce a soft-label scheme that encodes class proportions and improves robustness for mixed-class tokens. DSCC attains a CF1 of 0.728 at 197.75 FPS on the WHU-OHS dataset, offering a superior accuracy-efficiency trade-off compared with state-of-the-art methods. Extensive experiments further validate the effectiveness and generality of the proposed dual-stage paradigm for hyperspectral image classification. The source code is available at https://github.com/laprf/DSCC.

item.title: VibroML: an automated toolkit for high-throughput vibrational analysis and dynamic instability remediation of crystalline materials using machine-learned potentials
item.content:encoded: While machine-learned interatomic potentials (MLIPs) accelerate phonon dispersion calculations, merely identifying dynamical instabilities in computationally predicted materials is insufficient; automated pathways to resolve them are required. We introduce VibroML, an open-source Python toolkit driven by foundational MLIPs that shifts the paradigm from stability verification to automated structural remediation. VibroML employs an energy-guided genetic algorithm that vastly outperforms traditional soft-mode following, efficiently navigating the potential energy surface to uncover diverse, dynamically stable polymorphs. As 0 K harmonic stability does not guarantee macroscopic viability, an automated molecular dynamics workflow evaluates finite-temperature structural retention. VibroML also couples with ProtoCSP, our combinatorial structure prediction engine, to stabilize frustrated crystal topologies via targeted alloying, successfully rescuing functional perovskite networks like Cs$_2$KInI$_6$ and KTaSe$_3$. Demonstrating broader applicability, we mined the Alexandria database -- where ~50% of quaternary and 99.5% of quinary elemental combinations lack any structural entries -- to identify thousands of abandoned, high-symmetry stoichiometries. Deploying ProtoCSP's "cold start" retrieval and VibroML's evolutionary search on a sample, we successfully identified dynamically stable low-symmetry candidates. Through integrated structural remediation, thermal validation, and systematic compositional exploration, VibroML enables a comprehensive deep-screening approach, yielding physically sound structural propositions that far surpass standard high-throughput workflows.

item.title: Bridging Values and Behavior: A Hierarchical Framework for Proactive Embodied Agents
item.content:encoded: Current embodied agents are often limited to passive instruction-following or reactive need-satisfaction, lacking a stable, high-order value framework essential for long-term, self-directed behavior and resolving motivational conflicts. We introduce \textit{ValuePlanner}, a hierarchical cognitive architecture that decouples high-level value scheduling from low-level action execution. \textit{ValuePlanner} employs an LLM-based cognitive module to generate symbolic subgoals by reasoning through abstract value trade-offs, which are then translated into executable action plans by a classical PDDL planner. This process is refined via a closed-loop feedback mechanism. Evaluating such autonomy requires methods beyond task-success rates, and we therefore propose a value-centric evaluation suite measuring cumulative value gain, preference alignment, and behavioral diversity. Experiments in the TongSim household environment demonstrate that \textit{ValuePlanner} arbitrates competing values to generate coherent, long-horizon, self-directed behavior absent from instruction-following and needs-driven baselines. Our work offers a structured approach to bridging intrinsic values and grounded behavior for autonomous agents.

item.title: Do Sparse Autoencoders Capture Concept Manifolds?
item.content:encoded: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) are widely used to extract interpretable features from neural network representations, often under the implicit assumption that concepts correspond to independent linear directions. However, a growing body of evidence suggests that many concepts are instead organized along low-dimensional manifolds encoding continuous geometric relationships. This raises three basic questions: what does it mean for an SAE to capture a manifold, when do existing SAE architectures do so, and how? We develop a theoretical framework that answers these questions and show that SAEs can capture manifolds in two fundamentally different ways: globally, by allocating a compact group of atoms whose linear span contains the entire manifold, or locally, by distributing it across features that each selectively tile a restricted region of the underlying geometry. Empirically, we find that SAEs suboptimally recover continuous structures, mixing the global subspace and local tiling solutions in a fragmented regime we call dilution. This explains why manifold structure is rarely visible at the level of individual concepts and motivates post-hoc unsupervised discovery methods that search for coherent groups of atoms rather than isolated directions. More broadly, our results suggest that future representation learning methods should treat geometric objects, not just individual directions, as the basic units of interpretability.

item.title: Trace-Level Analysis of Information Contamination in Multi-Agent Systems
item.content:encoded: Reasoning over heterogeneous artifacts (PDFs, spreadsheets, slide decks, etc.) increasingly occurs within structured agent workflows that iteratively extract, transform, and reference external information. In these workflows, uncertainty is not merely an input-quality issue: it can redirect decomposition and routing decisions, reshape intermediate state, and produce qualitatively different execution trajectories. We study this phenomenon by treating uncertainty as a controlled variable: we inject structured perturbations into artifact-derived representations, execute fixed workflows under comprehensive logging, and quantify contamination via trace divergence in plans, tool invocations, and intermediate state. Across 614 paired runs on 32 GAIA tasks with three different language models, we find a decoupling: workflows may diverge substantially yet recover correct answers, or remain structurally similar while producing incorrect outputs. We characterize three manifestation types: silent semantic corruption, behavioral detours with recovery, and combined structural disruption and their control-flow signatures (rerouting, extended execution, early termination). We measure operational costs and characterize why commonly used verification guardrails fail to intercept contamination. We contribute (i) a formal taxonomy of contamination manifestations in structured workflows, (ii) a trace-based measurement framework for detecting and localizing contamination across agent interactions, and (iii) empirical evidence with implications for targeted verification, defensive design, and cost control.

item.title: Context as Prior: Bayesian-Inspired Intent Inference for Non-Speaking Agents with a Household Cat Testbed
item.content:encoded: Many agents in real-world environments cannot reliably communicate their goals through language, including household pets, pre-verbal infants, and other non-speaking embodied agents. In such settings, intent must be inferred from incomplete behavioral observations in context-rich environments. This creates a core ambiguity: observable behavior is often noisy or underspecified, while context provides strong prior information but can also induce brittle shortcut predictions if used naively. We present CatSignal, a Bayesian-inspired probabilistic framework for multimodal intent inference that models spatial context as a prior-like constraint and behavioral observations as evidence. Rather than treating context as an ordinary input feature, our method uses a context-gated Product-of-Experts formulation to compute posterior-like intent distributions from context, pose dynamics, and acoustic cues. We instantiate this formulation in a household cat setting as a focused proof-of-concept for intent inference in non-speaking agents. Under Leave-One-Video-Out evaluation on a multimodal domestic cat dataset, the proposed prior-guided fusion achieves the best overall accuracy of 77.72%, outperforming feature concatenation (71.83%) and stronger late-fusion baselines. More importantly, it substantially reduces context-driven shortcut failures in ambiguous cases. While simpler fusion strategies remain competitive in Macro-F1 and selective prediction, the proposed model provides the strongest overall accuracy and the best suppression of context-based shortcut collapse.

item.title: Predicting Upcoming Stuttering Events from Three-Second Audio: Stratified Evaluation Reveals Severity-Selective Precursors, and the Model Deploys Fully On-Device
item.content:encoded: Audio-based stuttering systems to date have been trained for detection -- what disfluency is present now -- leaving prediction, the capability needed for closed-loop intervention, unstudied at deployable scale. We train a 616K-parameter CNN on SEP-28k (Apple, 20,131 three-second clips) to predict whether the next contiguous clip contains any disfluency. (1) Severity-selective precursor signal: on the episode-grouped test set, aggregate preblock AUC is modest (0.581 [0.542, 0.619]), but stratifying by upcoming event type reveals concentration on clinically severe events -- blocks 0.601 [0.554, 0.651] and sound repetitions 0.617 [0.567, 0.667] both exclude chance, while fillers (0.45) and word repetitions (0.49) are at chance. The aggregate objective converges to a severity-selective predictor because severe events carry prosodic precursors; fillers do not. (2) Cross-population transfer: without fine-tuning, the same checkpoint applied to 1,024 pediatric Children-Who-Stutter utterances (FluencyBank Teaching) attains AUC 0.674 detection and 0.655 prediction; DisfluencySpeech and LibriStutter reach 0.58-0.60 AUC. (3) Deployable on-device: lossless export to CoreML (1.19 MB), ONNX (40 KB), TFLite. Neural-Engine latency per 3 s window: 0.25 ms (iPhone 17 Pro Max, A19 Pro) to 0.55 ms (iPhone SE 3rd-gen and M1 Max). A 4 Hz streaming simulation uses 0.54% of the real-time budget. Platt-calibrated outputs (test ECE 0.010, from 0.177 raw). Five negative ablations -- output-level Future-Guided Learning, multi-clip GRU, time-axis concatenation, asymmetric focal loss, direct block-targeted training -- none improved over the vanilla baseline.

item.title: RuC: HDL-Agnostic Rule Completion Benchmark Generation
item.content:encoded: Large Language Models (LLMs) have rapidly improved in performance across code-related tasks, making their integration into Register Transfer Level (RTL) development increasingly attractive. Mimicking the behavior of inline code assistants, many benchmarks evaluate LLMs' capabilities in code completion, either assessing the generation of entire hardware modules or the completion of a single line within a module. However both of these approaches lack the ability to control the granularity of the code-completion sample size and the syntactic range of completions. To overcome these limitations, we present a framework for language-agnostic rule completion (RuC), a grammar-driven, rule-selectable benchmark generator that automatically produces RTL code-completion tasks from a set of input hardware description sources. RuC uses the target Hardware Description Language (HDL) grammar to mask syntactically defined code regions and prompts a model to regenerate them using the surrounding unmasked code as context, enabling a controlled and scalable evaluation of the domain-specific model's code-understanding capabilities, ranging from assignments to the reconstruction of entire logic blocks. We use RuC to generate two SystemVerilog rule-completion benchmarks from the Tiny Tapeout shuttle TT07 and the CVE2 RISC-V core to demonstrate RuC's applicability to a broad range of designs, and conduct a comparative study of the code completion capabilities of modern open-source LLMs across diverse settings. Results indicate that completion performance strongly depends on the model type, the grammatical structure of the masked region, and the prompting strategy. Specifically, the highest scores are obtained with Fill-in-the-Middle (FIM) prompting. These findings highlight the value of grammar-driven, arbitrarily granular benchmarks for meaningful evaluation of LLM capabilities in RTL development workflows.

item.title: Low Rank Adaptation for Adversarial Perturbation
item.content:encoded: Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), which leverages the insight that model updates typically reside in a low-dimensional space, has significantly improved the training efficiency of Large Language Models (LLMs) by updating neural network layers using low-rank matrices. Since the generation of adversarial examples is an optimization process analogous to model training, this naturally raises the question: Do adversarial perturbations exhibit a similar low-rank structure? In this paper, we provide both theoretical analysis and extensive empirical investigation across various attack methods, model architectures, and datasets to show that adversarial perturbations indeed possess an inherently low-rank structure. This insight opens up new opportunities for improving both adversarial attacks and defenses. We mainly focus on leveraging this low-rank property to improve the efficiency and effectiveness of black-box adversarial attacks, which often suffer from excessive query requirements. Our method follows a two-step approach. First, we use a reference model and auxiliary data to guide the projection of gradients into a low-dimensional subspace. Next, we confine the perturbation search in black-box attacks to this low-rank subspace, significantly improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the adversarial attacks. We evaluated our approach across a range of attack methods, benchmark models, datasets, and threat models. The results demonstrate substantial and consistent improvements in the performance of our low-rank adversarial attacks compared to conventional methods.

item.title: HAVEN: Hybrid Automated Verification ENgine for UVM Testbench Synthesis with LLMs
item.content:encoded: Integrated Circuit (IC) verification consumes nearly 70% of the IC development cycle, and recent research leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to automatically generate testbenches and reduce verification overhead. However, LLMs have difficulty generating testbenches correctly. Unlike high-level programming languages, Hardware Description Languages (HDLs) are extremely rare in LLMs training data, leading LLMs to produce incorrect code. To overcome challenges when using LLMs to generate Universal Verification Methodology (UVM) testbenches and sequences, wepropose HAVEN (Hybrid Automated Verification ENgine) to prevent LLMs from writing HDL directly. For UVM testbench generation, HAVEN utilizes LLM agents to analyze design specifications to produce a structured architectural plan. The HAVEN Template Engine then combines with predefined and protocol-specific templates to generate all UVM components with correct bus-handshake timing. For UVM sequence generation, HAVEN introduces a Protocol-Aware Sequence Domain-Specific Language (DSL) that decomposes sequences into fine-grained step types. A set of predefined DSL patterns first establishes sequences that achieve a high coverage rate without LLM involvement. HAVEN continues to improve the coverage rate by iteratively leveraging LLM agents to analyze coverage gap reports and compose additional targeted DSL sequences. Unlike previous works, HAVEN is the first system that utilizes pre-defined, protocol-specific Jinja2 templates to generate all UVM components and UVM sequences using our proposed Protocol-Aware DSL and rule-based code generator. Our experimental results on 19 open-source IP designs spanning three interface protocols (Direct, Wishbone, AXI4-Lite) show that HAVEN achieves 100% compilation success, 90.6% code coverage, and 87.9% functional coverage on average, and is SOTA among LLM-assisted testbench generation systems.

item.title: MCPHunt: An Evaluation Framework for Cross-Boundary Data Propagation in Multi-Server MCP Agents
item.content:encoded: Multi-server MCP agents create an information-flow control problem: faithful tool composition can turn individually benign read/write permissions into cross-boundary credential propagation -- a structural side effect of workflow topology, not necessarily malicious model behavior. We present MCPHunt, to our knowledge the first controlled benchmark that isolates non-adversarial, verbatim credential propagation across multi-server MCP trust boundaries, with three methodological contributions: (1) canary-based taint tracking that reduces propagation detection to objective string matching; (2) an environment-controlled coverage design with risky, benign, and hard-negative conditions that validates pipeline soundness and controls for credential-format confounds; (3) CRS stratification that disentangles task-mandated propagation (faithful execution of verbatim-transfer instructions) from policy-violating propagation (credentials included despite the option to redact). Across 3,615 main-benchmark traces from 5 models spanning 147 tasks and 9 mechanism families, policy-violating propagation rates reach 11.5--41.3% across all models. This propagation is pathway-specific (25x cross-mechanism range) and concentrated in browser-mediated data flows; hard-negative controls provide evidence that production-format credentials are not necessary -- prompt-directed cross-boundary data flow is sufficient. A prompt-mitigation study across 3 models reduces policy-violating propagation by up to 97% while preserving 80.5% utility, but effectiveness varies with instruction-following capability -- suggesting that prompt-level defenses alone may not suffice. Code, traces, and labeling pipeline are released under MIT and CC BY 4.0.

item.title: RAY-TOLD: Ray-Based Latent Dynamics for Dense Dynamic Obstacle Avoidance with TDMPC
item.content:encoded: Dense, dynamic crowds pose a persistent challenge for autonomous mobile robots. Purely reactive planning methods, such as Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) control, often fail to escape local minima in complex scenarios due to their limited prediction horizon. To bridge this gap, we propose Ray-based Task-Oriented Latent Dynamics (RAY-TOLD), a hybrid control architecture that integrates obstacle information into latent dynamics and utilizes the robustness of physics-based MPPI with the long-horizon foresight of reinforcement learning. RAY-TOLD leverages a LiDAR-centric latent dynamics model to encode high-dimensional sensor data into a compact state representation, enabling the learning of a terminal value function and a policy prior. We introduce a policy mixture sampling strategy that augments the MPPI candidate population with trajectories derived from the learned policy, effectively guiding the planner towards the goal while maintaining kinematic feasibility. Extensive tests in a stochastic environment with high-density dynamic obstacles demonstrate that our method outperforms the MPPI baseline, reducing the collision rate. The results confirm that blending short-horizon physics-based rollouts with learned long-horizon intent significantly enhances navigation reliability and safety.

item.title: Universal statistical laws governing culinary design
item.content:encoded: Cooking is a cultural expression of human creativity that transcends geography and time through the orchestration of ingredients and techniques, much like languages do through words and syntax. Yet, beneath the apparent diversity of culinary traditions, whether recipes obey statistical laws comparable to those of other symbolic systems remains unknown. Here we analyze a large corpus of traditional recipes spanning global cuisines, annotated using a state-of-the-art named entity recognition algorithm into ingredients, cooking techniques, utensils, and other culinary attributes. We find that ingredient usage exhibits Zipf-like rank-frequency scaling, that culinary diversity grows sublinearly with corpus size in accordance with Heaps' law, and that recipe complexity follows Menzerath-Altmann-type relations between the number and average information of constituent units. Consistent with observations in packaged foods, macronutrient concentrations across recipes also display a log-normal signature. Minimal generative models based on preferential reuse, constrained sampling, and incremental modification recapitulate these regularities, suggesting generic processes that shape recipe architecture across cultures. Together, these findings establish recipes as a compositional symbolic system in which complex structure emerges from simple, constrained generative processes.

item.title: MSR:Hybrid Field Modeling for CT-MRI Rigid-Deformable Registration of the Cervical Spine with an Annotated Dataset
item.content:encoded: Accurate CT-MRI registration of the cervical spine is essential for preoperative planning because this region is anatomically complex,highly variable,and vulnerable to injury of the vertebral arteries and spinal cord. However,cervical CT-MRI registration remains underexplored,particularly for rigid-deformable hybrid modeling,and the lack of high-quality annotated multimodal data further limits progress. To address these challenges, we construct and release a comprehensively annotated CT-MRI dataset, R-D-Reg, and propose MSR, a rigid-deformable hybrid registration framework for complex joint structures. Specifically, MSR includes a rigid registration module for independent local rigid alignment of individual vertebrae and a deformable registration module with an MSL block that combines Mamba-based global modeling and Swin Transformer-based local modeling through adaptive gating. The rigid and deformable deformation fields are then fused to generate a hybrid field that better preserves local anatomical consistency. The code and dataset are publicly available at https://github.com/ssc1230609-spec/MSR-registration.

item.title: From Unstructured Recall to Schema-Grounded Memory: Reliable AI Memory via Iterative, Schema-Aware Extraction
item.content:encoded: Persistent AI memory is often reduced to a retrieval problem: store prior interactions as text, embed them, and ask the model to recover relevant context later. This design is useful for thematic recall, but it is mismatched to the kinds of memory that agents need in production: exact facts, current state, updates and deletions, aggregation, relations, negative queries, and explicit unknowns. These operations require memory to behave less like search and more like a system of record. This paper argues that reliable external AI memory must be schema-grounded. Schemas define what must be remembered, what may be ignored, and which values must never be inferred. We present an iterative, schema-aware write path that decomposes memory ingestion into object detection, field detection, and field-value extraction, with validation gates, local retries, and stateful prompt control. The result shifts interpretation from the read path to the write path: reads become constrained queries over verified records rather than repeated inference over retrieved prose. We evaluate this design on structured extraction and end-to-end memory benchmarks. On the extraction benchmark, the judge-in-the-loop configuration reaches 90.42% object-level accuracy and 62.67% output accuracy, above all tested frontier structured-output baselines. On our end-to-end memory benchmark, xmemory reaches 97.10% F1, compared with 80.16%-87.24% across the third-party baselines. On the application-level task, xmemory reaches 95.2% accuracy, outperforming specialised memory systems, code-generated Markdown harnesses, and customer-facing frontier-model application harnesses. The results show that, for memory workloads requiring stable facts and stateful computation, architecture matters more than retrieval scale or model strength alone.

item.title: Representative Spectral Correlation Network for Multi-source Remote Sensing Image Classification
item.content:encoded: Hyperspectral image (HSI) and SAR/LiDAR data offer complementary spectral and structural information for land-cover classification. However, their effective fusion remains challenging due to two major limitations: The spectral redundancy in high-dimensional HSI and the heterogeneous characteristics between multi-source data. To this end, we propose Representative Spectral Correlation Network (RSCNet), a novel multi-source image classification framework specifically designed to address the above challenges through spectral selection and adaptive interaction. The network incorporates two key components: (1) Key Band Selection Module (KBSM) that adaptively selects task-relevant spectral bands from the original HSI under cross-source guidance, thereby alleviating redundancy and mitigating information loss from conventional PCA-based spectral reduction. Moreover, the learned band subset exhibits highly discriminative spectral structures that align with discriminative semantic cues, promoting compact yet expressive representations. (2) Cross-source Adaptive Fusion Module (CAFM) that performs cross-source attention weighting and local-global contextual refinement to enhance cross-source feature interaction. Experiments on three public benchmark datasets demonstrate that our RSCNet achieves superior performance compared with state-of-the-art methods, while maintaining substantially lower computational complexity. Our codes are publicly available at https://github.com/oucailab/RSCNet.

item.title: Attractor FCM
item.content:encoded: In this paper an attractor FCM is created, tested, and analyzed. This FCM is neither a hebbian based nor agentic, nor a hybrid; it rather is a gradient descent based, physics constrained, Jacobian version of an FCM. Moreover, this model has several quirks; it uses residual memory, back propagation through time, and a fixed point anchor that is recursively implemented to update its weights. The residuals update the recursive part without losing the system memory. The model's anchor enables it to converge in a fixed point for which back propagation through time unrolls it and ensures that the error minimization is for an accurate gradient. Furthermore, a new learning algorithm is utilized. The Newton's method finds the system's fixed point attractor and then gradient descend is adaptively changing the landscape; an adaptive term is used to directly manipulate the weights through the attractor dynamics. As the adaptive term changes, the descent through the landscape is constantly adjusting according to sigmoid saturation, and that prevents premature convergence to a local minimum. Lastly, the updates are filtered by causal mask that informs the network about the physics, respecting the initial expert based opinions, for which model reduces the error to the target in an efficient way.

item.title: FiLMMeD: Feature-wise Linear Modulation for Cross-Problem Multi-Depot Vehicle Routing
item.content:encoded: Solving practical multi-depot vehicle routing problems (MDVRP) is a challenging optimization task central to modern logistics, increasingly driven by e-commerce. To address the MDVRP's computational complexity, neural-based combinatorial optimization methods offer a promising scalable alternative to traditional approaches. However, neural-based methods typically rely on rigid architectures and input encodings tailored to specific problem formulations. In real-world settings, heterogeneous constraints create multiple MDVRP variants, limiting the applicability of such models. While multi-task learning (MTL) has begun to accelerate the development of unified neural-based solvers, prior works focus almost exclusively on single-depot VRPs, leaving the MDVRP unaddressed. To bridge this gap, we propose Feature-wise Linear Modulation for Cross-Problem Multi-Depot Vehicle Routing (FiLMMeD), a novel unified neural-based model for 24 different MDVRP variants. We introduce three main contributions: (1) to improve the model's generalization, we augment the standard Transformer encoder with Feature-wise Linear Modulation (FiLM), which dynamically conditions learned internal representations based on the active set of constraints; (2) we provide an initial demonstration of Preference Optimization in the MTL setting, establishing it as a superior alternative to Reinforcement Learning for future MTL works; (3) to mitigate the generalization gap caused by the introduction of multi-depot constraints, we introduce a targeted curriculum learning strategy that progressively exposes the model to increasingly more complex constraint interactions. Extensive experiments on 24 MDVRP variants (including 8 novel formulations) and 16 single-depot VRPs confirm the effectiveness of FiLMMeD, which consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Our code is available at: https://github.com/AJ-Correa/FiLMMeD/tree/main

item.title: PhyCo: Learning Controllable Physical Priors for Generative Motion
item.content:encoded: Modern video diffusion models excel at appearance synthesis but still struggle with physical consistency: objects drift, collisions lack realistic rebound, and material responses seldom match their underlying properties. We present PhyCo, a framework that introduces continuous, interpretable, and physically grounded control into video generation. Our approach integrates three key components: (i) a large-scale dataset of over 100K photorealistic simulation videos where friction, restitution, deformation, and force are systematically varied across diverse scenarios; (ii) physics-supervised fine-tuning of a pretrained diffusion model using a ControlNet conditioned on pixel-aligned physical property maps; and (iii) VLM-guided reward optimization, where a fine-tuned vision-language model evaluates generated videos with targeted physics queries and provides differentiable feedback. This combination enables a generative model to produce physically consistent and controllable outputs through variations in physical attributes-without any simulator or geometry reconstruction at inference. On the Physics-IQ benchmark, PhyCo significantly improves physical realism over strong baselines, and human studies confirm clearer and more faithful control over physical attributes. Our results demonstrate a scalable path toward physically consistent, controllable generative video models that generalize beyond synthetic training environments.

item.title: 基于能量场的3D Gaussian Splatting优化方法
item.content:encoded:
EnerGS通过软几何引导提升3DGS重建质量与稳定性。



摘要： 本文提出EnerGS，一种基于能量场的3D Gaussian Splatting优化方法。针对大场景下几何先验信息不完整的问题，EnerGS将部分可观测的几何信息建模为连续能量场，提供软性几何引导，而非硬性约束。实验表明，在稀疏多视角和单目设置下，EnerGS显著提升了光度重建质量和几何稳定性，并有效缓解了过拟合问题。

查看原文

item.title: CoQuant：联合权重与激活子空间投影的低比特大语言模型量化方法
item.content:encoded:
CoQuant通过联合分析权重和激活噪声提升低比特LLM量化效果。



摘要： 本文提出CoQuant，一种基于联合权重与激活子空间投影的低比特大语言模型量化方法。传统方法仅依赖激活统计量构建高精度子空间，忽略了线性操作中权重和激活噪声的共同影响。CoQuant通过理论建模输出误差，提出一种闭合形式的加权PCA解决方案，平衡激活与权重协方差以选择最优子空间。实验表明，CoQuant在Llama-3.2和Qwen2.5模型上均优于现有PTQ基线，在WikiText困惑度和零样本常识推理准确率方面表现优异，验证了联合建模的有效性。

item.title: 创意写作中的校准惊喜与信息熵分析
item.content:encoded:
创意写作依赖于约束下的意外性，通过信息熵分析实现质量对齐。



摘要： 本文探讨了优秀创意写作的核心在于‘校准的惊喜’，即在多个维度的约束下，可行解决方案的空间被压缩到一个狭窄区域，使得最终选择显得出人意料。作者引入香农互信息作为分析工具，指出‘校准’对应条件熵趋近于零，而‘惊喜’则对应熵增加。通过静态和动态两个角度分析，发现约束共同作用使可选方案大幅减少，且每个写作选择受前后内容影响。案例研究和轻量级语言模型计算验证了该框架的有效性，为创意质量对齐（CQA）提供了理论基础。

item.title: 基于推测解码的强化学习推理加速方法研究
item.content:encoded:
推测解码提升RL推理吞吐量，实现训练加速。



摘要： 本文研究了在强化学习（RL）后训练中，通过推测解码技术提升自回归推理效率的方法。该技术能够在不改变目标模型输出分布的前提下，实现无损加速。作者在NeMo-RL中集成推测解码，并支持同步与异步流水线，适用于多种推测机制。实验表明，在8B规模的推理任务中，推测解码使推理吞吐量提升1.8倍；结合异步RL，235B规模下可实现最高2.5倍的端到端训练加速。该方法为前沿语言模型的RL训练提供了新的优化路径。

item.title: AlphaJet：自动化飞机设计的端到端合成流程
item.content:encoded:
AlphaJet实现飞机设计自动化，提升早期设计效率。



摘要： AlphaJet是一种端到端的自动化飞机设计合成流程，能够根据文本任务描述（如质量、航程、巡航速度等）实时生成可行的3D飞机模型。该系统通过多学科评分函数评估飞机性能，包括气动、结构、重量、稳定性等。其三大创新点包括：基于解剖参数的变分自编码器、保护多样性的遗传算法以及考虑发动机安装位置的几何评分机制。该系统可在CPU上实时运行，并通过浏览器实时展示设计结果，适用于早期设计空间探索。

item.title: 基于不确定性建模的强化学习框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种考虑人类偏好不确定性的强化学习框架，提升系统稳定性与对齐性。



摘要： 本文提出一种双源不确定性感知的奖励框架，通过建模价值估计中的认知不确定性和人类偏好的不确定性，提升强化学习系统的稳定性和对齐性。该方法利用集成模型的分歧表示认知不确定性，通过奖励标注的变异性衡量偏好不确定性，并结合置信度调整的可靠性过滤器调节动作选择。实验结果表明，该方法在多个离散和连续控制环境中有效减少了奖励黑客行为，提升了训练稳定性，但在峰值奖励上有所妥协。

item.title: Bian Que：大规模在线引擎系统运维的智能代理框架
item.content:encoded:
Bian Que提升系统运维效率，减少警报量并提高根因分析准确率。



摘要： 本文介绍了Bian Que，一个用于大规模在线引擎系统（如搜索、推荐和广告）运维的智能代理框架。该框架通过统一的操作范式、灵活的技能配置以及自我演进机制，显著提升了运维效率。Bian Que能够自动选择相关数据和知识，避免信息过载，并通过修正信号驱动两种路径进行知识提炼和技能优化。在快手电商搜索引擎上的部署结果显示，其警报量减少了75%，根因分析准确率达到80%，平均解决时间缩短了50%以上。该框架在离线评估中达到了99.0%的通过率。

item.title: 基于稀疏自编码器的视觉Transformer异常检测方法
item.content:encoded:
SAEs用于ViT的[CLS]标记，实现有效的异常检测。



摘要： 本文首次将稀疏自编码器（SAEs）应用于视觉Transformer的[CLS]标记，以进行分布外（OOD）检测。通过Top-k SAE框架，将密集的[CLS]特征解耦为结构化的潜在空间，揭示了分布内数据具有稳定的类别激活模式（CAPs），而分布外样本会破坏这一结构。基于核心能量分布的差异性，提出了一种评分函数，有效提升了FPR95指标表现，展示了SAEs在视觉模型中进行可解释的鲁棒OOD检测的潜力。

item.title: 基于ADMM的在线参数学习优化方法
item.content:encoded:
通过在线学习调整ADMM参数提升优化性能。



摘要： 本文提出了一种基于ADMM的在线参数学习方法，用于改进具有固定结构但参数变化的优化问题的求解性能。该方法通过动态调整松弛参数来提升计算效率，避免了惩罚参数更新带来的矩阵重构开销。在基准二次规划问题上验证了该方法的有效性，结果显示其在迭代次数和实际运行时间上均优于传统OSQP方法。

item.title: 基于HUBO的量子特征选择框架研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种基于HUBO的量子特征选择方法，提升分类性能。



摘要： 本文提出一种基于高阶无约束二元优化（HUBO）的量子特征选择框架，通过引入一阶、二阶和三阶相互信息交互项，更全面地捕捉特征相关性与统计结构。为避免全选解，引入结构化线性惩罚项以提高稀疏性。该方法在IonQ Forte量子处理器上进行优化，并与经典方法如SelectKBest和PCA进行比较。实验结果显示，量子方法在两个基准数据集上表现出良好的性能，验证了其在当前离子阱处理器上的可行性，同时展示了其在机器学习预处理中的潜力。

item.title: 深度学习在假设检验中的应用
item.content:encoded:
深度学习可用于构建高效假设检验方法。



摘要： 本文探讨了深度学习方法在假设检验中的应用，提出了一种名为“深度检验”的新方法。该方法通过深度神经网络从模拟数据中学习分类图作为检验统计量，利用其强大的区分能力进行统计推断。以独立性检验为例，实验表明该方法在多种复杂依赖结构下表现优于十九种现有方法，验证了其有效性。

item.title: 基于非线性动力系统分析的抑郁数字生物标志物研究
item.content:encoded:
抑郁患者语音动态呈现异常复发结构，可作为数字生物标志物。



摘要： 本研究利用DAIC-WOZ语料库中的抑郁数据集，通过将语音轨迹建模为非线性动力系统，提取基于复发性的生物标志物。实验结果显示，该方法在分类性能上优于传统静态声学特征和熵动态特征等方法，AUC达到0.689，具有统计显著性（p=0.004）。研究支持通过非线性状态空间分析来探索抑郁的数字生物标志物，为精神疾病诊断提供了新方向。

item.title: 基于预测的FIFO缓冲区管理在线算法研究
item.content:encoded:
提出一种兼具一致性、平滑性和鲁棒性的FIFO缓冲区管理算法。



摘要： 本文提出了一种学习增强型在线算法，用于解决可抢占的FIFO缓冲区管理问题。该算法在预测准确时具有1-一致性，在预测误差增加时平滑退化，并在预测严重错误时保持渐近√3-鲁棒性，与经典在线问题的最佳已知最坏情况保证一致。关键贡献包括引入基于输出的预测误差度量，避免对原始输入序列的误判，并通过动态监控和缓冲区清空策略确保鲁棒性。此外，该算法提供了一个通用框架，替换后备模块即可实现不同竞争比的鲁棒性。

item.title: 挪威电力价格预测模型评估与市场动态分析
item.content:encoded:
挪威电力价格预测模型在新市场环境下表现各异，需关注外部因素影响。



摘要： 本文评估了挪威五个Nord Pool竞价区的电力价格预测模型，构建了2019至2025年的多模态小时数据集，并测试了包括LightGBM、ARX和深度学习在内的八种模型。研究采用严格因果测试集和多种分析方法，发现LightGBM在各区域表现最佳，而线性ARX模型在北部区域仍具竞争力。特征消融分析表明，仅依赖滞后价格和日历变量的模型也能达到高精度，但外部特征如水库水位和天然气价格在压力市场条件下对误差分层至关重要，强调了模型可解释性和市场状态感知的重要性。

item.title: World2VLM：通过世界模型提升视觉语言模型的空间推理能力
item.content:encoded:
World2VLM提升VLM动态空间推理能力。



摘要： 本文提出World2VLM，一种将生成式世界模型中的空间想象能力迁移到视觉语言模型的训练框架。该方法利用视图一致的世界模型生成几何对齐的未来视图，并为前向和逆向空间推理提供结构化监督。通过在紧凑数据集上进行两阶段微调，World2VLM在多个空间推理基准测试中表现出色，优于基线模型和依赖推理时世界模型的方法，同时减少了计算开销。实验表明，世界模型不仅可在推理时使用，也可作为训练时的有效教师，帮助VLM高效地内化空间想象力。

item.title: 符号接地不足以实现组合泛化，推理需显式学习
item.content:encoded:
符号接地不等于组合泛化，推理需显式训练。



摘要： 本文针对神经符号AI中一个核心假设进行实证分析，即组合推理是否能作为符号接地的副产品自然出现。研究引入了迭代逻辑张量网络（iLTN），一种用于多步骤演绎的可微架构。通过测试新实体、未见关系和复杂规则组合等泛化任务，发现仅基于接地目标训练的模型无法实现泛化，而同时训练感知接地和多步推理的iLTN则在所有任务中均取得高零样本准确率。研究结果表明，符号接地虽必要，但不足以实现泛化，推理是一种需要显式学习的目标能力。

item.title: 非西方语境下生成式AI对青少年隐私与安全的影响研究
item.content:encoded:
研究探讨沙特青少年使用生成式AI的隐私与安全风险。



摘要： 本研究通过混合方法分析了736条Reddit和1262条X（Twitter）帖子，并对31名沙特阿拉伯参与者（包括8名青少年、13名家长和10名教师）进行了访谈，探索青少年、家长和教师在非西方文化背景下对生成式AI工具的互动及风险感知。研究发现，青少年在使用生成式AI时存在因披露个人信息而引发的文化冲突，特别是在寻求情感支持时，这与沙特社会对谦逊、隐私和荣誉的传统观念相悖。此外，经济因素导致家庭甚至陌生人共享AI账户，进一步加剧了风险。研究提出了符合家长和教师期望的设计建议，为建立符合文化规范的青少年AI使用控制机制奠定基础。

item.title: QYOLO：基于量子启发的单阶段目标检测架构压缩方法
item.content:encoded:
QYOLO通过量子启发通道混合实现目标检测模型压缩。



摘要： 本文提出QYOLO，一种基于量子启发的通道混合框架，用于压缩单阶段目标检测模型。该方法通过替换两个深层C2f模块为QMixBlock，实现全局通道重校准，从而减少参数和计算量。实验表明，QYOLOv8n在VisDrone2019基准上实现了20.2%的参数减少和12.3%的GFLOPs减少，仅损失0.4个百分点的mAP@50。结合知识蒸馏可恢复准确率。最终设计采用仅压缩主干网络的方案，平衡了压缩效果与性能。

item.title: 基于规则与强化学习的无人机任务决策框架
item.content:encoded:
提出一种无人机任务决策框架，提升救援任务中的安全性和效率。



摘要： 本文提出一种分层决策框架，用于无人机在有限模拟训练下的搜救任务。该框架结合了离线规则指导和在线目标条件强化学习控制器，以提高早期适应性和样本效率。高阶指导模块通过结构化任务规范生成确定性规则，提供可解释的任务与安全引导；低阶控制器通过密集奖励在线学习，并利用模式感知优先经验回放机制提升性能。评估结果显示，该方法在多目标递送和动态目标递送任务中有效减少了碰撞终止，同时保持对场景动态的在线适应能力。

item.title: 面向大语言模型微调的自适应联邦分割学习系统SplitFT
item.content:encoded:
SplitFT解决LLM微调中的资源分配与通信开销问题。



摘要： 本文提出SplitFT，一种用于大语言模型微调的自适应联邦分割学习系统。该系统通过允许不同客户端根据自身计算资源和模型性能设置不同的切分层，解决了数据和设备异构性带来的系统性能下降问题。同时，SplitFT通过降低切分层的LoRA秩来减少通信开销。此外，为模拟真实应用场景中的数据异构性，研究还引入了一种基于长度的Dirichlet方法进行数据划分。实验结果表明，SplitFT在微调时间和模型性能方面均优于现有最佳方法。

item.title: FaaSMoE：多租户环境下高效部署Mixture-of-Experts模型的架构
item.content:encoded:
FaaSMoE通过FaaS平台实现多租户MoE模型的资源高效利用。



摘要： 本文提出FaaSMoE，一种基于函数即服务（FaaS）平台的多租户Mixture-of-Experts（MoE）推理架构。该架构通过将专家模型部署为无状态FaaS函数，实现了控制与执行平面的解耦，支持按需调用和零扩展的专家模型。FaaSMoE还支持在函数内配置专家粒度，以减少调用开销。实验表明，在多租户工作负载下，FaaSMoE使用的资源不到全模型基准的三分之一，展示了其在多租户环境中高效部署MoE模型的可行性。

item.title: GIFGuard：面向GIF图像的深度伪造主动取证框架
item.content:encoded:
提出GIFGuard框架，用于检测GIF图像中的深度伪造。



摘要： 本文针对深度伪造技术对GIF图像真实性的威胁，提出GIFGuard，这是首个针对GIF图像的时空水印框架。该框架包含STARE和DIRD两个模块，分别用于嵌入和提取水印信号，以应对高级语义篡改。同时构建了GIFfaces数据集，推动该领域研究。实验表明GIFGuard在视觉质量和鲁棒性方面表现优异。

item.title: LATTICE：评估加密货币代理决策支持能力的基准测试
item.content:encoded:
LATTICE 提供了评估加密货币代理决策支持能力的新基准。



摘要： 本文介绍了 LATTICE，这是一个用于评估加密货币代理在真实用户场景中决策支持能力的基准。与以往主要关注推理或结果评估的基准不同，LATTICE 通过六个评估维度、16种任务类型以及基于大语言模型的评分机制，全面衡量代理在协助用户决策方面的能力。该基准设计可扩展，无需依赖专家标注或外部数据，且能根据新维度和反馈持续更新。研究使用 LATTICE 评估了六款实际使用的加密货币助手，并在1200个多样化查询上进行测试，结果显示各助手在总体得分相近，但在具体维度和任务上表现差异显著，表明决策支持质量存在权衡，用户需求不同可能影响最佳选择。

item.title: 基于未来世界环境的实时未来预测研究
item.content:encoded:
实时未来预测通过模拟真实事件提升智能体学习能力。



摘要： 本文介绍了实时未来预测任务，该任务旨在利用大语言模型构建能够持续学习的智能体。作者提出了一种名为FutureWorld的强化学习环境，用于闭合预测、结果实现和参数更新的训练循环。实验中使用三个开源基础模型进行了连续多日的训练，验证了方法的有效性。同时，基于该环境构建了每日基准测试，评估了多个前沿智能体的表现，为当前智能体系统建立了性能基准。

item.title: 基于课程学习的自进化视频理解框架CurEvo
item.content:encoded:
CurEvo通过课程学习提升视频理解模型的自主进化能力。



摘要： 本文提出CurEvo，一个基于课程学习的自进化视频理解框架，旨在解决现有方法在优化控制和难度渐进方面的不足。CurEvo通过动态调节任务难度、优化评估标准并平衡数据多样性，形成结构化的反馈循环，使模型学习复杂度与能力相匹配。该框架融合多维自适应问答机制，实现感知、识别和理解维度的联合进化，显著提升了多个视频问答基准的准确率和语义评分，验证了课程引导自进化方法的有效性。

item.title: Semantic Foam：提升3D场景重建的语义分解方法
item.content:encoded:
Semantic Foam提升3D场景语义分割性能。



摘要： 本文提出Semantic Foam，一种基于Radiant Foam的语义分解方法，通过在体素Voronoi网格中引入细胞级别的语义特征场，提升跨视角一致性并减少遮挡和不一致监督带来的伪影。实验表明，该方法在物体级分割上优于现有技术如Gaussian Grouping和SAGA，适用于交互式图形应用。

item.title: ClawGym：支持多步骤工作流的个人代理开发框架
item.content:encoded:
提出ClawGym框架，支持个人代理开发全流程。



摘要： 本文介绍了ClawGym，一个用于支持多步骤工作流的可扩展框架，旨在解决Claw-style环境在训练数据合成、模型训练和评估方面的不足。ClawGym包含三个核心组件：ClawGym-SynData，一个由13.5K个任务组成的多样化数据集；ClawGym-Agents，通过监督微调和轻量级强化学习训练的模型；以及ClawGym-Bench，一个经过自动化筛选和人工审核的200个实例基准测试。该框架为Claw-style代理的开发提供了完整的生命周期支持。

item.title: 神经集合学习变量间的因果方向
item.content:encoded:
神经集合可通过局部可塑性学习变量间的因果方向。



摘要： 文章探讨了神经集合是否能够学习变量之间的因果方向。尽管神经集合已被证明在分类、解析和规划中具有计算通用性，但尚未验证其是否能内化因果方向性。研究发现，神经集合的固有操作——投影、局部可塑性控制和稀疏胜者选择——足以实现方向性学习。作者提出了DIRECT机制，通过自适应增益调度共同激活源和目标集合，以建立有向关系。与基于反向传播的方法不同，DIRECT仅依赖局部可塑性，使得因果结论可在机制层面进行审计。研究通过双读出验证策略验证了结果，包括突触强度不对称性和功能传播重叠。在多个领域，该框架在监督设定下实现了完美的结构恢复，表明神经集合可以作为生物合理动态与形式因果模型之间的可审计桥梁。

item.title: ATLAS：用于长时序机器人演示标注的高效工具
item.content:encoded:
ATLAS提升机器人动作分割标注效率与精度。



摘要： 本文介绍了ATLAS，一款专为长时序机器人动作分割设计的标注工具。ATLAS支持多模态数据的时间同步可视化，包括多视角视频和本体感知信号，并提供动作边界、标签及任务结果的标注功能。该工具兼容多种机器人数据格式，如ROS bags和RLDS，并可通过模块化结构扩展至新格式。其键盘导向界面提高了标注效率，在接触密集型装配任务中，相比ELAN工具，ATLAS将每动作标注时间减少了至少6%，同时提升了时间对齐精度并降低了边界误差。

item.title: 基于图结构的验证工件重用方法研究
item.content:encoded:
本文提出一种生成程序与规范图表示的方法，支持跨语言验证工件重用。



摘要： 本文聚焦于通过图结构构建实现验证工件的重用。作者提出一个将命令式程序及其注释转换为类型化、属性化的图的流程，并利用抽象语法树解析和语义嵌入模型（如SentenceTransformer和CodeBERT）生成既包含结构关系又体现语义上下文的图表示。实验覆盖了C语言与ACSL、Java与JML以及C#与Dafny的数据集，结果表明不同语言和注释风格下可以构建一致的图表示。该研究为后续的语义增强和近似图匹配提供了实用基础，有助于提升验证工件重用的可扩展性。
